The House on Gryphon Hill.name: The House on Gryphon Hill
The House on Gryphon Hill.caption: The Weathermay-Foxgrove Twins, Gennifer and Laurie, along with Savra Sunstar and Ez d’Avenir, explore the infamous House on Gryphon Hill.

Haunted Terrors.caption: Characters in horror adventures are often haunted by greater terrors than the monsters they face.

Dhampir.name: Dhampir
Dhampir.caption: Dhampirs are living people who possess vampiric prowess but are cursed with macabre hunger.

Hexblood.name: Hexblood
Hexblood.caption: Hexbloods are individuals infused with fey magic as a result of eldritch energy or mysterious witchcraft.

Lupin.name: Lupin
Lupin.caption: With their bristling fur and snarling muzzles, lupins cast imposing shadows in the Mists

Reborn.name: Reborn
Reborn.caption: Reborn are individuals who have died yet inexplica- bly still live.

Aberrant Anatomy.name: Aberrant Anatomy
Aberrant Anatomy.caption: The Aberrant Anatomy Dark Gift might manifest as a distinctly piscine look.

Living Shadow.name: Żywy cień

Watchers.name: Watchers
Watchers.caption: The Watchers Dark Gift might manifest as anything from simple vermin to alien beings—like the Staring Cats of Uldun-Dar.

Village of Vallaki.name: Village of Vallaki
Village of Vallaki.caption: Nightly the village of Vallaki bars its gates against attacks from wolves and deadlier predators.

The Misericordia Estate.caption: At Misericordia, the Boritsi family estate, Ivana Boritsi indulges her research into rare and deadly plants.

Port-a-Lucine.name: Port-a-Lucine

Strahd von Zarovich.caption: “I am the Ancient. I am the Land. My beginnings are lost in the darkness of the past. I am not dead. Nor am I alive. I am undead, forever.” —Strahd von Zarovich

Ivana Boritsi.caption: “Your kind can’t possibly fathom how much this sort of interruption costs the House of Boritsi. Don’t make it worse by being tedious.” —Ivana Boritsi

Ivan Dilisnya.caption: “Oh ho oh! What succulent insipidity I see in those lumpish eyes! What divine folly! I swoon, I croon, I know! You’ll be a new favorite!” —Ivan Dilisnya

Azalin Rex.caption: “It is customary to kneel before your dread lord.” —Azalin Rex

Saidra d’Honaire.caption: “Welcome, treasured guests—you bright jewels in my city’s sterling crown. Bask in all you’ve earned and deserve. Oh … that’s not a loose button, is it?” —Saidra d’Honaire

Vladeska Drakov.name: Vladeska Drakov
Vladeska Drakov.caption: “If you’re reinforcements, I expect you to prove you’re worth the gear you’re wearing. I need soldiers, not more bait for the undead.” —Vladeska Drakov

Ankhtepot.name: Ankhtepot
Ankhtepot.caption: “Bow before the pharaoh — whether as a servant or as dust.” —Ankhtepot

Hazlik.name: Hazlik
Hazlik.caption: “You’re new, I can tell. Impress me, and maybe I’ll let you be my newest apprentice.” —Hazlik

Cthulhu.name: Cthulhu
Cthulhu.caption: Ph’nglui mglw’nafh Cthulhu R’lyeh wgah’nagl fhtagn. —Cult of the Chthonic Night chant

Ramya Vasavadan.name: Ramya Vasavadan
Ramya Vasavadan.caption: Are you loyal to your maharani? Or are you yet more rebel skulls to add to the Tower of Traitors? —Ramya Vasavadan

Harkon Lukas.name: Harkon Lukas
Harkon Lukas.caption: I must say, your skill is unlike any I’ve ever seen. The name’s Harkon. I’m sure you’ve heard of me. — Harkon Lukas

Viktra Mordenheim.name: Viktra Mordenheim
Viktra Mordenheim.caption: “Ah. You’re conscious. Good. Test your limbs, and get accustomed to your new body.” —Viktra Mordenheim

Wilfred Godefroy.name: Wilfred Godefroy
Wilfred Godefroy.caption: Speak your piece, then hold your tongue. No fools are more insufferable than the living. — Wilfred Godefroy

Ebonbane.name: Ebonbane
Ebonbane.caption: Questing knights? Champions of the realm? Look upon thy sterling souls. Tell me, heroes, by what fair deeds art thou known in this rotted world? — Ebonbane

Lord Soth of Sithicus.name: Lord Soth of Sithicus
Lord Soth of Sithicus.caption: Explain why you have interrupted my reverie, or I will turn your waking hours into nightmares. — Lord Soth

Mother Lorinda.name: Mother Lorinda
Mother Lorinda.caption: What precocious moppets you are! Little ones who show Mother love get rewarded, but bad children get punished. Which sorts of tots are you? — Mother Lorinda

Chakuna.name: Chakuna
Chakuna.caption: You seem capable, but do you have the heart of a hunter? —Chakuna

Backgrounds.name: Pochodzenia
Backgrounds.text:
This section presents backgrounds exploring origins suited to characters in horror adventures, using the same format presented in the Player’s Handbook.

Two of the backgrounds, Mist Wanderer and Spirit Medium, offer Dark Gift feats rather than Origin feats; these feats appear at the end of the chapter. The Haunted One and Investigator backgrounds provide a choice between an Origin feat and a Dark Gift feat, so you can tailor your character’s relationship to sinister forces in their past.

Adventures in Barovia.name: Adventures in Barovia
Adventures in Barovia.text:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.RD4KRPqez4XuigDF inline]

Adventures set in Barovia involve oppressive, gothic menace; suspicion; and the legendary threat of Strahd von Zarovich. Use this section to develop horror adventures in this domain.

Horror in Barovia

Adventures in Barovia often include the following elements.

The Ancient, The Land

Strahd remains bound to Barovia and it to him in a way few rulers or Darklords understand. He knows when any creature enters or dies violently within Barovia, and he takes offense when someone slays his servants or the domain’s wolves. Strahd can manifest a variety of dramatic effects, such as casting his voice on the wind, making his visage appear in the clouds, or changing the weather. These effects don’t aid him in combat, but they contribute to making him feel like an oppressive and ever-present threat.

Incarnations of the Past

The subject of Strahd’s obsession, Tatyana, perpetually reincarnates among the people of Barovia. No matter what form she takes, the Darklord unceasingly seeks her, determined to possess her and make her part of an idealized fantasy of his past. Tatyana’s incarnation might be anyone, including a player character. If her incarnation is an NPC, roll on the Tatyana’s Reincarnation and Connection to Strahd tables to inspire a new chapter in the count’s tale of obsession.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.tables.RollTable.rhwTatyanasReiZ0 rollable resultLabel="Tatyana’s Reincarnation Is …"]{Tatyana’s Reincarnation}

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.tables.RollTable.rhwConnectiontmy rollable resultLabel="Tatyana’s Reincarnation …"]{Connection to Strahd}

Roots of Evil

As the first Domain of Dread, Barovia might serve an important role in the metaphysical underpinnings of the setting. Some Darklords might have emerged from the Mists first in Barovia, encountered Strahd, and found their ways to their own domains later. Others might have a particular interest in Barovia and in undoing Strahd’s connection to his land. Consider these possibilities when developing plots involving conflicts between Darklords or escaping the Mists, as outlined in chapter 3.

Signs of the Count

Servants of Strahd are everywhere in Barovia. While in the domain, characters experience regular reminders that the Darklord watches them—and that they are unwelcome. Roll on the Strahd’s Presence table to inspire such encounters.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.tables.RollTable.rhwStrahdsPresLU rollable resultLabel="Strahd’s Presence Is Suggested …"]{Strahd’s Presence}

Barovia Campaign Arc

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.LMM1nh8GjqpuSjgV cite=false classes="float-right"]

Campaigns set in Barovia involve communities oppressed by fear of Strahd and his cycles of obsession. A campaign here might follow this outline:

Levels 1–5. The characters emerge from the Mists and seek shelter from danger in the village of Barovia, Krezk, or Vallaki. There they learn the people fear Count von Zarovich, and the characters might meet a reincarnation of Tatyana. They help strike back against agents of the count, such as deadly @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmWolf0000000000]{Wolves} or @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmZombie00000000]{Zombies}.

Levels 6–8. The characters learn more of the count’s history and about magical relics that might help oppose him. They seek these in haunted corners of the domain, facing servants of Strahd and remnants of his past, including @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmGhost000000000]{Ghosts}, @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmWerewolf000000]{Werewolves}, and deadly @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmDruid000000000]{Druids}.

Levels 9–10. Armed with knowledge and magical weapons, the characters lay siege to Castle Ravenloft, facing Strahd’s most terrifying servants and the count himself.

Strahd von Zarovich.name: Strahd von Zarovich
Strahd von Zarovich.text:
First of the Darklords 

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.gtKXC4M2LTBICjFA cite=false]

Count Strahd von Zarovich has lost all he ever desired—over and over again. Tormented by the Dark Powers, Strahd is haunted by visions of his lost family and tarnished nobility. The count spends long years brooding in the depths of Castle Ravenloft, but a reincarnation of his obsession appears every generation: Tatyana. Each time Tatyana’s reincarnation emerges, Strahd seeks to undo the failures of his past and find a way to win her love. Time and time again, Strahd’s heartless, domineering nature causes his bloody history to repeat itself and drives Tatyana beyond his clutches.

A severe, aristocratic figure, the count has the bearing of a former soldier and the impatience of one used to being obeyed. He feels little but occasionally embraces the novelty of toying with amusing strangers or breaking the spirits of his enemies. During his unnaturally long existence, he has learned many occult secrets and mysteries of the Domains of Dread. Strahd’s dignified bearing crumbles when someone breaks his laws or denies him what he desires. In such cases, Strahd makes bloody examples of his opponents, drawing out his vengeance into campaigns of terror.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwStrahdvonZaro statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Strahd’s Tale

Count Strahd von Zarovich was a ruthless conqueror. Retiring after years of war, Strahd settled in the beautiful valley where he’d won his greatest victory. There, in the way of his ancestors, he spilled his blood into the earth, sealing a pact between himself and the land. In honor of his parents, King Barov and Queen Ravenovia, he named the valley Barovia and constructed his home, Castle Ravenloft.

Strahd invited his few remaining family members to live with him at Castle Ravenloft and eventually was joined by his younger brother, Sergei. Sergei was everything Strahd wasn’t—youthful, empathic, and warm. Soon after he arrived, the younger von Zarovich and a local villager named Tatyana fell in love. Strahd resented his brother, even more so after meeting Tatyana and being &Reference[charmed] by her pure kindness. Obsessed, Strahd sought to woo her but was rebuffed. Unwilling to accept her wishes or the idea of Sergei as his better, Strahd made a pact with ancient evil forces to regain his vitality and ensnare Tatyana’s heart.

The day of Sergei and Tatyana’s wedding, Strahd murdered Sergei and, to seal his pact, drank his brother’s blood. He revealed his new might to Tatyana, expecting to enthrall her. Instead, horrified by Sergei’s murder, Tatyana fled Strahd, ultimately leaping from the height of Castle Ravenloft and vanishing into the Mists. At the same time, traitors from among the castle’s guards and wedding’s guests rose up to assassinate Strahd. Despite countless wounds, Strahd didn’t die. His bargain with the Dark Powers had made Strahd the multiverse’s first vampire. A night of slaughter followed; when dawn touched the parapets of Castle Ravenloft, no living soul survived within. But Strahd remained.

Strahd’s villainy and his connection to his land drew all of Barovia into the grip of the Dark Powers, which transplanted the vampire and his realm into the Shadowfell. This began a succession of torments that plague the count still. The Dark Powers’ attentions have spilled forth to drag others into the Mists as well, giving rise to the Domains of Dread.

Strahd’s Domain

Barovia is altered by Strahd’s presence, creating the following effects:

Closing the Borders. While in his domain, Strahd can close or open the borders to Barovia at will. While the borders are closed, noxious fog suffuses the Mists surrounding Barovia. A creature that enters the Mists for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there must succeed on a [[/save con dc=20 format=long]] or gain 1 &Reference[exhaustion] level. A creature makes this save only once per turn.

Dreadful Darkness. While in an area of Darkness, creatures other than Strahd and creatures of his choice have Disadvantage on saving throws to end or avoid the &Reference[frightened] condition.

Jealous Heart. When a character receives Heroic Inspiration but already has it, that character can’t give it to another character in the group.

If Strahd dies, these effects end immediately.

Strahd’s Lairs

While the notorious Castle Ravenloft is Strahd’s home, it isn’t his only redoubt in Barovia. Multiple bastions of evil serve as sanctuaries for the count and his servants, should fate demand.

Castle Ravenloft

Castle Ravenloft is Strahd von Zarovich’s accursed sanctuary. Tormented spirits and Strahd’s minions haunt the vast castle, each serving the count’s schemes, reflecting some aspect of his depraved past, or protecting his coffin in the castle catacombs. Mementos of lost times and fallen heroes lie throughout the count’s home, some of which might hold the key to his undoing. The adventure Curse of Strahd details Castle Ravenloft.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.iO2qWXfgXy4z9kF4 classes="hide-in-embed"]

Von Zarovich Sanctuaries

Hidden across Barovia lie secret sanctuaries known only to Strahd and his servants. These crypt-like boltholes are hidden from the sun and exist primarily to shelter the count should he require. Over the centuries, Strahd has granted trusted servants access to some of these sanctuaries and used others in plots or experiments that he keeps distant from Castle Ravenloft. Still others Strahd has forgotten, allowing other evils to fester there. An example of one of these sites is depicted on map 2.2 and has the following features:

Count’s Coffin. Hidden in each sanctuary lies a coffin bearing the von Zarovich crest. This might be obvious or in a space only accessible to vampires.

The Lord’s Presence. Behind a vault-like iron door, an entry chamber holds a high-backed throne. Strahd forbids servants from using these symbols of his authority.

Servants’ Space. Alcoves carved in the walls provide space for the coffins or bunks of Strahd’s living or dead servants. Sometimes, the Darklord uses these spaces for arcane experiments or as makeshift prisons.

Blood o’ the Vine.name: Blood o’ the Vine
Blood o’ the Vine.text:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.zWfXi59ij8Tc2J1d inline]

@UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.adventures.Adventure.uYSqaBBsmvMMPSvj]{Adventure for Level 6 Characters}

Situation. A discovery beneath the Blood of the Vine tavern in the village of Barovia has unleashed sinister magic, transforming the owners.

Hook. Disguised as a human, @UUID[Actor.Af67jpANrvwto4Du]{Urwin Martikov} (Medium Wereraven; see chapter 5) of the Blue Water Inn in Vallaki hires the characters to investigate why the Blood of the Vine tavern in the village of Barovia has closed, claiming he was in negotiations with the owners to buy it. Urwin offers to pay characters [[/award 500gp each]].

Scene Configuration Overview

  • If you encounter broken links below, they will appear once you install the adventure in your World.

  • Each map features distinct interior lighting options, accessible via Scene Controls, enabling transitions between brighter outdoor streets and dark interiors.

  • Weather effects are disabled for all interior scenes, ensuring indoor environments remain unaffected by outdoor scene configurations. By default, no weather effects are set up for the scene.

  • The tavern’s main floor is outfitted with preplaced area lights, while each table and private room includes unlit, prepositioned candles. Overhead area lights are labeled on the scene as OHL.

  • NPCs populate the scene in advance, ready for player interaction. The Minotaur skeleton and ghouls remain hidden at first, ready for their reveal at your discretion.

Encounters

This adventure uses map 2.2: Blood of the Vine Tavern and consists of these encounters.

Corrupted Owners. The three human sisters who owned the Blood of the Vine tavern—Alenka, Mirabel, and Sorvina—have been transformed into the following ravenous embodiments of primal hunger:

@UUID[Actor.XO9WWK01t9HqsJ9D]{Alenka} (Relentless Slasher; see chapter 5) wields kitchenware and prowls the first floor.

@UUID[Actor.lOJjKJhgplwaXcli]{Mirabel} (Medium Ghast Gravecaller) stalks the second floor with her two @UUID[Actor.Z7ero1r8spMMDfS3]{Ghoul} servants.

@UUID[Actor.IIBZhCUWAI3PM5tK]{Sorvina} (@UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwUnspeakableHo]{Unspeakable Horror}; see chapter 5) conducts strange rituals in the basement.

Taproom. Dust covers the taproom. Here, the animate corpse of Arik the barkeep mindlessly cleans pieces of shattered glassware at the bar. If attacked or otherwise interrupted, Arik falls to pieces.

Office. The office in the northeast corner of the first floor is cluttered with junk and unfinished notes to the count describing the activities of the tavern’s patrons. This suggests the owners spied on the locals. If the characters have visited the tavern before, their activities are detailed. A trapdoor descends to an area beneath the tavern.

Private Dining Room. The dining room in the southeast corner of the second floor is strewn with broken dishes, rotten food, and pages from a destroyed book. The pages are from the journal of Mirabel, one of the inn’s owners. Surviving entries note the following information:

First Entry. Mirabel’s sister Sorvina discovered a hollow hidden under the tavern. Strange ruins lie amid the dirt walls. Sorvina wants to excavate them, hoping to find a tomb filled with treasures.

Second Entry. Sorvina’s digging revealed strange standing stones. Mirabel worries about her sister, who’s started whispering to herself as she obsesses over learning more about the stones.

Third Entry. Sorvina discovered the bones of some monster at the center of the standing stones. All three sisters are having nightmares from which they wake up ravenous. Sorvina claims the hunger is a blessing. Food has lost its taste, but the inn’s patrons suddenly smell delicious.

Temple of Ravenkind. A circle of ancient menhirs carved with stylized ravens and sunbursts is partially unearthed beneath the tavern. The creature that was once Sorvina is here, chanting over a monstrous skeleton of black bones with stag horns and tusks. When she notices intruders, Sorvina howls, causing the bones to rise as a @UUID[Actor.dyFcbXHhkjY0OZqw]{Minotaur Skeleton}. Both it and Sorvina fight the characters to the death. When the skeleton is defeated, its bones remain intact.

Cursed Remains. The skeleton exudes evil magic. Casting @UUID[Compendium.dnd5e.spells24.Item.phbsplRemoveCurs]{Remove Curse} on the monster destroys the skeleton. However, if a character touches the skeleton (while it’s animate or otherwise), its spirit whispers to the character, bidding them to end its ancient curse. Doing so destroys the skeleton and grants the character the @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.options.Item.rhwSecondSkinkc1]{Second Skin} Dark Gift (see chapter 1). A character who already has a Dark Gift can’t accept the curse.

Returning to Urwin. If the characters defeat the monsters in the tavern and return to Urwin, he pays them and shares that a benevolent relic, the Holy Symbol of Ravenkind, was created at a site like the one hidden under the tavern. The circle was used later to imprison a vicious spirit of the Svalich Woods. If the characters destroyed the skeleton, Urwin rewards them with two random Quaal’s Feather Tokens (see Dungeon Master's Guide). Characters also receive a bonus [[/award 1400xp each]].

Treacherous Blood.name: Treacherous Blood
Treacherous Blood.text:
@UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.adventures.Adventure.3keaaeGBgPVLQ6qy]{Adventure for Level 9 Characters}

Situation. @UUID[Actor.fgFRAdzzfu9mXwrj]{Lyssa von Zarovich} (Medium, Chaotic Evil Vampire Nightbringer), a distant relative of Strahd, plots to undermine the count by unleashing her monstrous servants on Barovia.

Hook. A human resident of Vallaki named @UUID[Actor.AZpmhDP1e3H9rhic]{Ernst Larnak} (Medium, Lawful Evil Spy) tells the characters that locals have seen figures excavating the churchyard in the ruins of Berez. He offers to pay [[/award 2500gp]] if they investigate. He doesn’t share that his information comes from the count’s servants.

Scene Configuration Overview

  • If you encounter broken links below, they will appear once you install the adventure in your World.

  • Each map features distinct interior lighting options, accessible via Scene Controls, enabling transitions between the brighter outdoors and dark interiors.

  • The Below Ground area has pre-placed area lights that are turned off by default.

  • Hidden NPCs populate the scene in advance, ready for player interaction at your discretion.

Encounters

This adventure uses map 2.2: Von Zarovich Sanctuary and consists of these encounters.

Berez Churchyard. A stone slab covering a grave in the churchyard has been shattered, revealing stairs leading down to a von Zarovich sanctuary. There, two @UUID[Actor.M6dnHydwDEP8ewBB]{Vampire Spawn} guardians fight trespassers.

Lyssa’s Plot. Lyssa greets the characters in the sanctuary’s central room. Observing that they don’t look like servants of the count, she shares the following information:

  • She is a distant relative of Count Strahd von Zarovich. She, too, is a vampire.

  • She seeks to depose Strahd and raise Barovia to its deserved glory.

  • With the aid of allies beyond the Mists, she has created servants immune to many of Strahd’s powers. Now she needs to test them.

Regardless of whether the characters agree to aid Lyssa, she opens a door to the adjacent chamber and unleashes a pair of @UUID[Actor.9YtRjsWEFoulU2px]{Vampire Mind Flayers} (see chapter 5). The monsters obey Lyssa’s commands and fight the characters. Lyssa fights as well but flees if she takes damage, swearing her revenge.

Crypt. The chamber to the north holds the bloodless corpse of a mind flayer and a journal filled with accounts of strange dreams. The journal is a Mist talisman linked to the domain of @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwOtherDomainso.JournalEntryPage.Bluetspurk9jL2Ep]{Bluetspur} (see “Other Domains of Dread” later in this chapter).

Hidden Chamber. A character who investigates the large room as a &Reference[Study] action and succeeds on a [[/check investigation 16 format=long]] finds evidence of a bricked-over hidden chamber in the east wall. Characters can physically remove the wall in 1 hour (half that time if they have Mason’s Tools).

Elder Evil. Strahd buried a foe in a coffin behind the main chamber’s east wall. Opening the coffin releases a @UUID[Actor.bklo6V0TMxtVxwyq]{Vampire Nosferatu} and a @UUID[Actor.eZDvxEsiAkssUwDY]{Swarm of Infesting Insect} (both detailed in chapter 5) that fight to the death. Also in the coffin is a @UUID[Compendium.dnd-dungeon-masters-guide.equipment.Item.dmgGemOfBrightne]{Gem of Brightness}.

Conclusion. If the characters defeat or drive off Lyssa, each character gains a bonus [[/award 2600xp]].

Barovia.name: Barovia
Barovia.text:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.PRXcdtA17OTxz88G]

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.co3nyoeVsuAWKMvk]

Domain of the First Vampire

Darklord: @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwStrahdvonZaro]{Strahd von Zarovich}

Genre: Gothic horror

Hallmarks: Undead despot, notorious haunted stronghold, tragic resurrection

Mist Talismans: @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.items.Item.rhwBarovianWineB]{Barovian wine bottle}, @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.items.Item.rhwvonZarovichCr]{von Zarovich crest}, @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.items.Item.rhwMarkoftheRave]{Mark of the Raven talisman}

Aligned Tarokka Card: Darklord

Opposed Tarokka Card: Mists

In Barovia, the night is a curse. As darkness falls, wicked souls slip from the spires of Castle Ravenloft to work the will of an immortal overlord. This is the realm of the vampire Count @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwStrahdvonZaro]{Strahd von Zarovich}, whose depravities have doomed him and his land to endless cycles of obsession and despair.

The howls of wolves and shrieks of ravens echo through the dismal valleys and oppressive forests of Barovia. In isolated communities, superstitious villagers find the brightness in their lives smothered by dread of their aloof overlord and other festering evils. These Barovians fear the night, as agents of the devil Strahd watch in the dark and claim whatever he desires. Yet few in Barovia realize their torments have played out again and again. In every generation, echoes of Strahd’s past reemerge, compelling and confounding the Darklord as he endlessly obsesses over the one victim who’s eluded him for centuries.

Domain Features

Barovia is known for the following features:

The Devil Strahd. Few have seen Count von Zarovich, but all know he dwells in Castle Ravenloft, a citadel from which few visitors return. Strahd is infamous for viciously executing any who flagrantly break his laws, but otherwise he rarely interferes in the lives of his people. Some whisper that he is a vampire.

Haunted Tales. The stories of Barovia’s people are full of hidden evils: accursed ruins, portentous ravens, secretive cults, treacherous witches, vicious werewolves, and worse.

Isolation and Suspicion. Barovia is a realm of wolf-prowled forests and treacherous mountains where the sun is obscured. The land’s superstitious people live in scattered villages. They distrust strangers, fearing they’re agents of the count.

Locations in Barovia

Barovia is a land of long shadows where ancient wildernesses strangle the ruins of a near-forgotten empire. Mysteries pervade the domain, and those who leave the relative safety of Barovia’s dismal settlements enter perilous lands where mortals aren’t welcome.

The village of Barovia is described below, and other notable locations are summarized on the Barovia Locations table.

Village of Barovia

Lying in the shadow of Castle Ravenloft, the village of Barovia teeters on the brink of ruin. Its few residents rarely venture from their homes, suspecting their neighbors of wickedness and fearing evils in the night. Their dread is largely justified, as wolves, ghosts, and vampire spawn haunt the town, and many locals have been consumed by callousness or wicked temptations.

A rare exception to the village’s cold desperation can be found at the local tavern, the Blood of the Vine. Those few who oppose Strahd’s evil congregate here—under the covert oversight of the count’s agents.

Count Strahd von Zarovich has lost all he ever desired—over and over again. Tormented by the Dark Powers, Strahd is haunted by visions of his lost family and tarnished nobility. The count spends long years brooding in the depths of Castle Ravenloft, but a reincarnation of his obsession appears every generation: Tatyana. Each time Tatyana’s reincarnation emerges, Strahd seeks to undo the failures of his past and find a way to win her love. Time and time again, Strahd’s heartless, domineering nature causes his bloody history to repeat itself and drives Tatyana beyond his clutches.

A severe, aristocratic figure, the count has the bearing of a former soldier and the impatience of one used to being obeyed. He feels little but occasionally embraces the novelty of toying with amusing strangers or breaking the spirits of his enemies. During his unnaturally long existence, he has learned many occult secrets and mysteries of the Domains of Dread. Strahd’s dignified bearing crumbles when someone breaks his laws or denies him what he desires. In such cases, Strahd makes bloody examples of his opponents, drawing out his vengeance into campaigns of terror.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.osEKBeLjuyBeYSxS cite=false]

Barovia Locations

Location

Description

Amber Temple

A society of mages constructed the Amber Temple long ago to imprison vestiges of malevolent entities. The mages are long dead; mighty evil forces now obsess over the temple’s secrets.

Argynvostholt

The fortified Argynvostholt mansion lies in ruins haunted by the restless spirits of a knightly order destroyed by Strahd. A statue of a regal silver dragon stands before the manor’s entrance.

Balinok Mountains

Barovia spans valleys among the Balinok Mountains. Snow crowns the highest peak, Mount Baratok, while the smaller Mount Ghakis is riddled with deep caves.

Castle Ravenloft

The grim castle of Count Strahd von Zarovich looms over the village of Barovia. The haunted castle contains Strahd’s supernatural servants and the secrets of the Darklord’s origins.

Gates of Barovia

Mighty gates flanked by decapitated statues of soldiers stand along the Old Svalich Road.

They open mysteriously for travelers but allow only Vistani to leave the domain to the east.

Krezk

Above the fortified, wooded village of Krezk rises the Abbey of Saint Markovia. Here, a mysterious abbot tends to patients but, in doing so, leaves them permanently changed.

Old Bonegrinder

An ominous windmill called Old Bonegrinder is home to a coven of night hags that create magical pastries. Those who consume the pastries enter a stupor filled with vivid, joyful dreams.

Ruins of Berez

Moldering ruins are all that remain of Berez, a village that denied Strahd’s will and earned his wrath. Nearby stands a statue of a kneeling peasant clutching a rose.

Svalich Woods

Packs of wolves, dire wolves, and werewolves prowl the Svalich Woods, hunting those who oppose Strahd. Strange hermits and witches lurk within the deepest reaches.

Tser Pool

The Vistani who camp near the Tser Pool lake are often joined by other wanderers. This is also the home of the seer Madam Eva (see chapter 5), who knows much of Strahd and the Mists.

Vallaki

The walled town of Vallaki hosts regular festivals to lift the spirits of its despondent inhabitants. Those who don’t participate risk being punished as malcontents.

Yester Hill

A cult of druids devoted to Strahd conducts rituals amid the circle of boulders atop Yester Hill. The Mists nearby reflect a vision of the count’s ancestral home in the distance.

Bodytaker Plants.name: Bodytaker Plants
Bodytaker Plants.text:
Body-Snatching Alien Flora

Habitat: Forest, Grassland, Hill, Swamp; Treasure: Any

Bodytaker plants are intelligent organisms that originated on a long-ruined planet of the Material Plane. To outlive their doomed world, these enigmatic plants spread their seeds to the stars. In rare cases, their meteorite-like seeds fall to fertile worlds and take root. What emerges is a new bodytaker plant that seeks to grow and propagate until its kind dominates the entire world. As assistants, the plant creates eerie vegetative duplicates of the world’s inhabitants called bodytaker podlings. Podlings serve the bodytaker plants’ will, infiltrating societies and replacing the individuals they resemble. Ultimately, podlings seek to isolate inhabitants so they can become fertilizer for the bodytaker plant that created them or fodder for more podlings.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.xdgCiTvDvG078aOW inline]

Bodytaker Podling.name: Bodytaker Podling
Bodytaker Podling.text:
A bodytaker plant uses its powerful vines to capture victims or accept captives brought to it by podling servants. The plant drags prey into an attached pod where the captive is dissolved over the course of hours. After the process is complete, a duplicate of the captive emerges as a subservient podling. Nothing of the original creature remains, though its noncompostable gear might be recovered after the bodytaker plant is slain.

A bodytaker plant’s podlings are extensions of the plant’s will, and the plant has no qualms about sacrificing podlings or using them as shields in battle. While bodytaker plants can communicate telepathically, most do so only in desperate cases, preferring to speak through their podlings—potentially speaking through several at once.

A bodytaker plant grows where its seed fell from space, or it might later have its podlings relocate it to a hidden sanctuary. Roll on or choose a result from the Bodytaker Plant Sanctuary table to inspire where such a plant might grow.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwBodytakerPodl statblock caption=false classes="hide-in-embed"]

Bodytaker Plant.name: Bodytaker Plant
Bodytaker Plant.text:
A bodytaker plant uses its powerful vines to capture victims or accept captives brought to it by podling servants. The plant drags prey into an attached pod where the captive is dissolved over the course of hours. After the process is complete, a duplicate of the captive emerges as a subservient podling. Nothing of the original creature remains, though its noncompostable gear might be recovered after the bodytaker plant is slain.

A bodytaker plant’s podlings are extensions of the plant’s will, and the plant has no qualms about sacrificing podlings or using them as shields in battle. While bodytaker plants can communicate telepathically, most do so only in desperate cases, preferring to speak through their podlings—potentially speaking through several at once.

A bodytaker plant grows where its seed fell from space, or it might later have its podlings relocate it to a hidden sanctuary. Roll on or choose a result from the Bodytaker Plant Sanctuary table to inspire where such a plant might grow.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwBodytakerPlan statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Boneless.name: Boneless
Boneless.text:
Flayed Flesh with Murderous Intent

Habitat: Any; Treasure: None

Boneless are the animate skins of crushed or flensed humanoids. They rise from the corpses of those who suffered brutal ends such as flaying, crushing, or cannibalism. These hateful skins might seek vengeance, constricting and smothering victims using their elastic bodies. Alternatively, necromancers might create boneless to serve as stealthy assassins.

Vile magic-users might order a boneless to hang like a grotesque banner or adorn a skeleton or zombie. When foes approach, the boneless peels away from its surroundings or undead ally to face them.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwBoneless00000 statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.JaZ3Wjtk1xMUyXAO inline]

Brain in a Jar.name: Brain in a Jar
Brain in a Jar.text:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.RVEGLBM2qcdtVYXi inline]

Disembodied, Manipulative Mastermind

Habitat: Urban; Treasure: Arcana

A brain in a jar is the gray matter of one who cheated death through magic or strange surgeries. It now forces its will on those around it.

Some brains in jars are the result of strange experiments, bizarre punishments, or the aberrant preservation methods of creatures and @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwMiGo000000000]{mi-go} (detailed later in this chapter). A brain in a jar’s container might be an alien contraption, ostentatious glassware, or a mundane canister.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwBraininaJar00 statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Carrion Stalker.name: Carrion Stalker
Carrion Stalker.text:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.767zcpb8OqvkYEX9 inline]

Gut-Bursting Corpse Eater

Habitat: Swamp, Underdark, Urban; Treasure: None

Carrion stalkers are parasites that feed on corpses, infesting bodies with their maggot-like young. While stalkers can survive off corpses, they’re attracted to movement and ambush any who disturb them. The foul parasites attach to their prey, using their stingers to implant their larvae into living victims. If a carrion stalker doesn’t slay and consume its foe, its larvae grow rapidly within that creature. After a day of gestation, a larva bursts from its host as a mature carrion stalker in an eruption as horrifying as it is lethal.

Carrion stalkers have symbiotic relationships with carrion crawlers. Carrion crawlers won’t devour bodies infested by carrion stalkers, but they might pick up stalker larvae as they root among filth. The crawlers then spread these young, infecting sewers or graveyards with carrion stalkers. In return, carrion stalkers avoid preying on carrion crawlers.

Battlefields, mass graves, and similar sites make natural homes for carrion stalkers. While corpses left by plagues or wars might coax carrion stalkers into streets or alleys, these vile parasites rarely creep onto the surface unless they become an overwhelming infestation.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwCarrionStalke statblock caption=false classes="hide-in-embed"]

Carrionette.name: Carrionette
Carrionette.text:
Evil Toy Come to Life

Habitat: Urban; Treasure: Implements

When evil energies infest a children’s toy box, once-beloved playthings can become cursed monsters. Such is the case of the carrionette, an animated doll or puppet that uses its sinister magic to swap bodies with a living person.

Carrionettes can hide in plain sight amid a jumbled toy chest or crate of disused trinkets. A carrionette might wait patiently for years to be picked up by an unsuspecting victim—preferably an adventurer, a trader, or someone else with autonomy and a sturdy body—only to crawl out of the looter’s backpack in the middle of the night for a harrowing sneak attack.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwCarrionette00 statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.aYpoWkxQ72iT64so inline]

Death’s Heads.name: Death’s Heads
Death’s Heads.text:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.S7sIhkp6EfMgPsex inline]

Flying Undead Heads

Habitat: Forest, Swamp, Urban; Treasure: None

Death’s heads are disembodied, flying heads reanimated by wicked magic. These hateful heads vary in description and state of decay, with some possessing supernatural powers related to the beings they once were. Roll twice on or choose results from the Death’s Head Features table to inspire the features of a death’s head.

Death’s Head.name: Death’s Head
Death’s Head.text:
The most common type of death’s head arises from a person or animal, perhaps the victim of an unjust execution or a headhunting terror such as a @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwDullahan00000]{dullahan} (detailed later in this chapter). Flying and biting, a death’s head harries its victims until destroyed.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwDeathsHead000 statblock caption=false classes="hide-in-embed"]

Aberrant Death’s Head.name: Aberrant Death’s Head
Aberrant Death’s Head.text:
A death’s head that arises from an aberrant monster, such as a nothic or mind flayer, might retain a measure of the mind-warping power it had in life.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwAberrantDeath statblock caption=false classes="hide-in-embed"]

Death Head Features.name: Death Head Features

Podling Behaviors.name: Podling Behaviors
Podling Behaviors.text: @Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.tables.RollTable.rhwPodlingBeha58 rollable resultLabel="The Podling Exhibits the Strange Habit of …" classes="caption-top"]{Podling Behaviors}

Bodytaker Plant Sactuary.name: Bodytaker Plant Sactuary
Bodytaker Plant Sactuary.text: @Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.tables.RollTable.rhwBodytakerPlmU rollable resultLabel="The Bodytaker Plant Grows In …" classes="caption-top"]{Bodytaker Plant Sactuary}

Petrifying Death’s Head.name: Petrifying Death’s Head
Petrifying Death’s Head.text:
A death’s head animated from the head of a petrifying creature, like a medusa, retains the ability to temporarily petrify its victims.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwPetrifyingDea statblock caption=false classes="hide-in-embed"]

Death’s Head Tree.name: Death’s Head Tree
Death’s Head Tree.text:
Death’s head trees are blasphemous plants that grow death’s heads like foul fruit. They sprout from accursed lands, their roots snaking through mass graves, battlefields, or sites of terrible tragedies.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwDeathsHeadTre statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Dullahan.name: Dullahan
Dullahan.text:
Vengeful Headless Hunter

Habitat: Grassland, Hill, Urban; Treasure: Armaments

Headless, undying warriors, dullahans arise from decapitated villains and murderously seek to recover their missing heads. In their endless hunts, they terrorize sites relevant to their crimes or near where they died, beheading anyone they believe responsible (or connected to those responsible) for their deaths. Aside from their lethal blades, dullahans terrorize foes with their terrifying wails and by throwing the flame-wreathed skulls of past victims. Many pursue their victims astride undead or fiendish steeds.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.X5JZCcZE3L7ZOx9v inline]

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwDullahan00000 statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Elder Thing.name: Elder Thing
Elder Thing.text:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.YX5idwdiKM83KNIU inline]

Inscrutable Entity from Another Realm

Habitat: Mountain, Underdark; Treasure: Arcana

The aliens known as elder things have colonized countless worlds over unfathomable eons. Within hidden cyclopean cities, they collect mind-shattering secrets of the multiverse. Their empires have declined over the ages, suffering challenges by yithians and their creations, the shoggoths (both detailed later in this chapter). Those elder things that remain guard the remnants of their power and seek to subjugate those they consider lesser beings.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwElderThing000 statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Ez d’Avenir.name: Ez d’Avenir
Ez d’Avenir.text:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.COaNzaxr5pUOX0Mj inline classes="float-right size-three"]

Wandering Monster Hunter

Ez d’Avenir first encountered monsters among her manipulative family, the Radanaviches, who posed as Vistani to prey on travelers. Among their foul deeds, the Radanavich family kidnapped Erasmus van Richten, the son of Rudolph van Richten (detailed later in this chapter) and sold him to his death at the hands of the vampire Baron Metus. In the brief time Ez knew Erasmus, the youth spoke lovingly of a different sort of family than the one Ez knew. When Erasmus’s father tracked down Ez’s family and delivered her mother to justice, Ez didn’t stop the determined doctor.

In the years that followed, Ez joined a true Vistani band and adopted the name d’Avenir. She traveled far but never found the sense of belonging Erasmus described. She sought out Rudolph van Richten and, after a wary introduction, came to study with the doctor. During that time, she reconnected with Erasmus, now a ghost. She learned priceless lessons from the van Richtens, but she eventually left their household to pursue her own destiny.

Ez’s skillful slaying of monsters and her bravery in the face of supernatural perils have earned her a reputation as a folk hero and Mist wanderer of note. Although her adventures have cost her dearly—she has lost valuable bounties, dear companions, and her leg from the knee down—Ez works tirelessly to eradicate evil and protect innocents. Armed with wisdom from her mentor, encouragement from her departed friend, and hope forged in the depths of her heart, Ez aspires to someday create a place that feels safe enough to call home.

Whether she’s hunting monsters or investigating mysteries, Ez carries Vistani music with her wherever she goes. She boasts a significant repertoire of songs from across the Land of the Mists, which she’s happy to perform for anyone who travels alongside her.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.LEwzLd3uspq2WTYg inline]

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwEzdAvenir0000 statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Gallows Speaker.name: Gallows Speaker
Gallows Speaker.text:
Mass-Grave Ghost

Habitat: Grassland, Planar (Shadowfell), Swamp, Urban; Armaments: Arcana

 Gallows speakers are swirling maelstroms of restless spirits. They rise from sites of mass tragedies or pointless yet bloody battles. Gallows speakers rage endlessly with the weapons and death screams of the souls that compose its being, compelling those who come near to join in its suffering and uncontrollable violence.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.sum58K7WH2cNhX1l inline]

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwGallowsSpeake statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Gremishka.name: Gremishka
Gremishka.text:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.OKxjJgVnQSVvYeQ0 inline]

Magic-Reactive Mage Killer

Habitat: Urban; Armaments: Arcana

Gremishkas are magically unstable creations of misguided magic-users. These wildly destructive menaces delight in tormenting spellcasters and seek to destroy anything associated with them, particularly spellbooks, spell components, and familiars. If exposed to magic, a lone gremishka explodes into a destructive swarm of duplicate gremishkas.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwGremishka0000 statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Gug.name: Gug
Gug.text:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.aNUC76uelDu1lSfe inline]

Cavern-Stalking, Aberrant Glutton

Habitat: Underdark; Armaments: Armaments

Gugs are gigantic, subterranean horrors with heads split by toothy, vertical maws. They are stealthy predators that favor humanoid prey. Gugs fear and hate ghouls, perhaps because they compete to dominate the hidden necropolises and charnel depths of the Underdark, or for reasons related to a curse that forces gugs to dwell underground.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwGug0000000000 statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Jiangshi.name: Jiangshi
Jiangshi.text:
Vindictive, Vitality-Sapping Corpse

Habitat: Any; Treasure: Relics

When a soul becomes trapped within its corpse, its bitterness can reanimate its body, creating a jiangshi. These vengeful dead stalk their descendants, sowing terror and taking retribution for the slights or neglected burial rites that led to their cursed resurrections. Rigor mortis notoriously afflicts the limbs of jiangshi, causing them to hold their arms rigidly and to walk with a stiff gait. This, along with their flight, leads many to call them hopping vampires.

By day, jiangshi lurk within tombs or hidden ruins to avoid attention from the living. At night, they emerge to drain the life from those who dare wander in darkness. A Humanoid killed by a jiangshi rises as a life-hungry corpse that, if given the chance to slake its thirst, might also turn into jiangshi.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwJiangshi00000 statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.uE93gAvXQDYaVpp0 inline]

Loup Garou.name: Loup Garou
Loup Garou.text:
Legendary Lycanthrope

Habitat: Forest, Swamp, Urban; Treasure: Any

Loup garou are powerful lycanthropes capable of transforming into hulking dire wolves. They actively seek to spread the curse of lycanthropy, creating packs of subservient werewolves that can reduce communities to ruins and ravage whole regions. The strain of lycanthropy spread by a loup garou is harder to cure than typical varieties. Only slaying a loup garou can free the monster’s victims from their affliction.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.FOGfKN0KxQaltrYA inline]

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwLoupGarou0000 statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Madam Eva.name: Madam Eva
Madam Eva.text:
Duplicitous Diviner

The mysterious fortune teller Madam Eva shrouds herself in a carefully woven web of lies. Though she appears to be in her seventies, she is much older. She sets herself apart from any group, guarding the secrets of her origins and centuries-spanning agendas.

Madam Eva is a legendary figure known across the Land of the Mists. Few can rival her skill at interpreting omens, reading tarokka cards, or taming spirits during séances. While the fortunes she shares might have double meanings, they’re never wrong. She is renowned for revealing when best to enter the Mists to escape a domain or reach an intended destination, leading many travelers to seek her on the shores of the Tser Pool in Barovia to seek her insights. She often hints at knowledge of the Mists’ secrets and the tales (and weaknesses) of Darklords, and she’s not above revealing them for a price. Madam Eva holds grudges and usually shares her secrets only with those who do her a favor first. Whatever she discovers might soon also be known by Barovia’s Darklord, with whom she shares age-old ties.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwMadamEva00000 statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.uYtp50YOBD40gOVh inline]

Mi-Go.name: Mi-Go
Mi-Go.text:
Brain-Robbing Alien Fungus

Habitat: Forest, Hill, Mountain, Underdark; Treasure: Implements

Bizarre beings that profanely unite features of alien insects and otherworldly fungus, mi-go bend their unfathomable intellects toward colonizing worlds and controlling the inhabitants. They work with bizarre precision, learning all they can about how other creatures think and define identity. To this end, mi-go extract creatures’ brains for research and experimentation. Occasionally they swap brains between individuals, or they place stolen brains into the bodies of radically different creatures, then observe the results from afar.

Mi-go aren’t impeded by light, but they prefer the dark. They typically lurk in secluded caverns and amid remote heights.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwMiGo000000000 statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.NUiXKtMpLKDKecv4 inline]

Mist Horror.name: Mist Horror
Mist Horror.text:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.uDtGLlS4x2qY1oSL inline]

Shapeless Stalker in the Mists

Habitat: Any; Treasure: None

The Mists separating the Domains of Dread are far from safe—or empty. Mist horrors are animate clouds of vapor, barely perceptible within the Mists. In the presence of creatures, these vaporous terrors take the shape of their viewers’ greatest fears. Such appearances are subjective, and anyone who views a mist horror sees it in the way that terrifies them most. A mist horror outside the Mists swiftly loses cohesion and collapses into harmless vapor.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwMistHorror000 statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Mist Wanderer.name: Mist Wanderer
Mist Wanderer.text:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.PW27dUem3Xi00KGR inline]

Traveler Between Domains

Habitat: Any; Treasure: Arcana, Individual

Those who travel between the Domains of Dread develop traditions that hasten their travel and protect them from evil. Vistani, along with those who research their ways or the secrets of the Mists, are the most adept of such domain explorers.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwMistWanderer0 statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Mordenheim’s Monster.name: Mordenheim’s Monster
Mordenheim’s Monster.text:
Masterwork of Stolen Flesh

Habitat: Urban; Treasure: Individual

The creations of Dr. Viktra Mordenheim, Darklord of Lamordia, defy reason and classification as mere flesh golems. As she seeks to re-create her masterpiece, the Unbreakable Heart, Mordenheim has refined the creation of beings that are much more than the sum of their rotting parts. Known as Mordenheim’s monsters, these collections of body parts are reanimated into striking, mighty, thinking beings with wills of their own. While some might be convinced to serve their creator, others seek to recover some semblance of the lives they remember in fractured memories. Still others believe that they are something new and greater than what they were before and seek to create a new life—whether on their own or at the expense of communities that shun and fear them.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwMordenheimsMo statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.8lnBdTmwTntsaRgY inline]

Necrichor.name: Necrichor
Necrichor.text:
Reanimated Blood of an Evil Entity

Habitat: Underdark, Urban; Treasure: None

Necrichors are beings of sapient blood spawned from magical corruption, such as the gore of an obscene deity or a failed lich. They aspire to steal living bodies to fulfill megalomaniacal goals. Necrichors leave a trace of their essence within those they control, making necrichors difficult to destroy.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwNecrichor0000 statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.KrG81uTcFtzvZkDP inline]

Nightgaunt.name: Nightgaunt
Nightgaunt.text:
Faceless Fearmonger from the Far Realm

Habitat: Mountain, Underdark; Treasure: Implements, Individual

Inscrutable, gargoyle-like fiends, nightgaunts soar over subterranean mountain ranges and the skies of alien worlds. These hunters are infamous for abducting victims and dropping them from incredible heights. Those who have escaped nightgaunts describe the touch of their razor-like claws as “tickling” in a way that causes every sense to scream.

A nightgaunt’s head is a smooth, horned polyp. Before a nightgaunt kills, its head splits apart, revealing an otherworldly glow deep within.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.tm3BjnjSRyjicgoV inline]

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwNightgaunt000 statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Priest of Osybus.name: Priest of Osybus
Priest of Osybus.text:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.O100IWLMpCA3v43E inline]

Necromancer Cursed by Dark Powers

Habitat: Any; Treasure: Any

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.jAgmkBpKLuB2FAy3 classes="float-right size-two" cite=false caption=true]

The priests of Osybus are evil necromancers who steal souls to fuel their malevolent magic. Thanks to a foul bargain made with dread powers, these priests can defy death by transforming into undead.

Centuries ago, Osybus, a mysterious figure of immense ambition and evil, founded the order of priests that now bears his name. Osybus sought to use others’ souls to achieve immortality. He forged pacts with any entity that promised him more power, and he delved into any eldritch secret that could possibly prolong his life. He became a devotee of the Dark Powers and tapped into their immortal malice to fuel his apotheosis. As his power grew, he shared his secrets with disciples who wished to defy death in exchange for their worship.

Osybus achieved his goal and became a lich of almost godly power. He rewarded his disciples with a trace of his power in the form of shadowy tattoos. Osybus’s boon gave his priests the ability to steal souls and to cheat death by transforming into undead horrors.

The threat posed by Osybus and his disciples raised alarms far and wide. The Ulmist Inquisition—which at the time counted then-mortal Count Strahd von Zarovich as an ally—faced Osybus in battle. Fearing that their master eventually would consume their souls, Osybus’s disciples aided the inquisition by destroying Osybus’s physical form. As Osybus perished, he cursed his former allies: Their immortality would fail them eventually, and he would become one of the Dark Powers.

To rid themselves of this curse, Osybus’s former devotees turned to the same Dark Powers with whom their master had communed. They received a mission: Provide a mighty noble to serve as an earthly vessel for the Dark Powers to enter the world and conquer it. They found a suitable vessel in Strahd von Zarovich. Working in shadows and through intermediaries, the priests whispered hatred to the count. Once the priests corrupted Strahd’s heart, they laid the path before him that led to his fall into vampirism.

But Osybus hadn’t lied; he became one of the Dark Powers. Those powers conjured a misty prison to contain the newly immortal Strahd, thereby stymieing the priests’ ambitions to use the count as a weapon of conquest.

The priests of Osybus still seek to unleash Strahd from the Mists, often using adventurers as pawns. They continue to bear their hated founder’s name, for they know it is his original gift that gives them their horrific powers.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwPriestofOsybu statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Relentless Killers.name: Relentless Killers
Relentless Killers.text:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.9d39F5VBQHdjHXGJ inline]

Pact-Bound Murderers

Habitat: Any; Treasure: Armaments

Relentless killers are fiend-twisted maniacs that arise from mortals who make pacts with nefarious forces. Such killers’ powers vary depending on their obsessions and the source of their powers.

Relentless Slasher.name: Relentless Slasher
Relentless Slasher.text:
A relentless slasher pursues its prey with single-minded obsession. It conducts its bloody work in silence, then vanishes into shadow and infamy.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwRelentlessSla statblock caption=false classes="hide-in-embed"]

Relentless Nightmare.name: Relentless Nightmare
Relentless Nightmare.text:
A relentless nightmare uses foul magic to put its victims into a fitful slumber before attacking them. If destroyed, a relentless nightmare can re-form from the dreams of any creature marked with its curse.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwRelentlessNig statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Relentless Juggernaut.name: Relentless Juggernaut
Relentless Juggernaut.text:
Relentless juggernauts are massive brutes that ceaselessly stalk their targets. These juggernauts wield specially crafted blades and blunt stones as implements of obliteration.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwRelentlessJug statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Rudolph van Richten.name: Rudolph van Richten
Rudolph van Richten.text:
Cursed Vampire Hunter

In better days, Rudolph van Richten lived with his wife, Ingrid, and son, Erasmus, in their family home outside Rivalis in the domain of Darkon. The brash doctor Rudolph ran afoul of the Radanaviches, a family of brigands disguised as Vistani travelers. When the doctor refused to treat one of the family’s mortally ill members, the group’s leader, Irena Radanavich, ordered her band to kidnap Rudolph’s son. She then sold the young man to the vampire Baron Metus. Rudolph pursued the Radanaviches, shattered their criminal operation, and brought Irena to justice, but not before suffering her curse: “Live you always among monsters, and see everyone you love die beneath their claws.”

By the time Rudolph tracked down his missing son, the curse had already taken hold: Erasmus had been transformed into Metus’s vampire spawn. Rudolph put an end to his son’s suffering by pounding a wooden stake through the youth’s chest.

This violent deed begat more bloodshed: Baron Metus avenged his slain servant by killing Rudolph’s wife, Ingrid. Though Rudolph eventually slew the evil baron, he remains haunted by the horror of his family’s destruction.

Rudolph has committed himself to unmasking the evils lurking amid the Land of the Mists, hunting monsters, ending curses, and providing others with tools to do the same. His reputation and writings have spread among the domains. His published works, such as Van Richten’s Guide to Vampires and Van Richten’s Guide to Werebeasts, have earned him both adherents and disdain among scholarly communities. Nevertheless, his good works have touched lives and earned him allies far and wide.

Rudolph lives in Mordentshire in the domain of Mordent. From his modest herbalist’s shop, he corresponds with scholars and monster hunters across the domains, researches horrific creatures, and crafts tools to combat evil. Frequently he travels to other domains to conduct research or aid friends.

Over the years, Rudolph has adventured alongside heroes like monster hunter George Weathermay and detectives Alanik Ray and Arthur Sedgewick. A new generation of heroes, including the Ez d’Avenir and Gennifer and Laurie Weathermay-Foxgrove, have learned from the doctor and continue his fight against evil.

Unbeknownst to Rudolph, Erasmus watches over his father. The spirit of the artistic and thoughtful young man haunts the elder van Richten and aids him and his father’s allies with subtle direction and encouragement.

Rudolph knows that his age or vocation will eventually put an end to his endeavors. Increasingly he turns his research toward Barovia and its vampire overlord, Strahd von Zarovich, believing they hold keys to undoing myriad evils across the Land of the Mists.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.3v8Uxyqww4yPVDsT inline]

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwRudolphvanRic statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Shoggoth.name: Shoggoth
Shoggoth.text:
Hulking, Amorphous Predator

Habitat: Arctic, Coastal, Swamp, Underdark; Treasure: None

Shoggoths are massive, undulating horrors possessed of ancient and cruel intellects. Created by elder things (detailed in this chapter), the first shoggoths created cyclopean cities and performed dangerous labor in forgotten ages. Eventually, the shoggoths grew stronger and overthrew their makers. Since then, shoggoths have lurked in the ruins of their ancient cities and in hidden places across the multiverse. They tend to brood in remote, icy reaches where their bodies are invigorated by the blistering cold.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwShoggoth00000 statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.GF4fgKX1CjhgVVuI inline]

Star Spawn Emissaries.name: Star Spawn Emissaries
Star Spawn Emissaries.text:
Colossal, Shape-Shifting Alien Colonizers

Habitat: Any; Treasure: Any

Star spawn emissaries are the fingers of alien realms, digits that tip the scales of reality toward terror. Heralded by ominous astrological events, these ravenous invaders make worlds ready for their unimaginable masters. Employing two malleable forms, emissaries undermine perceptions of order, trust, and reality on global scales, readying worlds for sanity-shattering revelations and cascading apocalypses.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.Vj2GxmVd44yLJSrw cite=false]

Lesser Star Spawn Emissary.name: Lesser Star Spawn Emissary
Lesser Star Spawn Emissary.text:
A star spawn emissary’s lesser form allows it to appear as another creature. Emissaries readily appear as people, animals, or other creatures—the more unassuming, the better. Should an emissary’s true form be revealed and defeated, its greater form appears and seeks revenge moments or days later.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwLesserStarSpa statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Greater Star Spawn Emissary.name: Greater Star Spawn Emissary
Greater Star Spawn Emissary.text:
An emissary’s greater form is a destructive abomination. This massive amalgamation of violent flesh is composed of the meat and voices of every form the emissary has ever mimicked. Only once this greater form is destroyed is the emissary defeated.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwGreaterStarSp statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Star Spawn Emissary Lairs.name: Star Spawn Emissary Lairs
Star Spawn Emissary Lairs.text:
A star spawn emissary’s lair takes on macabre bodily features. A ruined watchtower might become a spire of pulsating flesh, or an abandoned mineshaft might transform into a maze of sticky, muscular innards.

The region containing a star spawn emissary’s lair becomes twisted by its presence, creating the following effects:

  • Cosmic Transformation. When a creature in the lair shape-shifts to its true form, creatures in a 10-foot Emanation originating from the shape-shifter take 10 (3d6) Force damage.

  • Terrifying Topography. Terrain in the lair is transformed into a mockery of flesh with unsettling features like thick veins, lolling tongues, and pulsating orifices. Creatures of the emissary’s choice within 1 mile of the lair have Disadvantage on saving throws to avoid or end the Frightened condition while in that area.

If the star spawn emissary dies or moves its lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.

Strahd Skeleton.name: Strahd Skeleton
Strahd Skeleton.text:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.sDNKGtexfPIXloVR inline]

Skeletal Soldier of the First Vampire

Habitat: Planar (Shadowfell), Underdark, Urban; Treasure: None

Vampires sometimes turn mortal servants unworthy of the gift of vampirism into skeletal soldiers. Known as Strahd skeletons—in recognition of the lord of Barovia who first created them—these silent, capable undead soldiers wield vampiric blades. Strahd skeletons that whet their blades in blood often carry their weapons back to their vampire lords. Tales disagree on whether vampires can feed from these bloody blades or merely revel in the sacrifice.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwStrahdSkeleto statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Strigoi.name: Strigoi
Strigoi.text:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.IZ8gB1QUE9VBcS4a inline]

Magically Mutated Bloodsucker

Habitat: Desert, Forest, Grassland, Hill, Mountain, Swamp, Underdark, Urban; Treasure: None

Strigoi are vampire-like beings that resemble half-humanoid, half-stirge terrors. By night, these bloodthirsty predators seek humanoids to feed on, draining victims and leaving behind bloodless corpses. Stirges and mosquitoes follow these repulsive monsters in their hunts.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwStrigoi000000 statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Swarms of Insects.name: Swarms of Insects
Swarms of Insects.text:
Flesh-Eating Parasites

Habitat: Coastal, Forest, Grassland, Swamp, Urban; Treasure: None

Few fates are more gruesome than falling victim to the parasitic or skeletonizing insect swarms that lurk in haunted sites and cursed dungeons.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.BP3DQggcc2f3QYOP inline]

Swarm of Infesting Insects.name: Swarm of Infesting Insects
Swarm of Infesting Insects.text:
Infesting insect swarms invade larger prey, burrowing into other creature’s bodies with varied, gruesome results.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwSwarmofInfest statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Swarm of Ravenous Insects.name: Swarm of Ravenous Insects
Swarm of Ravenous Insects.text:
Ravenous insect swarms rapidly overwhelm larger creatures. Those slain by these swarms are consumed swiftly and entirely.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwSwarmofRaveno statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Ulmist Inquisitors.name: Ulmist Inquisitors
Ulmist Inquisitors.text:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.QuGcvA38vQ0Efg9p inline]

Zealous Vanquishers of Evil Intent

Habitat: Any; Treasure: Any

“Evil lurks everywhere. With our minds, we will unearth it, we will plumb its depths, and we will annihilate it.” With those words, the psychically gifted priest Ulmed founded the Ulmist Inquisition, an order of psionic inquisitors that seeks to uncover and extinguish the wickedness lurking deep in people’s hearts. From the depraved city of Malitain on the Material Plane, the inquisition sends its members throughout the multiverse seeking to thwart the work of malevolent cults, reality-defying horrors, and the evil inherent to mortals. Sects of the inquisition exist within the Domains of Dread, working either in hidden cabals or infiltrating other faiths, such as the Church of Ezra.

The Ulmist Inquisition is organized into three orders named for Ulmed’s companions: the Order of Cosima, the Order of Ansel, and the Order of Tristian. Each order specializes in a different psionic power, and each bears a different symbol on its coat of arms. Some inquisitors entertain friendly rivalries with their fellows from other orders, but most put aside their grudges—at least temporarily—to work together against evil.

Inquisitor of the Mind Fire.name: Inquisitor of the Mind Fire
Inquisitor of the Mind Fire.text:
The Order of Cosima harnesses the Mind Fire—their name for the fire of thought that blazes within each person’s mind. Inquisitors of this order use their psychic powers to read thoughts, reshape memories, and dominate the recalcitrant. They represent their order with the symbol of a burning eye.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwFireInquisito statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Inquisitor of the Sword.name: Inquisitor of the Sword
Inquisitor of the Sword.text:
Inquisitors in the Order of Ansel subject themselves to harsh asceticism so they can empower their bodies with psionic energy. They are the martial arm of the inquisition, represented by a sword.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwSwordInquisit statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Inquisitor of the Tome.name: Inquisitor of the Tome
Inquisitor of the Tome.text:
The Order of Tristian harnesses the powers that come from careful study and observation rather than innate magic or honed muscle. They use telekinetic force to alter the environment, entrap their enemies, and destroy evildoers. As the inquisition’s scholars, they represent themselves with a tome.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwTomeInquisito statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Unspeakable Horror.name: Unspeakable Horror
Unspeakable Horror.text:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.aWKPGNBhBQHNYOC1 inline]

Terrifying Monster of the Mists

Habitat: Any; Treasure: None

Unspeakable horrors are anathema to all other life in the multiverse. Their shapes change constantly in ways that defy and disrupt reality. Those who look on these beings instantly understand their absolute and awful wrongness. Such revelations are often the last witnesses have before they’re dissolved by discordant, destructive forces.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwUnspeakableHo statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Vampires.name: Wampiry
Vampires.text:
Monstrous Life Drinkers

Habitat: Underdark, Urban; Treasure: Any

Horrific varieties of blood-drinking vampires spread their wickedness across the Domains of Dread.

Vampire Nosferatu.name: Vampire Nosferatu
Vampire Nosferatu.text:
Nosferatu are vicious, feral hunters cursed with a primal offshoot of the curse of vampirism. These ravenous, graceless predators stalk the night, desperately seeking blood and barely remembered comforts of life.

Nosferatu feed on anything with blood. Heaps of mutilated rats, stables turned into slaughterhouses, and fields covered in massacred livestock might mark a nosferatu’s presence. Nosferatu lurk in forgotten places scoured of the living, such as charnel caves, cursed ruins, and desecrated barrow mounds.

For a few moments after feeding, nosferatu become lucid enough to recollect glimpses of their past lives. Even in these instances, though, they remain duplicitous schemers.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwVampireNosfer statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.eK2gNR5UWrqSYq80 inline]

Vampire Mind Flayer.name: Vampire Mind Flayer
Vampire Mind Flayer.text:
Vampire mind flayers originate from the alien domain of Bluetspur (see “Other Domains of Dread” in chapter 2). In seeking a cure for their degenerating elder brain, the illithids of that realm tried radical experiments. The results were mind flayer tadpoles infected with vampirism. These terrors developed into mature mind flayers capable of harvesting psychic sustenance from sapient minds. After feeding on psionic energies, vampire mind flayers return to Bluetspur and are reabsorbed by the elder brain, releasing stolen psionic essences amid a hormone brine.

Vampire mind flayers are bestial creatures that lack control over their psychic abilities. Rather than focused mind blasts, they unleash waves of nauseating, mentally disruptive sensations. They use their viciously barbed tentacles to drain their victims’ mental energy.

Mind flayers loathe vampire mind flayers and view them as abominations. Nevertheless, they employ their warped brethren in desperate pursuits, such as preserving the elder brain of Bluetspur.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwVampireMindFl statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.zlQYa1zZrT1H6vMh inline]

Waxwork.name: Waxwork
Waxwork.text:
Murderous Wax Replacement

Habitat: Urban; Treasure: Implements

Realistic humanoid sculptures come to life, waxworks seek to kill and replace those they’ve been crafted to resemble. These creatures typically believe they’re the original living beings rather than duplicates. Any waxwork confronted with evidence of its true nature flies into a rage until it or its double is destroyed.

In rare cases, a waxwork might live as if it were a person. Such individuals are typically callous and vain. Some seek to create more waxworks to replace more people.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwWaxwork000000 statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.wFFC9mLSy7wzcgIk inline]

Weathermay-Foxgrove Twins.name: Weathermay-Foxgrove Twins
Weathermay-Foxgrove Twins.text:
Adventurous Paranormal Investigators

Twins Gennifer and Laurie Weathermay-Foxgrove are monster hunters who’ve built a reputation as heroes in the domain of Mordent, and their reputation is spreading in domains beyond. The daughters of the mayor of Mordentshire, Alice Weathermay, the twins can often be found at their home, Heather House, or at that of their adopted uncle, Doctor Rudolph van Richten.

In their youth, Gennifer and Laurie channeled their curiosity and propensity for troublemaking into solving local mysteries. The twins’ uncle—renowned monster hunter George Weathermay—returned to Mordent with his fiancée, Natalia Vhorishkova, just before the twins’ sixteenth birthday. The twins realized Natalia was manipulating their beloved uncle and eventually exposed her as a werewolf who was using George to pursue Rudolph van Richten. After the twins saved their uncle and the doctor from the werewolf, George and Rudolph encouraged the girls’ investigative instincts by training them as monster hunters.

Ever since, Gennifer and Laurie have aided Rudolph in his research and on adventurers across the Land of the Mists. Increasingly, the twins have struck out on their own, allying with heroes such as Ez d’Avenir and Savra Sunstar to solve mysteries and hunt evil across domains.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.txHrhAX0N3ZgtN6Q cite=false]

Gennifer Weathermay-Foxgrove.name: Gennifer Weathermay-Foxgrove
