He won’t fight the characters, but he uses the necklace Frantosh wears to transform the singer into a @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmWerewolf000000]{Werewolf}. Frantosh and four more Werewolf servants of Harkon assault the characters.

If Frantosh didn’t succeed on three or more checks during the show, Harkon has grown bored with Frantosh. The characters find Frantosh Bloodied and being attacked by four Werewolves.

Conclusion. If the characters escape the Old Kartakan with Frantosh (potentially now a Werewolf), each earns a bonus [[/award 1700xp]]. Even if forced into battle, Harkon Lukas won’t kill the characters—they’ve intrigued the Darklord, and he might appear to toy with them later.

Harkon Lukas.name: Harkon Lukas
Harkon Lukas.text:
Darklord Who Preys on Talent

The charming werewolf Harkon Lukas manipulates those around him to satisfy his narcissistic needs for adulation and control. Endlessly seeking fame, Harkon travels widely, earning applause and accolades. Yet, whenever he returns to a town he’s performed in, he finds he’s been forgotten. Lukas believes he needs just the right song or inspiration to make himself unforgettable. To that end, he searches for talented protégés, cultivating their skills, then stealing their work. He ensures their loyalty by gifting them copies of his cursed wolf-tooth necklace, Harkon’s Bite, or by transforming them into werewolves. He then manipulates these victims into believing only he will accept them.

Harkon wears a signature wide-brimmed hat and wolf-tooth necklace, and he carries a violin he calls Bleeding Heart. He uses flattery to charm those he takes an interest in. A skilled artist who declines the offer to become his protégé often earns his obsession. The Darklord might go to any extreme to claim such performers’ talents as his own.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwHarkonLukas00 statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Harkon’s Tale

Harkon Lukas weaves numerous self-aggrandizing tales of his origins. Among them, Harkon tells of being spurned for his ambitions by the werewolves that raised him. Through talent and sacrifice, he became a legendary performer, and people flocked to bask in his remarkable presence.

Eventually, the rulers of Harkon’s homeland realized the threat posed by Harkon’s growing fame. Officials tried to imprison him for invented crimes, but Harkon’s fans defended him. When royal agents came for him, Harkon feigned his death, and his followers revolted. When outraged masses bearing Harkon’s coffin besieged the royal palace, Harkon ended his ploy. He burst from his crimson coffin in wolf form and devoured the land’s monarch. Drenched in blood, the “resurrected” Harkon returned to his human form and donned the monarch’s crown amid the cheers of his deluded followers. Yet before Harkon’s reign could begin, the Mists rose around him. When they cleared, Harkon found himself in an unfamiliar land called Kartakass.

Harkon’s Domain

Kartakass is altered by Harkon’s presence, creating the following effects:

Beast Spies. From dawn until dusk, Tiny Beasts in the domain magically gain the ability to understand Common and can communicate telepathically with other Beasts and Monstrosities.

Children of the Night. Harkon exerts influence over the animals of his domain. From dusk until dawn, Medium or smaller Beasts within 1 mile of Harkon have the Charmed condition. If Harkon pleases, these animals become Hostile to creatures other than Harkon and his allies.

Closing the Borders. While in his domain, Harkon can close or open the borders to Kartakass at will. While the borders are closed, a strange melody fills the Mists surrounding Kartakass. A creature that enters the Mists for the first time on a turn or starts its turn in them must succeed on a [[/save 15 wis format=long]], or it has the Unconscious condition for [[/r 1d6#Hours]] hours, then awakes within sight of the town of Harmonia in Kartakass. A creature makes this save only once per turn.

If Harkon dies, these effects end immediately.

Harkon’s Lair

The Darklord owns a tavern in Skald called the Old Kartakan Inn and Taverna. The inn occupies a small island on the Sing Song River. Nightly, Harkon Lukas or artists under his tutelage perform there.

Old Kartakan Inn and Taverna

Welcoming and rustic, the Old Kartakan features subtle wolf-themed decor. The site is depicted on map 2.22 and has the following features:

Bedrooms. The inn’s rooms are insulated to block the sounds of performances and maulings alike.

Harkon Lukas’s Room. Several secret doors and a trapdoor lead to Harkon’s tower bedroom, where he hides evidence of his own uninspired music and trophies from past victims.

Secret Doors and Chambers. The inn is riddled with secret spaces to spy into other chambers, hide bodies, or indulge in bestial meals.

Taproom. Among the drinks the taverna offers is Meekulbrau, a blood-red berry wine that soothes the throat and improves vocal performances.

Lamordia.name: Lamordia
Lamordia.text:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.yVdVAzO9TfuF53u1]

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.64ms3WiKjJjVPwPY]

Domain of Snow and Stitched Flesh

Darklord: @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwViktraMordenh]{Viktra Mordenheim}

Adventure Genres: Body horror and gothic horror

Hallmarks: Amoral science, bizarre constructs, frigid wilderness, mutagenic radiation

Mist Talismans: @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.items.Item.rhwAnimatedDigit]{Animated digits}, @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.items.Item.rhwGlowingMinera]{glowing minerals}, @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.items.Item.rhwPreservedHuma]{preserved humanoid limb}

Aligned Tarokka Card: Transmuter

Opposed Tarokka Card: Marionette

A domain of amoral science, Lamordia shudders amid the icy environment and terror of its residents’ creations. Revolutions of steam and steel spur Lamordia’s people into frenzies of amoral invention. From industrialized communities and the iron walls of Ludendorf, the domain’s largest city, flow streams of scrap and chemical poisons that taint the land and transform creatures into horrors. Yet, the domain’s reckless scientists don’t pursue just mechanized inventions—the greatest goal is reinvention of life itself, transmuting people into ideals and unlocking generations of potential. In the name of such ambitions, the depraved reshape life and unleash abominations. And no aspirations have unleashed greater evils than those of the domain’s Darklord, Dr. Viktra Mordenheim.

Domain Features

Lamordia is known for the following features:

Frigid Environs. Lamordia is a frigid land of bleak mountains, frozen swaps, and icy seas. Few voluntarily trek into its wilderness, fearing freak blizzards and starving beasts.

Mutated Creatures. Creatures warped by alchemy roam Lamordia. The most infamous are the monstrous “whales” of the Sea of Secrets—violent, unnatural leviathans, no two of which are alike.

Rational Culture. The people of Lamordia are direct and skeptical of superstition. They revere scientific innovation—as it’s all that stands between them and a life of frigid starvation. Magic is acknowledged but widely considered backward.

Steam Technology. Steam power driven by boiling sewers fuels clockwork inventions and massive cranes in the city of Ludendorf.

Locations in Lamordia

Lamordia is a frigid land. In the north, the Sea of Secrets is choked with ice, while the mountains known as the Sleeping Beast are never without snow. The cities of Ludendorf and Neufurchtenburg are detailed below, and other notable locations are summarized on the Lamordia Locations table.

Ludendorf

The city of Ludendorf is a morass of steam-powered factories operating at the behest of morally bankrupt barons. Boiling sewers and steam tunnels thread the city’s foundations, heating cramped tenements and powering cranes serving the city’s fleet of ironclad ships. These ice-breaking, whaling vessels dare the Sea of Secrets to bring back massive beasts that provide vital food and fuel for the city.

At the city’s heart, the unscrupulous Ludendorf University conducts risky investigations into such fields as alchemical combustion, automated ingenuity, chemical sentience, and speculative anatomy. The university continually needs new subjects for experiments and hires volunteers or pays individuals for “flesh rights”—ownership of their cadavers once they die. A secret society within Ludendorf University idolizes Viktra and follows the reckless paths laid by her work.

Neufurchtenburg

The smoking refineries of Neufurchtenburg process minerals harvested from the Sleeping Beast. These rare, little-understood elements are then shipped to Ludendorf’s factories. Radiation-warped monsters from the nearby bogs frequently attack, disrupting operations, but the town’s people use hulking clockwork machines, cutting-edge firearms, and destructive alchemy to fend off incursions.

From her laboratory at Schloss Mordenheim, Dr. Viktra Mordenheim uses her scientific genius to create horrors and wonders. All manner of undead and constructs march from her laboratory, but such flawed creations don’t satisfy Mordenheim. Rather, she seeks to re-create or reclaim her greatest creation, the Unbreakable Heart, a miraculous device that grants life to Mordenheim’s elusive reanimated victim, Elise.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.hpiNbElHdRcx9HtC cite=false]

Lamordia Locations

Location

Description

Chokingmere

Lamordia’s ancient druidic peoples buried their dead in the Chokingmere. Prospectors seeking pockets of rare gas in this swamp disturb forgotten ritual sites and bog mummy burial sites.

Forest of Rust

Ludendorf dumps its waste and scrap metal in the Forest of Rust. Creatures of living iron, @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmRustMonster000]{Rust Monsters}, and hungry beasts prowl the wooded junk heaps.

Isles of Agony

Lamordians know the Isles of Agony as freezing death traps. At the hidden sanctuary of Hope’s Heart, Mordenheim’s runaway creation, Elise, shelters all manner of outcasts.

Lorelei Falls

The waters of the Boiling River are poisoned by mining. Those who listen at Lorelei Falls claim to hear screaming, as though the water were burning someone behind the falls.

Schloss Aubrecker

Baron Rudolph von Aubrecker, the reclusive ruler of Lamordia, sends proclamations to his people from the remote castle Schloss Aubrecker to hide the fact that he’s a @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwBraininaJar00]{Brain in a Jar} (see chapter 5).

Schloss Cutter

A former academic, Shoshan Cutter isolates her family at her ancestral home, Schloss Cutter. She supports any who fight against greed and amorality cloaked as innovation.

Schloss Mordenheim

Dr. Viktra Mordenheim’s home, Schloss Mordenheim, is an imposing castle that hosts the doctor’s laboratory and servitor creations. She frequently needs new lab assistants.

The Sleeping Beast

Radioactive minerals vein this jagged mountain range. Legends claim the range is a single titanic, sleeping monster and that its blood and bones are the source of rare minerals.

Tallman’s Wood

Tallman’s Wood is the storied home of Tallman, a being that “wears” a too-tall, humanlike body. Tallman kidnaps those who sleep in its wood but also knows much of other lands.

Whale End

A theater overlooks the fishing village of Whale End. Concrete encloses every entrance to the theater, and the sealed main entry bears a message: “Forgive me, all those I love.”

Winter’s Mouth

Tales say the sea answers questions posed to it through a fissure in the ice called Winter’s Mouth. Those who fall into the waters here return plagued by visions of the drowned.

Adventures in Lamordia.name: Adventures in Lamordia
Adventures in Lamordia.text:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.SNQzkrwpowDTHkdS inline]

Lamordia provides a stage for adventures featuring amoral science and altered or resurrected beings. Use this section to develop horror adventures in this domain.

Horror in Lamordia

Adventures in Lamordia often include the following elements.

Elise von Brandthofen

Elise knows she died yet finds herself alive again. She is now a @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwMordenheimsMo]{Mordenheim’s Monster} (see chapter 5), and her life is sustained by the Unbreakable Heart (see the following section). Elise is learning to accept her new life but avoids Lamordia’s people, who label her as an outcast and a monster. She also knows that Viktra Mordenheim seeks to recover the Unbreakable Heart. The doctor’s actions—as well as the mercenaries she sends to abduct Elise—have shattered the pair’s bond. Now Elise hides at a secret sanctuary in the Isles of Agony called Hope’s Heart. Here, she shelters other victims and creations of Viktra, showing them the esteem their creator denies them. Those in Elise’s care have taken to calling themselves the Children (detailed below).

Mordenheim’s Masterpiece

The Unbreakable Heart is a nonmagical scientific wonder. Despite being the creator of the Unbreakable Heart, Viktra can’t re-create it and is obsessed with reclaiming it and rediscovering its secrets.

@UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.items.Item.rhwUnbreakableHe]

@embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.items.Item.rhwUnbreakableHe inline]

The Children

The Children are discarded creations of Dr. Viktra Mordenheim who have found shelter with Elise. Composed a few dozen Constructs and chemically animated Undead, the Children follow Elise’s lead and seek to live peacefully away from those who would misunderstand and harm them.

The exception to this is Adam, a @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwMordenheimsMo]{Mordenheim’s Monster} who recently joined the Children. Adam was created by Viktra to capture Elise, but Elise convinced him to join her instead. Unlike Elise, Adam was created from disparate parts and knows no previous life. Although Elise seeks to teach Adam humanity, he shows little respect for the lives of more fragile beings. Moreover, Adam is fascinated by Mordenheim’s work and quietly seeks ways to create life that will serve him.

Lamordia Campaign Arc

Campaigns set in Lamordia often make the characters subjects or accomplices to Viktra’s schemes. A campaign here might follow this outline:

Levels 1–2. A dean of Ludendorf University hires the characters to escort the coach of students traveling to an assistantship at Schloss Mordenheim. Along the way, they are plagued by mismatched @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmHomunculus0000]{Homunculi} that sabotage their travels. These are members of the Children, seeking to stymie folk they take for Mordenheim’s allies.

Levels 3–6. At Schloss Mordenheim, @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwViktraMordenh]{Viktra Mordenheim} hires the character to recover her invention, the Unbreakable Heart. She explains it was stolen by a monster that dwells on the Isles of Agony. Viktra wishes the characters to bring back the heart and the monster. The characters face dangers in the wilds, but the quest culminates in the “Isles of Agony” adventure (see below).

The conclusion of the campaign depends on whether the characters aid Viktra or Elise. When they return to Schloss Mordenheim, events might play out in the following ways:

Levels 7–9 (Aiding Viktra). If the characters aid Dr. Mordenheim, the doctor seeks to remove the Unbreakable Heart from Elise or test it by reanimating a new being. This results in a dramatic operation where an enraged @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwMordenheimsMo]{Mordenheim’s Monster} (see chapter 5) breaks free and rampages through the castle.

Levels 7–9 (Aiding Elise). If the characters aid Elise, the characters must free innocent victims of the doctor from the castle and prevent Dr. Mordenheim from animating new terrors by destroying her laboratory. Ultimately the characters must face Viktra Mordenheim (detailed earlier in this section) and two Flesh Golems made from the assistants the characters brought to the castle.

The campaign might continue with the characters continuing to aid or being targeted by the Children.

Isles of Agony.name: Isles of Agony
Isles of Agony.text:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.gfVAWabtwpy2Xgtx cite=false]

@UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.adventures.Adventure.W27hfVZBc6HARasx]{Adventure for Level 5 Characters}

Situation. The characters must recover the @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.items.Item.rhwUnbreakableHe]{Unbreakable Heart} (detailed earlier in this section). They don’t know it’s inside the outcast Elise.

Hook. Viktra hires the characters to recover the Unbreakable Heart, which was stolen by a monster. They are offered [[/award 2500gp]], plus a [[/award 1000gp]] bonus if they capture the monster.

Encounters

The adventure begins when the characters reach the Isles of Agony and consists of these encounters.

@UUID[Actor.PllGD74fBLVyqCF9]{Shipwreck.} Soon after reaching the icy isles, the characters find six human survivors of a wrecked whaling ship. These sailors (@UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmPirate00000000]{Pirates}) are led by @UUID[Actor.GPUd9tglTHRlrTKc]{Mate Herzogin,} and their captain was carried off by a half-human, half-machine monster. The survivors seek help returning home but want to recover their captain’s corpse (and their pay, which he carried).

The Monster. The monster the survivors fear is one of the Children, a @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmFleshGolem0000]{Flesh Golem} named @UUID[Actor.Rm54Mr8fw8lJSZS2]{Kartad.} He’s peaceful, but the survivors’ captain, @UUID[Actor.2jfFI8vkNpWyXklT]{Hans Giesbrecht} (Medium @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmPirateCaptain0]{Pirate Captain}), attacked him on sight. Kartad knocked out Hans, panicked, picked him up, and fled to Hope’s Heart.

Track of the Monster. Characters who succeed on a [[/check survival 12 format=long]] find and can follow Kartad’s tracks to Hope’s Heart. If the characters fail, a survivor points out the path. Roll on or choose results from the following table for three encounters that happen on the way (rerolling duplicates).

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.tables.RollTable.rhwTrackoftheMGr rollable resultLabel="Encounter" caption=false]

Hope’s Heart. Hope’s Heart is a hidden canyon home to numerous harmless Constructs that is accessed via a passage in an abandoned cabin. When the characters approach, @UUID[Actor.A7LExw1XxvxxaPvz]{Elise} appears on the cliffs 20 feet above and demands to know their business. Elise is a @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwMordenheimsMo]{Mordenheim’s Monster} (see chapter 5). The @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.items.Item.rhwUnbreakableHe]{Unbreakable Heart} glows from her chest.

Fighting Elise. Elise attacks to prevent entry to the cabin and to defend herself. She avoids killing and won’t pursue those who flee. If the characters attack, they’ll likely have to make multiple attempts to wear down Elise or use trickery to defeat her.

Negotiating with Elise. Elise is Indifferent to the characters or Hostile if they harmed any Constructs on the isle. She’s wary of those who working for Viktra and can share the details described in @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwLamordia00000.JournalEntryPage.ViktraMordenheZm#viktras-tale]{“Viktra’s Tale.”} If a character explains they’re seeking the missing captain, she brings forth @UUID[Actor.2jfFI8vkNpWyXklT]{Hans} and @UUID[Actor.Rm54Mr8fw8lJSZS2]{Kartad.} Kartad has nursed Hans back to health; he apologizes and releases the bewildered captain.

The Crew. The @UUID[Actor.PllGD74fBLVyqCF9]{six survivors} arrive to see the characters speaking with Kartad. They overreact on seeing the “monster” and initiate combat. Kartad panics and defends himself. Hans has 10 Hit Points but is too weak to command his crew or fight.

Aftermath. If the characters fight alongside the crew, Kartad and Elise defend themselves but flee after 3 rounds or if anyone is killed. Elise leads her followers to hide elsewhere on the island. The sailors thank the characters and offer to help them rally aid in Ludendorf to return and hunt the monsters.

If the characters deescalate the conflict or defend Kartad, Elise’s attitude toward them improves. She won’t give them the Unbreakable Heart or return to Schloss Mordenheim. Rather, she tries to convince the characters to see Viktra for the villain she is. She entreats them to return to the castle, save captive members of the Children, and thwart the doctor, as detailed in @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwLamordia00000.JournalEntryPage.AdventuresinLaI9#lamordia-campaign-arc]{“Lamordia Campaign Arc.”} In return, Elise offers a trove of [[/award 4000gp]] and an @UUID[Compendium.dnd-dungeon-masters-guide.equipment.Item.dmgImmovableRod0]{Immovable Rod}.

Characters who defeat or choose to aid Elise earn a bonus [[/award 1100xp]].

Viktra Mordenheim.name: Viktra Mordenheim
Viktra Mordenheim.text:
Darklord Who Pursues Perfection

From her laboratory at Schloss Mordenheim, Dr. Viktra Mordenheim uses her scientific genius to create horrors and wonders. All manner of undead and constructs march from her laboratory, but such flawed creations don’t satisfy Mordenheim. Rather, she seeks to re-create or reclaim her greatest creation, the Unbreakable Heart, a miraculous device that grants life to Mordenheim’s elusive reanimated victim, Elise.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwViktraMordenh statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Viktra’s Tale

From an early age, Viktra Mordenheim sought to understand the complexities of anatomy. She pursued medicine not to aid patients but to reveal the secrets of life. Eventually, her research bent toward reanimating dead flesh and improving humanoid bodies. When Viktra began employing thieves to procure specimens for her tests, she met Elise, a beautiful resurrectionist whose spontaneity

entranced the methodical surgeon. Viktra was smitten, and with Elise she discovered there might be more to life than chemical reactions.

When Elise started exhibiting signs of an incurable disease, Viktra hastened her experiments. On the doctor’s operating table, victims were killed, returned to life, and died again as Viktra sought a breakthrough. When finally Elise passed away, Viktra refused to accept failure. The doctor worked feverishly crafting her masterpiece, an artificial organ she called the Unbreakable Heart. But as she fitted the device into Elise’s corpse, constables burst into the lab and accused the doctor of numerous murders. As Viktra struggled against arrest, smoking chemicals, arcing electricity, and a strange mist filled the laboratory.

When the chaos cleared, Viktra awoke in Lamordia, an unfamiliar land where her genius was celebrated. Elise and the Unbreakable Heart were nowhere to be found, but the doctor soon heard rumors of a wondrous and terrifying figure who wandered the icy realm and whose chest glowed as though her heart were the sun. Ever since, Viktra has continued her experiments, striving to re-create her successes but failing every time. Between disappointments she searches for whatever Elise has become—and with her, the miracle of the Unbreakable Heart.

Viktra’s Domain

Lamordia is altered by Viktra’s presence, creating the following effects:

Air of Obsession. When a creature fails a Constitution saving throw to maintain Concentration, that creature takes [[/damage 1d6 psychic average]] damage.

Closing the Borders. While in her domain, Viktra can close or open the borders to Lamordia. While the borders are closed, lightning storms roll through the Mists surrounding Lamordia. A creature that enters the Mists for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there makes a [[/save dex 15 format=long]], taking [[/damage 4d6 lightning average]] damage on a failed save and half as much on a successful one. A creature makes this save only once per turn.

If Viktra dies, these effects end immediately.

Viktra’s Lair

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.ExaVT7WPCIVIsdWH cite=false classes="float-right"]

Schloss Mordenheim is a castle that stands amid dramatic cliffs overlooking the sea. Travelers from Ludendorf reach Schloss Mordenheim by crossing the ice and following a treacherous road.

Schloss Mordenheim

Schloss Mordenheim holds dozens of rooms, but most are empty or have furnishings pushed aside to make room for scientific supplies. Prominent locations from this site are depicted on map 2.24 and have the following features:

Auxiliary Workshops. Viktra’s assistants toil in workshops where they craft tools and devices to the doctor’s exacting specifications.

Disused Chambers. Rooms originally constructed for relaxation serve as storage rooms, act as extra laboratories, or sit eerily empty. A dining hall half filled with crates is set for a single diner, but it’s rarely used.

Gate and Entry. A stone bridge crosses a chasm leading to the castle’s keep. Stairs flanked by grim statuary lead to the keep’s entrance.

Laboratory Tower. A heavily renovated turret holds Viktra’s laboratory. Mechanisms allow the tower’s roof to part and its operating table to rise 30 feet, exposing subjects to lightning during the island’s frequent storms.

Mordent.name: Mordent
Mordent.text:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.s97kkxbrindavhSy]

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.62zz3YprBlvbqmmr]

Domain of the Haunted

Darklord: @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwWilfredGodefr]{Wilfred Godefroy}

Adventure Genre: Ghost stories

Hallmarks: Ancestral curses, haunted mansions, mist-shrouded moors, vengeful spirits

Mist Talismans: @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.items.Item.rhwBrokenJewelry]{Broken jewelry}, @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.items.Item.rhwDeathMask0000]{death mask}, @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.items.Item.rhwFadedLoveLett]{faded love letter}, @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.items.Item.rhwFamilyPortrai]{family portrait}

Aligned Tarokka Card: Miser

Opposed Tarokka Card: Horseman

In Mordent, the dead don’t rest. Here, farmers and fishers toil in scattered villages, cleaving to stoic, hardworking traditions. In the manors of landed, aristocratic families, the bearers of noble names view themselves as conservators of the land and of stifling reputations. Tradition, memory, and expectation haunt the lives of Mordent’s people, and when they die, nothing changes.

Little known to the living, the dead in Mordent rise as spirits, and for most—knowing no other ways—their work continues. Abandoned estates hide spectral generations of frustrated nobles and their downtrodden servants. While some spirits drift idly or deteriorate to mere impressions of their past selves, others greedily cling to their former possessions and ambitions. Some pernicious spirits become infamous hauntings, while others—such as the domain’s Darklord, the ghostly Lord Wilfred Godefroy—seek to dominate the living and dead.

Domain Features

Mordent is known for the following features:

Lingering Spirits. All manner of spectral undead and other hauntings lurk in Mordent. Ghost stories and rumors of haunted sites are common.

Local Nobility. Mordent is governed by town mayors and local lords. Few nobles remain, and some have only decrepit manors left to their names.

Old Faiths. People casually worship vague deities in Mordent, but evidence of forgotten druidic traditions lies scattered around the wilds.

Spiritualism. Attempting to communicate with the dead is a popular pursuit for investigators, bored nobles, and charlatans.

Tradition and Shame. The people of Mordent follow traditions that prioritize hard work, self-sufficiency, and avoiding public embarrassment. Locals overlook blatant strangeness so not to shame their neighbors, and every family has secrets.

Locations in Mordent

Mordent is a foggy land of forests, fields, and moors that endures frequent rainstorms. The land is dotted with tiny farming hamlets and the rotting homes of forgotten nobles.

The town of Mordentshire is detailed below, and other notable locations are summarized on the Mordent Locations table.

Mordentshire

Mordentshire is the largest community in Mordent. Sturdy fisherfolk work the town docks and, after dusk, fill crowded tavern common rooms. Cold winds frequently blow in from Arden Bay. When they stop, they’re replaced by a shroud of fog the locals call “the breath of the dead.”

To the chagrin of the town’s no-nonsense populace, Mordentshire is home to a small community of scholars and adventurers. Saulbridge Sanitarium provides a refuge for the ill while also welcoming researchers from other domains. Across town, an unassuming herbalist shop is run by @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwRudolphvanRic]{Rudolph van Richten} (see chapter 5), an investigator of all things monstrous and mysterious.

The lands around Mordentshire host two prominent estates. Heather House is the home of the Weathermay family, regional lords who count numerous heroes among their number. To the east stands Gryphon Manor, the notorious House on Gryphon Hill. The mansion has a history of violence, tragedy, and hauntings and is the home of the domain’s Darklord, @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwWilfredGodefr]{Lord Wilfred Godefroy}.

Lord Wilfred Godefroy rules the afterlife of all who die in Mordent. Arrogant, impatient, and vengeful, the Darklord seeks any opportunity to exert his control and reinforce his undying noble standing.

Lord Godefroy haunts the House on Gryphon Hill, along with countless other spirits. Wilfred can send mobs of obedient spirits to do his will anywhere in the domain. He uses his control over the dead to influence the living, threatening to harm his victims’ dead loved ones if they don’t obey his fickle commands.

A generation ago, the creators of the mist-piercing device called the Apparatus vanished from Mordent. Lord Godefroy has discovered the hidden machine, but, to his frustration, lacks a way to use it.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.OyNJLCgvdU2DVR3Q cite=false]

Mordent Locations

Location

Description

Abbey Point

A group of silent Ezra worshippers maintain a sanctuary at Abbey Point. Maps there claim that only the sea lies north of the abbey, making the nearby wooded coast a mystery.

Blairsbay

Little more than a dock, market, and tavern, Blairsbay serves a line of coastal noble estates. Many of these great homes are abandoned, including Lumley House and Preston Hill.

Candle Cross

The hamlet of Candle Cross spills from the remains of a ruined castle, the drawbridge of which extends over the Arden River. The bridge rises of its own accord to bar evil from entering.

Crawford

Near the village of Crawford, countless of ravens roost at Crawford Manor. A carving in the manor reads: “The Death of Ravens Shalt Thou Abhor, Lest the Crawford Line Be Nevermore.”

Glaston

The residents of the village of Glaston cleave to druidic traditions. Rumors say ancient druids forged magical passages between the nearby Sigil Lakes and similar sites in realms beyond.

Glenwich

Years ago, the sand cliff that bore half the village of Glenwich—including its ancient church—collapsed into the bay. Locals avoid the tide pools mingling amid dilapidation and the dead.

Haworth Hall

Haworth Hall numbers among the domain’s dozens of lonely, decaying manors. Something inhabits the estate, but whatever it is terrifies Mordent’s ghost and they avoid it at all costs.

Idlethorp

Miniature constructs made of stitched-together body parts run amok in this abandoned hamlet’s Punchinel Manor, once home to an eccentric inventor.

Locustbranch Hospital

Locustbranch was once a renowned hospital, but it was abandoned after a series of mysterious deaths. Now, ghostly patients pack its wards, crying out for cures to eternal ills.

Waterford

The hamlet of Waterford stands at a confluence of rivers and roads. Here, the Globe inn quietly keeps a back entrance and rooms to accommodate silent, ghostly travelers.

Whelk Spring House

The last of the noble Esker family recently died, bequeathing Whelk Spring House to distant relatives. At low tide, the waters of Bent Key Causeway reveal a muddy path to the estate.

Adventures in Mordent.name: Adventures in Mordent
Adventures in Mordent.text:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.B4fO7uj1um7xvHRC inline]

Mordent is a realm of @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwGenresofHorro.JournalEntryPage.GhostStories019O]{ghost stories} (see chapter 4). Moldering manors, lonely moors, neglected graveyards, and similar gothic sites provide natural settings for encounters with spirits and the tragic dead. Use this section to develop horror adventures in this domain.

Horror in Mordent

Adventures in Mordent often include the following elements.

The Apparatus

Wilfred is obsessed with control over the dead and the living. He knows where the magical device known as the Apparatus is hidden but has limited understanding of how to use it. Nevertheless, he knows it is powerful and can produce devastating effects. He cares nothing for the machine’s remarkable abilities and seeks to use it as a weapon to force all in Mordent to obey him.

To fulfill his goals, Wilfred seeks individuals with magical and alchemical insights, as well as rumors of the Apparatus’s missing piece, the Rod of Rastinon. He might threaten or blackmail the living into aiding him. Should he suspect the Rod of Rastinon or other vital information lies beyond Mordent, he might send his agents to other domains.

The Apparatus can exchange souls, open passages through the Mists, and create more dangerous effects, but the magical device might have any properties an adventure demands.

Bastion of Heroes

Despite the horrors lurking in Mordent, the domain hosts several quiet, relatively peaceful communities. Characters from Mordent might have had relatively peaceful, mundane lives before mysteries or tragedies led them to adventure. The abandoned estates of Mordent also make fine places to use the rules for @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwHauntedBastio]{Haunted Bastions}, detailed in chapter 3.

Ghosts of Mordent

Ghost stories often involve spirits that linger as the result of some tragedy or unfinished work. In Mordent, spirits linger and might request help from the living. Roll on or choose a result from the Mordent Hauntings table to inspire adventures featuring the lingering dead.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.tables.RollTable.rhwMordentHaunAc rollable resultLabel="The Spirit Seeks Aid In …"]

Even though spirits are trapped in Mordent, many find secluded places where they reflect on nature, their lives, and eternity. Over generations, such spirits often become little more than vague shapes or disembodied voices or emotions before fading away entirely.

Van Richten’s Herbalist Shop

@UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwRudolphvanRic]{Rudolph van Richten} (see chapter 5) is the foremost scholar of monsters and strange occurrences in the Domains of Dread. He lives and works from the Mordentshire herbalist shop (see map 2.27). Beyond the storefront, Rudolph’s studies and prepares methods of combating evils plaguing the Land of the Mists. Adventurers might find lore and tools to combat all manner of monsters here.

When absent from his shop, Rudolph leaves it in the care of his protégés, @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwGenniferWeath]{Gennifer} and @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwLaurieWeather]{Laurie Weathermay-Foxgrove} (detailed in chapter 5) and Beatrice Polk, an elderly local gardener and accomplished detective.

Mordent Campaign Arc

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.KInB2y2C38HovuVk cite=false classes="float-right"]

Mordent campaigns often involve ghostly mysteries. A campaign here might follow this outline:

Levels 1–2. Caarey Gelthik, a local ne’er-do-well in Mordent, has become a @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmGhast000000000]{Ghast} and lurks beneath the town docks. By night, he robs graves to consume the bodies within. The characters take note when silent spirits—perhaps of allies—begin wandering town, each missing limbs, tongues, and other soft features. Only finding and defeating Gelthik causes the dismember spirits to depart.

Levels 3–4. Beatrice Polk, caretaker at the Mordentshire herbalist shop, hires the characters to end a haunting afflicting the shop. See the adventure “@UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwMordent000000.JournalEntryPage.TheGhostHunterP3]{The Ghost Hunter’s Haunting}.”

Levels 5–10. Alice Weathermay, mayor of Mordentshire, hires the characters to find a lost piece of local history: the Rod of Rastinon. She shares leads involving various strange sites, but the rod doesn’t appear. Eventually, Lord Godefroy—who has been working through Alice Weathermay—grows impatient. He threatens the party’s dead loved ones, insisting the characters find the rod swiftly and help him use the Apparatus (see chapter 3).

The Ghost Hunter’s Haunting.name: The Ghost Hunter’s Haunting
The Ghost Hunter’s Haunting.text:
@UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.adventures.Adventure.UouWfVgKRZHp86xl]{Adventure for Level 3 Characters}

Situation. An accident at Rudolph van Richten’s herbalist shop broke a strange relic and unleashed a deadly feline spirit from another domain.

Hook. Beatrice Polk, the shop’s caretaker, offers the characters [[/award 400gp]] if they end the haunting.

Scene Configuration Overview

  • If you encounter broken links below, they will appear once you install the adventure in your World.

  • The interior map is fully prepared for your adventures in an herbalist shop, complete with walls and lighting. Global Illumination is enabled by default.

  • Some areas of the scene have operational interior lighting, while other lights are pre-placed but disabled by default.

  • NPCs are placed in the scene, but remain hidden until you are ready for the PCs to interact with them.

  • Scene regions for teleporting tokens are set up on the first and second floors, as well as in the cellar.

Encounters

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.KvHQDXFv7HbBvFOm classes="float-right" cite=false]

This adventure uses map 2.27 and consists of these encounters.

Beatrice Polk’s Aid. Beyond her folksy politeness and dislike of curse words, Beatrice Polk takes her stewardship of the herbalist shop seriously but doesn’t enter the shop during the haunting. She can provide the characters with a number of @UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.equipment.Item.phbagPotionofHea]{Potions of Healing} equal to the party’s members. She scuttles plans that involve harming or stealing Rudolph’s possessions.

Ghost Cat. A Wraith that appears as a @UUID[Actor.rUMIKjnqH5gZegH4]{black cat} the size of a panther haunts the shop. Whenever the characters enter a room without monsters present, roll a die. If the number is odd, the cat is in the room. If the cat has more than half its Hit Points, it fights for a round, then flees to another room or floor; otherwise, it flees without attacking. If destroyed, the wraith reappears 24 hours later with full Hit Points. Its threat ends only if the ritual in the cellar is completed.

Laboratory. Two @UUID[Actor.lczxO4VPGWch7s20]{Poltergeists} haunt this room.

Stairs. An unnatural wind blows against anyone who tries to ascend either stairway to the second floor. A character must succeed on a [[/check athletics 16 format=long]] to climb the stairs.

Dumbwaiter. A dumbwaiter connects the kitchen and the upstairs hall. It can hold one Medium or smaller creature. A creature outside the dumbwaiter can use an action to raise or lower the dumbwaiter and its contents.

Upstairs Hall. Broken on the floor is a strange relic: a mummied cat. Shelves nearby hold accounts of Rudolph’s travels in a land called @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwHarAkir000000]{Har’Akir}.

Library. Rudolph’s library is filled with occult texts. Any character who spends a minute investigating the library finds a copy of Van Richten’s Guide to Ghosts. Those who spend at least 10 minutes reading this book and have seen both the relic in the upstairs hall and the ritual room in the cellar understand how to use the cellar room to exorcise the ghostly cat. Additionally, a character who succeeds on a [[/check investigation 12 format=long]] finds a @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.items.Item.sFkpE6htKlEP4D2I]{Spell Scroll} of Protection from Evil and Good.

Guest Room. Standing facing a corner of this room is @UUID[Actor.QHBNdSxvXcM32EB8]{Gennifer Weathermay-Foxgrove} (see chapter 5). She is possessed by a @UUID[Actor.LD8rETLPgblzcrXH]{Ghost}, which holds her in a catatonic state. Gennifer is freed of her possession if she’s affected by a spell like @UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.spells.Item.phbEvilAndGoodPr]{Protection from Evil and Good} or if she’s removed from the shop. The feline-featured wraith attacks the party if its possession ends or if Gennifer is moved from the room. If freed from the ghost’s control, Gennifer is aware of the ghostly cat, as she accidentally broke the relic in the hall. She knows how to exorcise the spirit using the wards in the cellar.

Cellar. A room in the cellar is prepared for magic rituals. A character who succeeds on a [[/check arcana 12 format=long]] realizes this space is warded to contain or dispel spirits. If the characters place the relic from the upstairs hall within the space, the ghostly cat appears 1 minute later and fights until destroyed. If the cat is defeated here, the relic repairs itself and the haunting ends. Characters gain a bonus [[/award 400xp each]] for ending the haunting.

Wilfred Godefroy.name: Wilfred Godefroy
Wilfred Godefroy.text:
Darklord Tormented by Ghostly Undeath

Lord Wilfred Godefroy rules the afterlife of all who die in Mordent. Arrogant, impatient, and vengeful, the Darklord seeks any opportunity to exert his control and reinforce his undying noble standing.

Lord Godefroy haunts the House on Gryphon Hill, along with countless other spirits. Wilfred can send mobs of obedient spirits to do his will anywhere in the domain. He uses his control over the dead to influence the living, threatening to harm his victims’ dead loved ones if they don’t obey his fickle commands.

A generation ago, the creators of the mist-piercing device called the Apparatus vanished from Mordent. Lord Godefroy has discovered the hidden machine, but, to his frustration, lacks a way to use it.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwWilfredGodefr statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Wilfred’s Tale

In life, Lord Wilfred Godefroy was an unremarkable minor aristocrat. He inherited the estate of Gryphon Hill near Mordentshire centuries ago by murdering his father and framing it as an accident. During a bloody night years later, the entire

Godefroy family—Wilfred; his wife, Estelle; and their daughter, Penelope—died violently. But instead of passing on to the afterlife, the Godefroys’ souls lingered at Gryphon Hill. In death, Wilfred is haunted by the ghosts of his family, recent and the past generations, who blame him for their deaths and the family’s disgrace.

A generation ago, a mysterious alchemist created a device in Mordent called the Apparatus. This magical machine had many fantastic powers, including the ability to open passages between the planes and separate a creature’s good and evil natures into separate beings. The Apparatus drew multiple powerful beings to Mordent and eventually attracted the Mists themselves when the device malfunctioned, causing a deadly disaster. The Mists collected Mordent into the Domains of Dread, and the mysterious alchemist vanished. With the departure of mightier beings, Lord Godefroy remained as the land’s most fearful villain. He inherited the mantle of Darklord of the new domain of Mordent.

Since then, Wilfred has claimed lordship of all who die in Mordent. He knows the location of the Apparatus and seeks to master its powers, but how to do so and the location of a vital piece of the machine—the Rod of Rastinon—both elude him. He seeks to blackmail anyone who might be able to reveal the device’s secrets or find the missing rod. Wilfred forces Alice Weathermay, mayor of Mordentshire, to keep him appraised of useful visitors to the domain, by holding her dead husband, Daniel Foxgrove, hostage.

Wilfred’s Domain

Mordent is altered by Lord Godefroy’s presence, creating the following effects:

Closing the Borders. While in his domain, Lord Godefroy can close or open the borders to Mordent. While the borders are closed, the groans and whispers of the dead fill the Mists surrounding Mordent. Creatures in the Mists have the Deafened condition.

Ears Everywhere. While in his domain, Lord Godefroy can cast @UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.spells.Item.phbsplSending000]{Sending}, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability. When casting the spell in this way, Lord Godefroy can target any Undead creature in Mordent, in addition to the usual targets.

Spectral Serfs. A Humanoid that dies in Mordent rises 7 days later as a @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmSpecter0000000]{Specter} under Lord Godefroy’s control, unless the Humanoid is restored to life.

If Wilfred dies, these effects end immediately.

Wilfred’s Lair

The House on Gryphon Hill lies just outside Mordentshire and is home to Lord Wilfred Godefroy. Hundreds of spirits remain bound to the house and the surrounding grounds, and all serve the lord of the house and struggle to stay in his good graces. Spirits that rebel are punished—either forced to face Wilfred’s kennel of spectral hounds or dragged into the deepest recesses of the house, which even the eldest spirits fear.

House on Gryphon Hill

Cobwebs and dust coat the House on Gryphon Hill. Lurking spirits remain out of sight until trespassers pose a threat or Lord Godefroy orders them to attack. The house has the following features:

Basement. Lord Godefroy orders ghostly teams to endlessly dig deeper and wider basements beneath the house. As if creating his own underworld, Wilfred imprisons spirits who displease him in the expanding haunted dungeons beneath the house. Somewhere amid these dungeons, a passage connects to the site where the Apparatus is hidden.

Haunted Rooms. Estelle and Penelope Godefroy haunt the house’s attic, avoiding Wilfred and doing all they can to frustrate the Darklord and stymie his ambitions. The house’s other spirits obey Estelle and Penelope unless their commands contradict an order from Wilfred.

Withered Estate. Wilfred’s ghostly hounds patrol the house’s grounds, while spectral groundskeepers tend to dead topiaries and hedges as if they still thrived.

The Agency.name: The Agency
The Agency.text:
Darklord: Flimira Vhage

Hallmarks: Detective work, memory loss

Everything inside the office of the Agency is a monotone gray. Anyone who passes the frosted glass door into this single-room domain is expected by Flimira “Flintlock” Vhage, the detective agency’s owner. From here, Flimira collects cryptic correspondence relating to mysteries across the Domains of Dread. She enlists agents to investigate these cases, then report back to her. However, she never reveals her own past as a detective-turned-criminal, her involvement in every case her agency investigates, or that the Agency exists within her mind.

Bluetspur.name: Bluetspur
Bluetspur.text:
Darklord: The God-Brain

Hallmarks: Abductions, false memories, illithids

The entity that would become the God-Brain of Bluetspur was an illithid elder brain whose existence-violating experiments revealed a malignant truth of the multiverse. This secret manifested in the elder brain’s mind as an alien disease. Terrified of learning this pernicious, devouring knowledge themselves, the other illithid elder brains banished their peer from existence. In actuality, the Dark Powers claimed the afflicted elder brain and granted it an alien domain. Now called the God-Brain by the mind flayers and stranger entities that serve it, this elder brain pursues radical methods of saving itself from the thoughts destroying it. Its servants claim trespassers and beings from other domains, violating their minds with their bizarre experiments.

Carnival.name: Carnival
Carnival.text:
Darklords: Isolde and Nepenthe

Hallmarks: Bargains, misfits, wandering exiles

The Carnival is a wandering domain that appears within other Domains of Dread. Before the Carnival appears, mysterious fliers blow into communities near where it’s about to arrive. When it arrives, the Carnival sets up a surreal wonderland of entertainment and oddities staffed by performers, outcasts, and cursed individuals from countless lands. Attractions include grand shows at the big top, sideshow performances, and the Hall of Horrors. The Carnival’s mysterious leader is Isolde, a good but wrathful warrior of the Feywild who’s cursed to lead the Carnival even as she hunts an elusive foe across the Domains of Dread. She carries the bladelike Nepenthe, a weapon that hungers for blood. Although driven by vengeance and painful memories, Isolde protects the Carnival at all costs.

Cyre 1313, the Mourning Rail.name: Cyre 1313, the Mourning Rail
Cyre 1313, the Mourning Rail.text:
Darklord: The Last Passenger

Hallmarks: Escape from disaster, lightning rail

The disaster known as the Mourning numbers among the greatest tragedies to befall the world of Eberron—a mysterious calamity that killed nearly everyone in the land of Cyre. In the nation’s capital of Metrol, some citizens foresaw the coming devastation and sought to escape on lightning rails: elemental-powered engines capable of pulling trains of passenger carts. As scared innocents packed Metrol’s last lightning rail, known as Cyre 1313, the evacuation was delayed at the demand of a late-arriving VIP. Hundreds were forced from passenger carts to admit and maintain the secrecy of this last passenger and their retinue. When the lightning rail finally departed, it was too late—the Mourning overtook the train and its escapees. But even as it did so, the Mists claimed Cyre 1313 and all aboard it. Now, the last lightning rail from Metrol hurtles through the Mists as a traveling domain.

Those on board fear the disaster pursuing them, the mysterious passenger seated in the train’s foremost cart, and the necrotic energy now infusing the engine’s elemental spirit. Yet none of the passengers realizes that their endless quest for escape is pointless, as Cyre 1313 carries only the dead.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.ZOzDs0XRCeleH801 cite=false]

Forlorn.name: Forlorn
Forlorn.text:
Darklord: Tristen ApBlanc

Hallmarks: Life and death, strange invention

Tristen ApBlanc was born amid tragedy, the son of a vampire father and a human noble. Fearful villagers murdered his parents, leaving the boy to be adopted by druids. But during his teenage years, his dhampir nature revealed itself (see chapter 1). When the druids discovered the youth’s hunger for blood, they cast him out. Tristen grew enraged and slaughtered them, draining them all. The sacred waters of the druids’ rituals had infused their blood, though, making it poison to the dhampir. Tristen died with his adopted family, but as the Mists closed in around their sacred stone circle, he rose as a ghost.

Unexpectedly, with the dawn, the Darklord’s dhampir body was restored. Now, he lives by day, a perpetually young, charming, invention-obsessed dhampir dwelling in Castle Tristenoira, the smoking fortress his goblin servants built over the druid circle of his onetime family. At night, though, the dhampir dies a painful death, then rises in his spectral form to scour all that is green and vibrant from
his land.

Ghastria.name: Ghastria
Ghastria.text:
Darklord: Stezen d’Polarno

Hallmarks: Cursed art, dour population

A notorious hedonist, Marquis Stezen d’Polarno enjoyed popularity among his noble peers but craved immortality. A mysterious artist offered him eternity by painting Stezen’s portrait on a magical canvas. But the artist didn’t mention that the painting would strip Stezen of his love of life and natural charm. Despite his dulled existence, the marquis enjoys one reprieve: Once every season, when he shows his portrait to an audience, it consumes their souls and refreshes his thrill for life. Stezen’s artistic predations captured the attention of the Dark Powers, which drew his lands into the Mists. Now, Ghastria is a fertile island that, like its lord, lacks an essential vim—except for once per season, when vigor fleetingly returns.

G’henna.name: G’henna
G’henna.text:
Darklord: Yagno Petrovna

Hallmarks: Corrupt theocracy, false deity

Born of a Barovian family, Yagno Petrovna went missing on the slopes of Mount Ghakis as a youth. As a violent storm rose, he took shelter in a mysterious ruin and was found weeks later wandering the hills, babbling about an amber idol and the god he’d discovered: Zhakata the Provider and the Devourer. His family sought to help him, but when they learned Yagno had been secretly sacrificing people to his fictitious god, they chased him into the Mists. When he emerged, the domain of G’henna sprawled before him.

Life is hard in G’henna, a rocky land home to fierce, ravenous animals. The domain’s starving people worship the bestial god Zhakata and regularly travel to the cathedral-city of Zhukar. There, they offer their crops in sacrifice and hear Zhakata’s will through the revered prophet Yagno Petrovna.

I’Cath.name: I’Cath
I’Cath.text:
Darklord: Tsien Chiang

Hallmarks: Labyrinth, inescapable dreamworld

In her youth, Tsien Chiang accidentally slew her gold dragon mentor to create a magic, wish-granting bell capable of driving invaders from her homeland. Her scheme worked, and the people made Chiang their queen. Over time, though, the people bristled under Chiang’s rule and rose up in revolt. Slaughter and loss on both sides led Chiang to ring her magic bell again, but this time the bell cracked, and the Mists spilled forth. When they faded, Tsien Chiang was the ruler of I’Cath, a labyrinthine, nightmare city where the people sleep endlessly. In their dreams, they toil forever in a fantasy version of the city, working to maintain the perfectionism of their harsh queen.

Invidia.name: Invidia
Invidia.text:
Darklord: Gabrielle Aderre

Hallmarks: Bad parents, possessed children

Gabrielle Aderre is convinced that her son, Malocchio, is destined for greatness. From her estate outside the village of Karina, she employs an endless string of servants to provide Malocchio with the best possible upbringing. Inevitably, though, every servant flees or vanishes, either as a result of Gabrielle’s unreasonable expectations or the deadly tricks and accidents that frequently occur around the child. Gabrielle isn’t content to leave her son’s grand destiny to chance, though. Using her precious bone spirit board, she calls on supernatural forces to guide and protect her son. Spirits, angels, fiends, and worse answer her summons—but as long as they chart Malocchio’s path to glory, the Darklord eagerly accepts their gifts.

Kalidnay.name: Kalidnay
Kalidnay.text:
Darklord: Thakok-An

Hallmarks: Magical apocalypse, obsession

The city of Kalidnay was once the seat of power of Kalid-Ma, one of the monstrous sorcerer-kings of Athas. Thakok-An, the greatest of Kalid-Ma’s enforcers, desired the sorcerer-king so greatly that when she learned of a plot against her master, she didn’t reveal it. Instead, she performed a magical rite to grant Kalid-Ma even greater power, sacrificing her own family to do so. Her ritual went wrong, though, and life-draining magic washed over Kalidnay, destroying the city and draining life from nearly all its residents. Awaking amid the ruins, Thakok-An found Kalidnay surrounded by an endless sea of silt and mist. Worse, rather than being empowered, Kalid-Ma languished in a cursed torpor, the sorcerer-king’s vast magic bleeding away.

Now Thakok-An obsesses over her sleeping king. From Kalidnay’s survivors, she’s created a new cult of servants. Following Thakok-An’s wishes, these templars condemn trespassers in Kalidnay to ritual combat in the arena, where the lives of the slain sustain the secretly withering sorcerer-king.

Keening.name: Keening
Keening.text:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.VL43jsMEq8ltOKmW inline]

Darklord: Tristessa

Hallmarks: Banshee, silent village

The forbidding land of Keening is dominated by Mount Lament, at whose base lies the village of Anwrtyn. The people of Anwrtyn deafen themselves so they won’t hear the shrieking of the banshee Tristessa, a mournful spirit who roams Mount Lament and whose wail carries through the night. In life, Tristessa dwelled under Mount Arak in Tepest (detailed earlier in this chapter), but she was exiled for crimes against her people. In death, she endlessly seeks to reunite with the family slain by her misdeeds.

Klorr.name: Klorr
Klorr.text:
Darklord: Klorr

Hallmarks: Impending doom, surreal environment

Klorr is the end of worlds. Here, shattered islands drift through a misty netherworld, caught in a swirling, clockwork spiral that ends at the unignorable, burning eye called Klorr. Thirteen stars orbit this sunlike sphere, one winking out every hour. Each time one of the stars dies, one of the domain’s ruined islands is drawn into Klorr and consumed by flames. Afterward, each other island wrenches ahead, then halts, one hour closer to the same doom. When all thirteen stars have winked out, they return hourly to the sky.

Those who dwell on the crumbling islands trapped in the domain count the hours until their end. Few know how they came to Klorr or when new islands get added to the cycle, only that the Mists closed in and doomed them. Amid the realm’s surreal skies float the ruins of lost domains—among them, a city of skulls, a realm overrun by dogs, and an array of bloody, iron arenas. Echoes of domains that exist elsewhere in the Domains of Dread also drift here, including at least one duplicate of Castle Ravenloft. Those cast away amid this orderly apocalypse grow ever more desperate to defy the doomsday clock and the will of a hidden Darklord, the obsessed clockmaker named Klorr.

Markovia.name: Markovia
Markovia.text:
Darklord: Frantisek Markov

Hallmarks: Depraved science, sapient animals

Dr. Frantisek Markov is a genius—but less so than yesterday. His Markov Formula grants him unparalleled intellect, but it insidiously steals more than it gives, making him increasingly dull-witted and bestial in form. Despairing, the doctor fled to a tropical island he dubbed Markovia, where he tests new versions of his formula on the local fauna in hopes of recovering his waning genius. As a result of these tests, animals across Markovia now possess sapience and have been deluded into worshipping Markov as their god.

Nightmare Lands.name: Nightmare Lands
Nightmare Lands.text:
Darklord: The Nightmare Court

Hallmarks: Nightmares, recurring dreams

Any who sleep might become forever trapped in the Nightmare Lands, a phantasmagoric realm whose features shift endlessly. Those who visit and escape speak of malicious wildernesses; the empty city called Nod; and uncountable drifting spheres, each containing a stranger’s unending nightmare. Insidious entities called the Nightmare Court rule the domain. No one knows how many members make up the court, but they include the tragically graceful Ghost Dancer, the tomb-bound Hypnos, the witch Mullonga, the trickster Morpheus, and the embodiment of terror known as the Nightmare Man. These beings, artisans of nightmares, visit terrors on any whose sleeping minds brush against the domain.

The Nightmare Court’s members share one commonality: All are the living nightmares of Caroline Dinwiddy, a potent psychic who has repressed memories of her own heartless deeds. These memories torment her sleeping mind, creating the Nightmare Court. Deep within the City of Nod, inside a warped reimagining of the clinic where she once worked, Caroline sleeps without waking, refusing to face the terrors her dreams unleash on innocents across the Domains of Dread and beyond.

Niranjan.name: Niranjan
Niranjan.text:
Darklord: Sarthak

Hallmarks: Asceticism, brainwashing, shadows

The Ashram of Niranjan is a vibrant vihara, or monastery, on a misty chain of islands. Here the elderly scholar Niranjan leads ascetics in practicing Ramsana, a way of life prioritizing nonattachment to the material world. In truth, Niranjan is Sarthak, a wicked bronze dragon who sends agents into the Mists bearing his philosophical writings. These works promise escape and peace to any who adopt their teachings and search the Mists for their source. Those who come to the ashram must divest themselves of worldly goods, which become part of Sarthak’s hidden hoard. Sarthak then helps his victims enter a trance that causes their souls to slip away from their bodies. Sarthak consumes these souls and replaces them with shadows, leaving the victims’ bodies under his control.

Nova Vaasa.name: Nova Vaasa
Nova Vaasa.text:
Darklord: Myar Hiregaard

Hallmarks: Nomadic riders, transformation

An unparalleled warrior, Myar Hiregaard united the nomadic tribes of the vast plains of Nova Vaasa. But, while respected as a soldier, Hiregaard made a poor peacetime leader. When brutal games could no longer keep her interest, she incited hostilities between two of her vassal tribes, then led her own forces to crush them. Subtly, she did this again and again.

After Hiregaard’s greatest massacre, the Mists enfolded all of Nova Vaasa, splitting the Darklord’s personality in two when they did. Now she rules her people with strict fairness—but when her bloodlust is piqued, she transforms into the raging knight called Malkan and sows discord across the plains.

Odaire.name: Odaire
Odaire.text:
Darklord: Maligno

Hallmarks: Evil toys, village of children

The toymaker Guiseppe had his wish for a family granted when his creation, the marionette Figlio, came to life. A proud father, the toymaker presented his son to all the people of his village, Odaire. The local children loved Figlio, but their skeptical parents said the marionette was nothing but a toy. Over time, this doubt enraged Figlio, and the marionette convinced Guiseppe to craft siblings for him. Then, when the time was right, Guiseppe’s creations did away with all the adults in Odaire.

Claimed by the Mists, Odaire is a village populated only by children and ruled by Figlio the @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwCarrionette00]{Carrionette} (see chapter 5), who now calls himself Maligno.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.qRNWatQMEhp5ANym inline]

Richemulot.name: Richemulot
Richemulot.text:
Darklord: Jacqueline Renier

Hallmarks: Martial law, plague, wererats

The once-beautiful cities of Richemulot crumble as season after season of the Gnawing Plague afflicts the domain. Countless commoners and aristocrats have died, causing rule to settle with the vain noble Jacqueline Renier. Unbeknownst to the people, Jacqueline is a wererat whose servants create and spread the plague. She loathes what her domain has become but refuses to cede power back to the people by ending the ongoing disaster. Jacqueline’s obedient state police, the Casques Silencieux, enforce martial law and quarantines across the domain.

Rider’s Bridge.name: Rider’s Bridge
Rider’s Bridge.text:
Darklord: The Headless Rider

Hallmarks: Haunted bridge, murderous legend

Nearly every domain knows some version of the apparition called the Headless Rider. It appears as a mercenary in dark armor in Mordent, a ghostly cataphract in Har’Akir, and a mutated centaur in Lamordia. But in each incarnation, certain details remain true: the rider lacks its head, it appears on a prominent bridge, and it decapitates victims as it endlessly searches for its own head. Anyone who escapes the Headless Rider might find a different domain on the opposite side of the spirit’s bridge. (See “Dullahan” in chapter 5 for more details.)

Risibilos.name: Risibilos
Risibilos.text:
Darklord: Doerdon

Hallmarks: Misdirection, ventriloquism

The Risibilos is a small music hall, similar to those found in any decent-sized city. Its lord, Doerdon, was once a king—one so thoroughly humorless that he forbade his subjects the privilege of laughter, on pain of death. He now finds himself cursed to entertain others—a task he is utterly unqualified for. Fortunately, the Mists delivered him a partner, a ventriloquist’s dummy carved in the likeness of Strahd von Zarovich. The eerie dummy has a mind of its own, insisting it is the real Strahd and that the creature currently sitting on the throne of Castle Ravenloft is a mere impostor. It rages at the audience and makes audacious promises to any who will help it regain its station. Anyone with the slightest inkling of who Strahd is finds this hilarious. Their peals of laughter are agony to Doerdon’s ears.

Scaena.name: Scaena
Scaena.text:
Darklord: Lemont Sediam Juste

Hallmarks: Reality-manipulating theater

Lemont Sediam Juste fancied himself a serious playwright, and he achieved popular, if not critical, acclaim in Dementlieu for his works of grisly horror. But he craved respectability, and with his new play Apparitions, Lemont believed he would find it. The night of the premiere, when the audience signaled their boredom, the playwright was crestfallen. His supporters wanted blood, so he gave them what they craved.

By the play’s end, Lemont had joined the drama and brutally murdered every member of the cast while the crowd roared their approval. As the show ended, the playhouse broke from Dementlieu, and Scaena was formed. Comprising a single playhouse, the domain can create any reality Lemont desires on its stage. The Darklord’s immersive performances become somewhat predictable, though, as they always end in slaughter.

Sea of Sorrows.name: Sea of Sorrows
Sea of Sorrows.text:
Darklord: Pietra van Riese

Hallmarks: Island domains, nautical horror

The murderous pirate Pietra van Riese, captain of the Relentless, had an unsavory reputation for attaching her captives to ropes and dragging them through the water until they drowned. She never removed the detritus of her victims, even though some returned to life as zombies. Rival captains ultimately sank the Relentless, but death couldn’t keep Pietra. She awoke in the Sea of Sorrows, water in her lungs and sea creatures making their homes in her flesh. Her crew stirred with her, now fish-eaten corpses. When the captain sought to speak with them, her voice emerged from their mouths.

The Relentless sails a domain that can overlap any body of water in any other domain. Some domains border the Sea of Sorrows, while others call these mysterious waters by other names. Those who venture into the Mists by ship might enter the sea’s endless debris-choked expanse of eerie beasts and shifting islands, including the following:

Blaustein. The island-fortress domain known as Blaustein once was ruled by the notorious Bluebeard, but his spectral wives overthrew him and now endlessly torment him.

Dominia. The asylum of the vampire Dr. Daclaud Heinfroth rises up from the stormy island of Dominia. The asylum’s patients are all dramatically different versions of Heinfroth, inspired by who he was at various points during his lengthy life.

Isle of the Ravens. A storm of ravens surrounds this forested islet. Hidden amid the feathered gale, an impossibly tall tower reaches into the sky. The Isle of Ravens is home to the Lady of Ravens, and any who offend her join her ebon-winged flock.

The Lighthouse. Otherworldly light shines from the Lighthouse, a twisted spur of bizarre fossils. Anyone who enters finds that instead of broadcasting a warning across the sea, the light calls down to what lurks in the pit hidden beneath it.

Vigilant’s Bluff. An undead paladin holds vigil atop Vigilant’s Bluff. Weary travelers can find refuge here if they respect the paladin’s faith. The bones of those who don’t litter the surrounding coral.

Souragne.name: Souragne
Souragne.text:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.FW20Mr0J5EOJpRUo inline]

Darklord: Anton Misroi

Hallmarks: Imprisonment, swamp magic

In society, Anton Misroi presented himself as an upstanding gentleman who served as a prison warden. But within the walls of the prison, he became a sadist who believed righteousness was on his side. When his torturous punishments finally drove the inmates to rise up, the bloody riot that ensued drew the attentions of the Dark Powers. During the uprising, Anton drowned in the swamps surrounding the prison. But he rose again soon after, an undead warden in search of inmates.

Beyond Anton’s prison, alligator-filled swamps cover the domain of Souragne right up to the sinking settlement of Port d’Elhour and Marais d’Tarascon, a village where above-ground crypts outnumber the residences of the living.

Staunton Bluffs.name: Staunton Bluffs
Staunton Bluffs.text:
Darklord: Teresa Bleysmith

Hallmarks: Endless warfare, repeating history

Eerily faceless mercenary regiments sweep the countryside of Staunton Bluffs. Burning villages and killing helpless residents, they push ever eastward toward Castle Stonecrest, hereditary home of the Bleysmith family. Teresa Bleysmith, spurred by jealousy of her brother, Torrence, gave her family’s foes the intelligence they needed to raid Staunton Bluffs. The attack was never supposed to go so far, but the duplicitous mercenaries stormed through the countryside to take Castle Stonecrest within a day. Teresa survived the attack—but when she surveyed the damage done and found her family dead by her own designs, she threw herself from the bluffs. Now she haunts her own domain, where she repeatedly relives the day of her betrayal.

Tovag.name: Tovag
Tovag.text:
Darklord: Kas the Bloody Handed

Hallmarks: Undead military dictatorship

Notorious across the planes, the vampire Kas was once the champion of the lich Vecna. Wielding the mighty sword that bears his name, he betrayed his master, and the resulting battle supposedly destroyed them both. In truth, Vecna escaped and grew in power over ages and across worlds. Kas, though, was claimed by the Mists, and in his wasteland domain of Tovag, he believes his war with Vecna rages on. Patrols of prisoner-soldiers under undead commanders scour the land, dragooning strangers to serve in Kas’s armies and to manufacture bizarre war machines. When Kas deems the time right, he sends his forces into the Mists, believing that Vecna’s realm lies just beyond. Invariably, those troops never return, leaving the vampire to rage, rebuild his forces, and continue his search for the @UUID[Compendium.dnd-dungeon-masters-guide.equipment.Item.dmgSwordOfKas000]{Sword of Kas} (detailed in the Dungeon Master’s Guide), which he considers his key to victory.

At least once, the Dark Powers have released Kas from his domain to torment their onetime prisoner Vecna. Inevitably, when Kas fails to defeat his ancient rival, the Mists return him to his domain to rage for another era.

Zherisia.name: Zherisia
Zherisia.text:
Darklord: Sodo

Hallmarks: Serial murderers, urban decay

Each day, the city of Paridon in Zherisia erupts in riots over food scarcity, taxation, and the disappearance of dozens of citizens every night. At least one murderer stalks Paridon: the ancient doppelganger Sodo, which has impersonated so many people that it now finds it impossible to hold a form for more than a few days at a time. As Sodo’s flesh runs like hot wax, it staves off dissolution by consuming the organs of humanoids. Paridon’s streets serve as the Darklord’s hunting ground, and those who enter the sewers risk attracting the notice of countless insectile horrors, including @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwCarrionStalke]{carrion stalkers} (see chapter 5) and their monstrous Hive Queen.

Random Domains.name: Random Domains
Random Domains.text:
Characters might find themselves in an unexpected domain when the Mists initially carry them to the Domains of Dread or if they aimlessly enter the Mists. When this occurs, roll on or choose a result from the Random Domains of Dread table to determine where the characters emerge. Domains marked with an asterisk (*) are featured domains detailed earlier in this chapter.

The Random Domains of Dread table also lists what tarokka cards are aligned with and opposed to each domain. Cards marked with a dagger (†) are also aligned with another Domain of Dread. Randomly determine which domain the Mists transport characters to when this card is drawn.

Other Domains of Dread.name: Other Domains of Dread
Other Domains of Dread.text: None can say how many domains drift through the Mists. This section hints at the multitudes of additional Domains of Dread. Detail and explore them in your adventures, or use them as examples when creating your own domains using chapter 4.
