Shadowlands.name: Shadowlands
Shadowlands.text:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.wQq2TGsykIXf4jZP]

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.5bRwlBONrbShQput]

Domain of Faded Glory and False Virtue

Darklord: @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwEbonbane00000]{Ebonbane}

Genre: Gothic horror

Hallmarks: Cursed magic items, doomed quests, falls from grace, knightly lineages, obsessive zeal

Mist Talismans: @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.items.Item.rhwBrokenHolySym]{Broken holy symbol}, @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.items.Item.rhwReligiousReli]{religious relic}, @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.items.Item.rhwSymboloftheCi]{symbol of the Circle}

Aligned Tarokka Card: Torturer

Opposed Tarokka Card: Broken One

For the knights of the Shadowlands, the struggle with evil isn’t a philosophical trial—it is an active war. East of the rugged Theospine Mountains spreads the peaceful realm of Nidala, where the virtuous knights of the Circle guard their lands against wickedness beyond and within. To the mountains’ west lies the ruined land of Avonleigh, the birthplace of the knightly Circle; this realm fell to evil long ago and has become overgrown by the spirit-stalked Phantasmal Forest. Many knights of the Circle view themselves as inheritors of a legacy of shame. They aspire to the heroism of their storied ancestors and battle to absolve themselves of imagined failures. They throw themselves into crusades against foes, real and imagined, sacrificing their lives—and those of their people—for redemption and glory.

For all the Circle’s good intentions, these knights are cursed. Monsters and villains they’ve defeated reappear in the Shadowlands seeking vengeance. Most infamously, deep in the Phantasmal Forest lingers the evil that caused Avonleigh’s downfall: Ebonbane, Darklord of the Shadowlands and the sapient sword of the Circle’s founder, Kateri Shadowborn.

Domain Features

The Shadowlands is known for the following features:

Bastions on the Borderlands. The Circle seeks to reclaim a foothold in Avonleigh in a region called the Borderlands. They often reward allies with Bastions here (see chapter 3 and the Dungeon Master’s Guide for details on Bastions).

The Circle. The @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwEnemiesandAll.JournalEntryPage.Organizations5af#the-circle]{knights of the Circle} (described in chapter 3) make their home in this domain.

Nidala’s Ruler. Elena Faith-hold rules Nidala. But this knight is rarely present in the land, as she leads deadly quests beyond the domain’s borders (see “Faith-hold’s Crusades”).

Locations in the Shadowlands

The Shadowlands is a dualistic domain: Avonleigh remains an overtly haunted wilderness home to lurking evils, while Nidala touts itself as the virtuous opposite. But the land’s harmony is artificial, its people forced to smile while trapped in the service of Elena Faith-hold and her sycophants. Accordingly, the domain’s conflict isn’t one of good versus evil, but rather of overt and covert evils.

The Phantasmal Forest is detailed below, and other notable locations are summarized on the Shadowlands Locations table.

Phantasmal Forest

The Phantasmal Forest dominates the haunted land of Avonleigh, a shifting, surreal woodland cloaked by perpetual night. A dreamlike place controlled by Ebonbane, the forest changes constantly, and locations within drift like flotsam on a dark sea. The River Avalon and the King’s Highway—an overgrown route of misaligned stones—enter the forest, but within the woods they circle endlessly and never leave. Only those descended from the Circle’s founding members can navigate the Phantasmal Forest without becoming lost. All others wander at Ebonbane’s whim, becoming prey for the wolves, corrupted fey, cursed knights, or wicked souls that haunt the forest.

Ebonbane is an evil, sapient blade that seeks to annihilate the legacy of the Shadowlands’ greatest hero and her allies. This includes all with the Shadowborn name and every member of the Circle.

Ebonbane is a magic weapon (see “@UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwShadowlands00.JournalEntryPage.EbonbaneDCxigWKK#ebonbane-as-a-magic-item]{Ebonbane as an Magic Item}” later in this section), but within Shadowborn Manor, it becomes a creature that can move on its own (see the @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwEbonbane00000]{Ebonbane} stat block). Beyond the manor, it doesn’t use its stat block—it requires a wielder to work its evil in the world.

Many who attempt to attune to the weapon are slain outright by its evil. Those who do manage to wield Ebonbane must endure the Darklord’s constant demands and risk becoming its pawn (see “@UUID[Compendium.dnd-dungeon-masters-guide.content.JournalEntry.dmgMagicItems000.JournalEntryPage.AMFpeeVHTKXvGbHA]{Sentient Magic Items}” in the Dungeon Master’s Guide). If unattended outside Shadowborn Manor, Ebonbane eventually finds its way back there. No one can carry Ebonbane out of the Shadowlands. If someone tries, the sword vanishes and reappears in Shadowborn Manor.

Ebonbane has a flamboyant personality and a penchant for the dramatic and sadistic. It has no regard for life and delights in animating the corpses of foes, then destroying them in front of their allies. When it speaks, it uses language that mocks the elevated speech of knights.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.3lkLZnLc3rCv6p9f cite=false]

Shadowlands Locations

Location

Description

Banemaw’s Lair

After being slain by Elena Faith-hold, the dragon Banemaw returned as a dracolich. Elena keeps this a secret and sacrifices a dozen villagers to the creature every three years.

Borderlands

Several abandoned keeps stand within sight of the Phantasmal Forest. The Circle grants these holdings to members and allies who swear to restore them as bastions of light.

Faith Hold

From her castle, Elena readies knights for bold quests, makes her “Decrees of Faith,” and employs harsh inquisitors in hidden torture chambers and reeducation cells.

Lake Amenta

Icy Lake Amenta is said to hide a golden realm beneath its waters. Only Elena remembers the city of “heretical” dwarf artisans she drowned in a flood here long ago.

Proll

Levity is forbidden in the lumbering community of Proll. People are wary of smiling and report those suspected of secretly being bards. Those who transgress are sent to the Faith Hold.

Ruins of Eckham

The peasants of Eckham rebelled against their harsh treatment. Elena claimed the people succumbed to demon worship and had her followers slay the locals and burn the town.

Ruins of Ludyard

Ludyard met a fate similar to Eckham. Freedom fighters hide out here and plan Elena’s undoing, but they endlessly squabble over how best to rule after her downfall.

Shadowborn Manor

Ebonbane tempts virtuous souls to Shadowborn Manor, crushes their faith, and takes control of their bodies. The manor is still haunted by the spirit of Kateri Shadowborn.

Stone Circle

The Circle was founded here long ago. Amid rune-carved dolmens is a circular slab marked with the impression of thirteen distinct swords. Ebonbane sees nothing that happens here.

Tergeron Manor

Long ago, the wizard Morgoroth betrayed the Circle but was trapped in a mirror in Tergeron Manor. Within the manor lies a crystal coffin that holds the hero Aurora Shadowborn in stasis.

Touraine

The gleaming, white-walled city of Touraine is peaceful and safe. The people are wary of strangers and the evils they bring. A grand temple of Ezra serves as a meeting place for the Circle.

Wynderton

Wynderton is a farming community protected by a family of knights of the same name. To prove their piety, the family insists the villagers attend religious services three times a day.

Adventures in the Shadowlands.name: Adventures in the Shadowlands
Adventures in the Shadowlands.text:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.1Kb7p270IbsBMpc6 inline]

Adventures set in the Shadowlands feature individuals striving to prove their worth or superiority. Characters might be welcome to join the knights of the Circle, or perhaps they belong to families with heroic histories. Such ties could connect characters to the domain’s history and future quests. Use this section to develop horror adventures in this domain.

Horror in the Shadowlands

Adventures in the Shadowlands often include the following elements.

Faith-hold’s Crusades

Elena Faith-hold rules Nidala, crushing its people under the weight of her intolerance and need to maintain her virtuous reputation. While she doesn’t lead the Circle, she is its most respected member.

Elena leads forays of knights into the Mists on lengthy quests to defeat wicked foes in mysterious lands. An invitation to join one of Elena’s crusades is a great honor and a death sentence. Whenever Elena returns from the Mists, she always does so alone, claiming to be the sole survivor of a great victory.

Elena Faith-hold isn’t a Darklord, but should someone ever destroy Ebonbane, she might ascend to that position.

Quest of the Four Keys

One can destroy Ebonbane permanently using the sword of Kateri Shadowborn. However, the Dark Powers shattered her sword and scattered its pieces. These fragments are known as the Four Keys—emblems of Kateri’s valor and faith. Each embodies a different virtue and one of the four elements.

The Keys’ Locations. The Four Keys might appear anywhere in the Shadowlands or other domains. The ghost of Kateri Shadowborn or lore hidden amid tragic sites might point toward the keys.

Using the Keys. Should the characters collect the Four Keys, they can reforge them into a weapon that—if used to reduce Ebonbane to 0 Hit Points—negates its Darklord Restoration trait. It’s up to you whether this ends the Darklord’s threat or unleashes a greater evil bound within.

Shadowlands Campaign Arc

Campaigns in the Shadowlands often involve quests that reveal evils steeped in tragedies and abuses of power. A campaign here might follow this outline:

Levels 1–4. The characters aid members of the Circle in defeating a dangerous @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmBasilisk000000]{Basilisk} in Nidala. Impressed, the knights charge the characters with reclaiming a fort in the Borderlands from a wicked knight. The knight—a Circle member who died trying to recover Kateri Shadowborn’s sword—is now a @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmRevenant000000]{Revenant}. Once the knight is defeated, the Circle gives the characters its fort as a Bastion (detailed in the Dungeon Master’s Guide).

Level 5–8. In their Bastion, the characters find a piece of a broken sword. The spirit of Kateri Shadowborn appears to the most virtuous character and asks for their help freeing her spirit and defeating Ebonbane. She shares the details in the “@UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwShadowlands00.JournalEntryPage.Adventuresinthih#quest-of-the-four-keys]{Quest of the Four Keys}” section. The characters begin a journey across the Shadowlands and beyond to recover the other three sword fragments.

Levels 9–11. Once the characters have the Four Keys, Kateri’s spirit instructs them to travel to the Stone Circle in the Phantasmal Forest, where the sword can be magically reforged as a Holy Avenger. With this weapon, the characters journey to Shadowborn Manor and face Ebonbane.

The Shadowborn Sword.name: The Shadowborn Sword
The Shadowborn Sword.text:
Adventure for Level 10 Characters

Situation. A knight seeks to prove herself by finding a hero’s lost sword.

Hook. The human knight @UUID[Actor.45jMRFYy6ylcggbS]{Lucia Lambert} (Medium, Lawful Good @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmQuestingKnight]{Questing Knight}) invites the characters to join her mission to recover the lost sword of the hero Kateri Shadowborn. In return, she’ll reward them with [[/award 4000gp]]—or double that amount in additions to the characters’ Bastions.

Encounters

The adventure starts at the edge of the Phantasmal Forest and consists of these encounters.

Phantasmal Forest. The journey to Shadowborn Manor is 18 miles, with the forest’s density and perpetual Darkness limiting characters to a Slow pace. As Lucia’s family members have belonged to the Circle since its founding, she can navigate the forest at a Normal pace, so the journey takes only 8 hours.

Fallen Knight. Outside the manor, a @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmDeathKnightAsp]{Death Knight Aspirant} wearing the armor of a Circle knight bars the path and warns characters not to enter. A Good character who succeeds on a [[/check persuasion 16 format=long]] can convince the corpse to let them pass; otherwise, it attacks those who try to enter.

Shadowborn Manor. @UUID[Actor.2r6iwoVSx5235GLq]{Armored corpses} lie about Shadowborn Manor (use map 2.29). If the characters disturb them or enter the great hall, five rise as @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwStrahdSkeleto]{Strahd Skeletons} (see chapter 5).

Shadowborn’s Ghost. The Lawful Good @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmGhost000000000]{Ghost} of @UUID[Actor.gEEWbauX5BFNp0ek]{Kateri Shadowborn} haunts the manor. If the characters seem near defeat, she appears and can distract Ebonbane or lead them to the hidden dungeon.

Ebonbane. @UUID[Actor.176P8YHFm0zuI5Uu]{Ebonbane} (see @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwShadowlands00.JournalEntryPage.EbonbaneDCxigWKK]{earlier in this chapter}) lies in the great hall. If with the characters, Lucia claims the sword and is stricken dead by Ebonbane, who animates her body. Otherwise, Ebonbane animates another corpse. The Darklord tries to convince the characters to take it from the manor so it can slay their foes. If they reject it, Ebonbane fights.

Conclusion. Should the characters escape Shadowborn Manor, each of them earns a bonus [[/award 3100xp]]. If they don’t take Ebonbane, Kateri’s ghost appears to them the following night and explains that Ebonbane’s evil isn’t ended. She goes explains the Darklord’s history and how to destroy it (see “@UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwShadowlands00.JournalEntryPage.Adventuresinthih#quest-of-the-four-keys]{Quest of the Four Keys}”). The Circle rewards the characters in Lucia’s stead if she is slain.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.Cl0IRnQaKLnwB8Q7 cite=false]

Ebonbane.name: Ebonbane
Ebonbane.text:
Darklord of Endless Vengeance

Ebonbane is an evil, sapient blade that seeks to annihilate the legacy of the Shadowlands’ greatest hero and her allies. This includes all with the Shadowborn name and every member of the Circle.

Ebonbane is a magic weapon (see “Ebonbane as an Magic Item” later in this section), but within Shadowborn Manor, it becomes a creature that can move on its own (see the @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwEbonbane00000]{Ebonbane} stat block). Beyond the manor, it doesn’t use its stat block—it requires a wielder to work its evil in the world.

Many who attempt to attune to the weapon are slain outright by its evil. Those who do manage to wield Ebonbane must endure the Darklord’s constant demands and risk becoming its pawn (see “@UUID[Compendium.dnd-dungeon-masters-guide.content.JournalEntry.dmgMagicItems000.JournalEntryPage.AMFpeeVHTKXvGbHA]{Sentient Magic Items}” in the Dungeon Master’s Guide). If unattended outside Shadowborn Manor, Ebonbane eventually finds its way back there. No one can carry Ebonbane out of the Shadowlands. If someone tries, the sword vanishes and reappears in Shadowborn Manor.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwEbonbane00000 statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Ebonbane’s Tale

Kateri Shadowborn was a great hero who, alongside her order of valorous knights called the Circle, spent her life in service of good. Among her greatest deeds was leading an army against a nation whose king had been possessed by a fiend. She defeated the fiend, though doing so caused her magic sword to shatter.

After a life of great deeds, Kateri Shadowborn retired to live in peace with her family at Shadowborn Manor. But the hero had made many enemies. Thinking her vulnerable, wicked cultists recovered the pieces of Kateri’s broken sword and conjured the lingering malice of the evils she had thwarted. These the cultists forged into a terrible weapon, Ebonbane—and they became its first victims.

Animated by hatred for Kateri and her allies, Ebonbane took control of its creators and became a curse on the world. For years it worked to destroy what Kateri had created, ruin what she loved, and slaughter those who aided her. Many times the evil sword’s wielder was captured, but none ever suspected that the weapon itself was behind the grisly crimes.

Finally, in the hands of an unassuming monk, Ebonbane found its way to Shadowborn Manor, where grim news had isolated Kateri and worn her down. Aged but still a mighty knight, Kateri recognized Ebonbane’s threat, and a great battle ensued. During the clash, Kateri kept Ebonbane’s attention and ensured her family and friends escaped the manor. When only the hero and the wicked sword remained, Kateri summoned her remaining might into a blow to destroy Ebonbane. But the wicked sword proved too powerful. Kateri’s blade shattered, and Ebonbane struck down the legendary knight. Exalting in its victory, the evil weapon possessed Kateri’s body and pursued her fleeing family. But as it did so, it witnessed its quarry fading into the rising Mists.

When the Mists receded, Ebonbane found itself in a realm where the residents regarded the ancestors of Kateri Shadowborn and her fellow knights as heroes. Thrilled to continue its spree, Ebonbane sought to leave Shadowborn Manor, but it had no one to carry it beyond the grounds. Now, the evil blade seethes in frustration, waiting for a worthy bearer to carry it into the world so it might destroy all the good that came from Kateri’s work.

Ebonbane as a Magic Item

Ebonbane’s magic weapon form is described below.

Ebonbane

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.items.Item.rhwEbonbane00000 caption=false]

Ebonbane’s Domain

The Shadowlands is altered by Ebonbane’s presence, creating the following effects:

Closing the Borders. While in its domain, Ebonbane can close or open the borders of the Shadowlands at will. While the borders are closed, whirling blades of magic fly through the Mists surrounding the Shadowlands. A creature that enters the Mists for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there must succeed on a [[/save dex 15 format=long]] or take [[/damage 3d10 force average]] damage. A creature makes this save only once per turn.

Haunted Woods. The forest within 1 mile of Ebonbane’s lair is wreathed in a powerful illusion. Whenever a creature other than Ebonbane or the sword’s allies finishes a Short or Long Rest in that area, it must succeed on a [[/save int 15 format=long]], or it is lost in the forest, always finding itself at the location it started in until it finishes a Short or Long Rest.

Turning Weapons. While in its lair, Ebonbane can transform nonmagical Greatswords and Longswords within 1 mile of the lair into @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmAnimatedFlying]{Animated Flying Swords}}by completing a 24-hour ritual.

If Ebonbane is destroyed, these effects end immediately.

Ebonbane’s Lair

Ebonbane remains imprisoned in Shadowborn Manor, a fortified estate hidden in the Phantasmal Forest. The grounds are strewn with the bodies of those who sought the legendary blade and failed.

Shadowborn Manor

Though abandoned, Shadowborn Manor remains in decent repair. The estate is depicted on map 2.29 and has the following features:

Dungeon. The Circle understood that some evils needed to be contained. A secret stairway in the east wing leads to a dungeon that has remained hidden from Ebonbane.

Great Hall. This hall hosts the round table where members of the Circle once met. Ebonbane poses the bodies of knights here to possess as it pleases.

Lady’s Chambers. Kateri Shadowborn’s armored corpse lies in the largest bedroom on the second floor. It is Ebonbane’s favorite body to reanimate, and if destroyed, it reappears in this chamber.

Sithicus.name: Sithicus
Sithicus.text:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.na01aUx80A9Wi2MT]

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.3Nbf2i0SENc2ogkO]

Domain of the Soul’s Shadow

Darklord: @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwEbonbane00000]{Ebonbane}

Genre: Gothic horror

Hallmarks: Cursed magic items, doomed quests, falls from grace, knightly lineages, obsessive zeal

Mist Talismans: @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.items.Item.rhwBrokenHolySym]{Broken holy symbol}, @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.items.Item.rhwReligiousReli]{religious relic}, @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.items.Item.rhwSymboloftheCi]{symbol of the Circle}

Aligned Tarokka Card: Torturer

Opposed Tarokka Card: Broken One

Whatever Sithicus was or could have been, it will never be. The dramatic ruins of a fabulous elven realm cover a land realm forested by gigantic trees and burning with the flames of a forgotten magical disaster. Scattered communities appear among the dilapidation, the domain’s predominantly elf population living like strangers amid structures to which they have no connection. At dusk, people withdraw, hiding from the domain’s true residents: the shadows.

By night, the shadows of Sithicus reenact forgotten lives or mimic the deeds of questing knights, devoted lovers, and lurking monsters. Most of these shadows are harmless, if unnerving, and seem not to notice other beings. Some, however, resent the domain’s other residents and torment the living.

Though few in the domain realize it, Sithicus has ties to the world of Krynn on the Material Plane. Ruins suggest the work of elves from that world, while the night sky blazes with the five-headed dragon constellation of the deity Takhisis. As on Krynn, the black moon Nuitari is visible only to evil beings. But the domain’s clearest tie to Krynn is its Darklord, the accursed knight Lord Soth.

Domain Features

Sithicus is known for the following features:

Bound to Krynn. Sithicus is a warped reflection of the lands of Krynn. This world and its people, such as kender, are described in Dragonlance: Shadow of the Dragon Queen.

Cataclysmic Fire. Accursed flames blaze amid prominent ruins and turn shadows into monsters drawn from Lord Soth’s past.

Living Shadows. Shadows in Sithicus move on their own. Most are harmless, but some are deadly creatures such a @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmShadow00000000]{Shadows} or @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmShadowDemon000]{Shadow Demons}.

Soth’s Traitor. Lord Soth’s undead soldiers hunt for the ghostly knight Caradoc, whom Soth blames for his imprisonment in Sithicus.

Locations in Sithicus

Two significant features of Sithicus are detailed below, and other notable locations are summarized on the Sithicus Locations table.

Endless Ruins

Sithicus lies blanketed in endless ruins. Ancient forests rise from them, rivers flow in their broken avenues, and whole mountain ranges are sculpted with crumbled towers. These structures suggest the work of a vast, magical elven nation, but all evidence of these people has been reduced to enigmatic scraps of art and lore. At the heights of crumbling towers or in empty crypts blaze violet, cataclysmic fire (see “Cataclysmic Flames”). In rare cases, these phantasmal flames reveal glimpses of Lord Soth’s past and details that the clever might use to win the Darklord’s favor.

Great Chasm

The Great Chasm gapes in Sithicus like a wound. None can say how deep it goes, as light fails to pierce more than a few dozen feet into the ravine. Those who descend deeper vanish into the shadows and rarely return. Those who do tell of a malicious darkness beneath the domain, a cacophony of hateful voices, and shadows that hunger.

Hidden amid ruins spilling into the chasm is the home of Inza Kulchevich, a hateful witch who can speak to shadows and believes all virtue is a lie. Despite her cynicism, she might perform prophetic fortune-telling or share strange lore in return for her visitors’ shadows.

Lord Loren Soth seethes within a realm that mocks and frustrates him. Having known the rigid standards of knighthood and warfare, Soth finds Sithicus empty and chaotic, his people apathetic and undisciplined. The elven beauty that once captivated him lies ruined, the land burns with the flames of Krynn’s Cataclysm, and the realm’s shadows remind Soth of his past failures. Worse, no worthy foes exist to challenge him. So Soth broods on his throne in Nedragaard Keep, haunted by banshees that sing the tale of his disgrace and by rotted, undead copies of the knights who once served him.

Lord Soth cares nothing for his domain. But should noteworthy threats or remarkable individuals appear, he seeks to crush or enlist them. The only exception is the spirit of his treacherous former ally, Caradoc. Soth’s undead servants and his werebadger seneschal, Azrael Dax, scour the land for the elusive ghost.

Soth shares his imprisonment in Sithicus with all in the domain by keeping its borders perpetually closed. Only those who rouse him to activity or invade Nedragaard Keep might convince him to let them escape. This risks provoking the mighty death knight’s wrath, but Soth still thinks of himself as a true knight—he might assist those who present themselves as honorable warriors or show knowledge of his home (see “Influencing Lord Soth”).

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.ZqaM2gpQlUFpUEs0 cite=false]

Sithicus Locations

Location

Description

Giant’s Cloak

Throughout the Giant’s Cloak forest are petrified trees carved with strange runes and weeping tar-like sap. Stone giant hermits tend these groves and set deadly traps against trespassers.

Har-Thelen

Har-Thelen is largely abandoned. Its gates stand open, and only a few hundred people reside at its center. By night, stray shadows work and live among the abandoned structures.

Hroth

The decrepit town of Hroth is built amid giant swamp trees, many of which lean at precarious angles. The bogs nearby are said to be magic and can return to life those buried within.

Iron Warden Inn

The Iron Warden Inn is closed by day, but at night, those with enough wealth to buy entry gamble at games of cards and death’s-head dice. Sometimes the dead come to play.

Kendralind

A hidden village of faded tree houses, huts, and burrows, Kendralind is home to a few dozen kender vampires. The residents are equally delighted by games, whimsical tales, and bloodshed.

Lake of Glass

The Lake of Glass moves like water but is as solid as glass. At night, the lake reflects the hidden moon, Nuitari, and magic cast within its reflected light is empowered but cursed.

Mal-Erek

Forgotten scholars built Mal-Erek amid giant trees. Now only squatters and obsessed scholars reside here, with few noticing the decay of the vaults and archives around them.

Merchant’s Slash

The Merchant’s Slash trail is littered with the wreckage of merchant and traveler caravans. Some are still occupied by eccentric traders selling eerie wares from strange lands.

Nedragaard Keep

The towering fortress of Lord Soth is strangled by black thorns and blazes with cataclysmic flame. Within, undead knights and spectral elves reenact and recite Soth’s great failures.

Sorrow

A hidden prison for the living guarded by the dead, Sorrow accepts mysterious transfers from beyond the Mists. Many thought dead in other domains are imprisoned here.

Veldrava

A dismal work camp stands at the entrance to a maze of unfathomably deep salt mines. Caustic minerals burn explorers who seek the source of whispers from the depths.

Adventures in Sithicus.name: Adventures in Sithicus
Adventures in Sithicus.text:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.El7xdhNMabhZ9bJG inline classes="float-right size-three"]

Adventures set in Sithicus feature mysterious shadows, grim ruins, and strange foes from the warped memories of Lord Soth. Use this section to develop horror adventures in this domain.

Horror in Sithicus

Adventures in Sithicus often include the following elements.

Cataclysmic Flames

Illusory violet flames burn across Sithicus—fires from the Cataclysm that devastated Krynn. Powerful shadows guard prominent, bonfire-like pillars of these flames. These shadows might use the stat blocks of any monster, but they resemble dragons, knights, and figures from Soth’s past.

Whenever someone defeats one of these guardians, the flame it protected reveals an illusory scene from the Darklord’s past before vanishing. Any character who witnesses such a scene learns something of Soth’s history, the world he comes from, or the knighthood he once belonged to. One of the viewers then gains the Charm of the Black Rose (see “@UUID[Compendium.dnd-dungeon-masters-guide.content.JournalEntry.dmgDmsToolbox000.JournalEntryPage.U9qU1oUFTPAK7g50]{Supernatural Gifts}” in the Dungeon Master’s Guide for additional details).

@UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.items.Item.rhwCharmoftheBla]{Charm of the Black Rose}

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.items.Item.rhwCharmoftheBla inline]

Influencing Lord Soth

Lord Soth is typically Hostile to strangers, but characters can adjust his attitude to Indifferent by succeeding on a [[/check Deception Persuasion 20 format=long]]. Soth might employ characters he deems worthy on quests, such as the hunt for the ghost Caradoc. Making a second successful [[/check Deception Persuasion 20 format=long]] while offering Soth something he covets can improve Soth’s attitude to Friendly for a short time, during which he might let others leave his domain.

Living Shadows

Shadows in Sithicus often act with a life of their own. Roll on or choose a result from the Shadows of Sithicus table to inspire these shapes’ behavior.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.tables.RollTable.rhwShadowsofSi78 rollable resultLabel="The Shadows Behave As …" classes="caption-top"]{Shadows of Sithicus}

Sithicus Campaign Arc

Campaigns set in Sithicus often involve trying to gain Lord Soth’s favor and win escape from the domain. A campaign here might follow this outline:

Levels 1–4. Azrael Dax, Lord Soth’s werebadger seneschal, brusquely recruits the characters to hunt down deadly shadows. These shadows take the form of and behave as noble knights.

Level 5–9. Impressed by the characters, Azrael sends them to Inza Kulchevich to ask the witch to use her powers to find Lord Soth’s elusive former servant, Caradoc. Inza can do this, but first she requires the characters to collect the cataclysmic flame from three ruins across the domain in a magical iron pendant. Each flame is guarded by a shadow creature that resembles some foe from Soth’s twisted memories—like a lance-wielding @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmWarriorCommand]{Warrior Commander} or a pompous @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmArchpriest0000]{Archpriest}.

Levels 10–14. Inza uses the pendant to reveal that Caradoc’s soul is trapped in a black sapphire that Azrael already possesses. The characters must recover the gem. They might then infiltrate Nedragaard Keep to offer it to Lord Soth. Soth is fickle though, and this approach might end in a confrontation with the death knight.

Shadow of the Black Rose.name: Shadow of the Black Rose
Shadow of the Black Rose.text:
@UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.adventures.Adventure.e71ba2cQqk7fvkZN]{Adventure for Level 14 Characters}

Situation. The characters seek to escape Sithicus or the Domains of Dread.

Hook. @UUID[Actor.L4bN0TNL8vaBz9Fl]{Inza Kulchevich} (Medium, Neutral Evil Death Cultist) offers to tell the characters how to escape Sithicus if they recover a magical gem from Lord Soth’s seneschal, Azrael Dax.

Scene Configuration Overview

  • If you encounter broken links in this adventure, they will appear once you install the adventure in your World.

  • The adventure map for Sithicus is configured, but NPC tokens have not been pre-placed because the adventure does not specify their locations. These encounters do not use Nedragaard Keep, though it is available if desired. Global Illumination is enabled by default.

  • Encounter actors are available for each result on the Encounters in the Ruins table.

Encounters

The adventure consists of these encounters.

Inza’s Quest. The witch Inza loathes Lord Soth. She knows he has tasked Azrael with recovering a magical gem that holds the spirit of the Darklord’s treacherous former ally, the knight Caradoc. Inza gives the characters an iron pendant bearing an image of a rose that’s missing its central gem. She asks them to follow Azrael to a ruin in the Misttop Mountains, where he hunts the missing gem, and recover it before him.

Inza’s Secret. Inza doesn’t share that she seeks to release Caradoc to provoke Soth into a conflict with the characters.

Searching the Ruins. The ruins where Azrael hunts for the gem appear on map 2.31. For every 10 minutes the characters are exploring, roll 1d6 on the table below for an encounter. For each previous encounter rolled on this table, add 1 to the result.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.tables.RollTable.rhwEncountersiTh rollable resultLabel="Encounter" classes="caption-top"]{Encounters in the Ruins}

Kender Nosferatu. A morbidly whimsical pair of @UUID[Actor.TKCglBDx0dxTg7CG]{Vampire Nosferatu} (see chapter 5) pesters the characters, hoping to be entertained. If the characters don’t keep them amused, the nosferatu attack. Because these monsters were kender in life—mischievous folk from the world of Krynn—they are Small.

Shadow Highlord. A dragon rider made of shadows guards a pillar of illusory violet flame. It reflects one of Lord Soth’s warped memories and uses the stat blocks of a @UUID[Actor.LYg15qFrQADo6bY3]{Dullahan} (see chapter 5) riding a @UUID[Actor.QOKtXONGSIzeoBCX]{Young Blue Dragon}, followed by a withered @UUID[Actor.f2X3MVHe5Tg9jslh]{Death’s Head} (see chapter 5). The monsters are Hostile. If they’re defeated, the nearby pillar of flames shows a scene of Lord Soth being knighted and grants a character the benefits noted in “Cataclysmic Flames.” If you roll this encounter a second time, treat it as a meeting with Azrael Dax.

Azrael Dax. @UUID[Actor.BxKbg5bbgUvubFXV]{Azrael Dax} is Lord Soth’s dwarf seneschal and a murderous werebadger (use the Werebear stat block). Azrael and the six @UUID[Actor.pmzSA1kg8pmEsa2r]{Strahd Skeletons} (see chapter 5) attending him are Hostile. If reduced to 50 Hit Points or fewer, Azrael bargains for his life and offers the characters the black sapphire they’re looking for, which he’s been hiding from Soth for moons. After this, don’t roll for more encounters in the ruins.

Repairing the Pendant. Place the black gem in Inza’s iron amulet releases @UUID[Actor.yJAceNHhzTSyfRpv]{Caradoc} (Neutral Evil Ghost). The arrogant creature is Indifferent to the characters. If they don’t free Caradoc, Inza does when the group returns the gem and pendant to her.

Soth Appears. Three rounds after Caradoc is freed, @UUID[Actor.kwRxdZ9BeQsnax70]{Lord Soth of Sithicus} (see earlier in this chapter) appears from the shadows. Soth fights Caradoc and mistakes the characters for the ghost’s allies. A character might convince him otherwise—see “@UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwSithicus00000.JournalEntryPage.AdventuresinSiBU#influencing-lord-soth]{Influencing Lord Soth}.”

Escape. Characters who flee or defeat Lord Soth gain an additional [[/award 6200xp]]. Those who change the Darklord’s attitude to Friendly or help him defeat Caradoc can convince him to let them leave Sithicus. Defeating Soth also causes the domain’s borders to open. If the characters confront Inza about her method for leaving Sithicus, she cryptically explains that the only route to do so leads through Soth, encouraging them to seek the Darklord’s aid or confront the death knight again.

Lord Soth.name: Lord Soth
Lord Soth.text:
Darklord Cursed by Memories

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.2SAbTM94bJLevM1Y cite=false]

Lord Loren Soth seethes within a realm that mocks and frustrates him. Having known the rigid standards of knighthood and warfare, Soth finds Sithicus empty and chaotic, his people apathetic and undisciplined. The elven beauty that once captivated him lies ruined, the land burns with the flames of Krynn’s Cataclysm, and the realm’s shadows remind Soth of his past failures. Worse, no worthy foes exist to challenge him. So Soth broods on his throne in Nedragaard Keep, haunted by banshees that sing the tale of his disgrace and by rotted, undead copies of the knights who once served him.

Lord Soth cares nothing for his domain. But should noteworthy threats or remarkable individuals appear, he seeks to crush or enlist them. The only exception is the spirit of his treacherous former ally, Caradoc. Soth’s undead servants and his werebadger seneschal, Azrael Dax, scour the land for the elusive ghost.

Soth shares his imprisonment in Sithicus with all in the domain by keeping its borders perpetually closed. Only those who rouse him to activity or invade Nedragaard Keep might convince him to let them escape. This risks provoking the mighty death knight’s wrath, but Soth still thinks of himself as a true knight—he might assist those who present themselves as honorable warriors or show knowledge of his home (see “Influencing Lord Soth”).

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwLordSothofSit statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Villain of Two Worlds

Lord Soth features in adventures in both the Dragonlance and Ravenloft campaign settings. If you want to give Soth a unique relationship to the Domains of Dread, consider these possibilities:

  • The nature of Soth’s curse creates aspects of the death knight in both Krynn and Sithicus. When Soth broods in his fortress in Krynn, he remains active in Sithicus. When the evil god, Takhisis, calls for his service on Krynn, Soth enters a magical torpor in his fortress in Sithicus.

  • Soth traveled from Krynn to Sithicus and is destined to return. If he leaves Sithicus, the domain might be destroyed, or a villain like Inza Kulchevich might take over.

  • Soth was trapped in Sithicus, escaped, and was trapped again, perpetuating a cycle of despair. Soth’s story and where characters encounter him are for you to detail in your adventures.

Lord Soth’s Tale

Lord Soth was given a quest to save the people of his world, Krynn, from the wrath of the gods. Possessed by baseless paranoia and possessiveness of his elf wife, Soth forsook that quest, and the world was devastated in a great Cataclysm. In punishment, Soth was resurrected as a death knight and, for centuries, brooded on his disgrace in the charred ruins of his fortress, Dargaard Keep.

When the evil god Takhisis whispered to Soth, promising to restore his glory and avenge him against the heroic knights who banished him long ago, Soth answered and served Takhisis. During this time, he called on his undead servants, including the ghost of the knight Caradoc. Seeking to forge a new knighthood of loyal dead, Soth became obsessed with Dragon Highlord Kitiara Uth Matar and charged Caradoc with claiming her soul. When Kitiara died in battle, Caradoc refused to capture her spirit unless Soth bargained with Takhisis to restore Caradoc to life. Enraged, Soth tried to destroy Caradoc as the Mists rose around them.

When the Mists faded, Soth found himself in the domain of Barovia. For a time, he found an ally in Count Strahd von Zarovich, but the two villains couldn’t abide one another for long. Soth eventually pursued rumors of his treacherous servant, Caradoc, into the Mists. When the Mists parted again, the domain of Sithicus spread before him.

Lord Soth’s Domain

Sithicus is altered by Lord Soth’s presence, creating the following effects:

Closing the Borders. While in his domain, Lord Soth can close or open the borders to Sithicus. While the borders are closed, purple flames fill the Mists surrounding Sithicus. A creature that enters the Mists for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there takes [[/damage 1d10 fire average]] damage plus [[/damage 1d10 necrotic average]] damage.

Fearless Soldiers. Undead allies of Lord Soth have Immunity to the &Reference[Frightened] condition.

Guilt of Sithicus. Few sleep soundly in Sithicus. Whenever a creature other than Lord Soth and his allies finishes a Long Rest in the domain, the creature must succeed on a [[/save con 10 format=long]] or gain 1 &Reference[Exhaustion] level.

If Lord Soth dies, these effects end immediately.

Lord Soth’s Lair

Lord Soth rules from Nedragaard Keep, a twisted, copy of his accursed fortress on Krynn, Dargaard Keep. Nedragaard Keep grows and withers over time like a living thing. Undead soldiers patrolling the keep regularly forget their duties or collapse into lifelessness. The disorder within the rigid death knight’s fortress endlessly frustrates Lord Soth.

Nedragaard Keep

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.bITJfuTAo1UZA8Wi cite=false classes="float-right"]

Nedragaard Keep is a towerlike fortress of crumbling, flame-scarred stone. Prominent locations in the keep are depicted on map 2.31 and have the following features:

Gatehouse. Undead soldiers guard the gates to Nedragaard Keep, demanding to know trespassers’ identities. Those who identify themselves with the proper passphrase, such as “a seeker of the rose” or “a servant of Nightlund’s lord,” are admitted. Stairs beyond the gate climb several floors to the top of the keep’s walls.

Garden. Gardens on the keep’s second floor are overgrown with thorny plants—use the rules for @UUID[Compendium.dnd-dungeon-masters-guide.actors.Actor.dmgRazorvine0000]{razorvine} from the Dungeon Master’s Guide. From here spread massive vines that bloom with black roses and strangle the entire castle.

Throne Room. High in the keep is Soth’s throne room. Here Lord Soth spends much of his time dwelling on failures and twisted fantasies. Thirteen @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmBanshee0000000]{Banshees} haunt the keep, and at any time, at least three linger here, singing of Soth’s disgrace.

Species.name: Gatunki
Species.text: Remarkable events and supernatural horrors have transformed individuals, creating species that spread throughout the Mists and the multiverse. A character might have been born as a member of one of the species in this section or transformed into one after harrowing events.

Subclasses.name: Podklasy
Subclasses.text: This section presents seven new subclasses: the @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwSubclasses000.JournalEntryPage.ReanimatorArtifi]{Reanimator} (Artificer), @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwSubclasses000.JournalEntryPage.CollegeofSpirits]{College of Spirits} (Bard), @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwSubclasses000.JournalEntryPage.GraveDomainCleri]{Grave Domain} (Cleric), @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwSubclasses000.JournalEntryPage.HollowWardenRang]{Hollow Warden} (Ranger), @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwSubclasses000.JournalEntryPage.PhantomRogueBU0w]{Phantom} (Rogue), @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwSubclasses000.JournalEntryPage.ShadowSorcerySor]{Shadow Sorcery} (Sorcerer), and @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwSubclasses000.JournalEntryPage.UndeadPatronWarl]{Undead Patron} (Warlock). The Artificer class is detailed in Eberron: Forge of the Artificer.

Subclass Spell Lists.name: Listy czarów podklasowych
Subclass Spell Lists.text: The follow pages contain the subclass spell lists for this source book.

High Deck.name: High Deck
High Deck.text: The cards that make up the high deck aren’t considered one suit, although they all have a crown icon to mark their importance. Each card represents an important power. If one of these cards appears during a reading and contradicts the prediction of an earlier card, Vistani always assume this card takes precedence.

Common Deck.name: Common Deck
Common Deck.text:
The four suits of the common deck are described below.

Coins

The suit of coins symbolizes desire for personal and material gain. It also represents gluttony, lust, and obsession. On the side of good, this suit can suggest the accumulation of wealth to benefit a charity or a just cause. On the side of evil, it embodies the worst aspects of greed.

Glyphs

The suit of glyphs symbolizes faith, spirituality, and inner strength. It is the suit of priests and those who devote themselves to the service of a deity, a higher power, or a heightened philosophy. It symbolizes health and healing, as well as illness and disease. On the side of good, this suit represents willpower and dedication. On the side of evil, it signifies weakness of character, self-doubt, and betrayal of one’s ideals or beliefs.

Stars

The suit of stars symbolizes the desire for personal power and control over things beyond mortal ken. It is the suit of mages, sages, and intellectuals. It also represents the triumph of magic, science, and reason over religion, mysticism, and superstition.

Swords

The suit of swords symbolizes aggression and violence. It is the suit of warriors, be they paladins, soldiers, or mercenaries. It also symbolizes the power of governments and leaders, whether noble or corrupt.

Tarokka Readings.name: Tarokka Readings
Tarokka Readings.text:
Prop Tarokka Decks

Tarokka decks for use in your game are available separately, with each card featuring imagery to aid you in creating portentous fortunes. If you don’t have a tarokka deck, you can use a regular deck of playing cards instead. To do so, separate the numbered cards from the face cards and jokers, and treat them as two separate decks: the common deck (the numbered cards) and the high deck (the face cards and jokers).

Common Deck. Each suit in a regular deck of playing cards corresponds to a suit in the tarokka deck.

Hearts = Glyphs Diamonds = Coins

Spades = Swords Clubs = Stars

An ace represents a one in the tarokka deck, while a ten in a regular deck represents a master card in the tarokka deck.

High Deck. Each jack, queen, king, and joker corresponds to a card in the tarokka high deck.

Playing Card

Tarokka Card

King of hearts

Ghost

Queen of hearts

Innocent

Jack of hearts

Marionette

King of spades

Darklord

Queen of spades

Mists

Jack of spades

Executioner

King of diamonds

Broken One

Queen of diamonds

Tempter

Jack of diamonds

Beast

King of clubs

Donjon

Queen of clubs

Raven

Jack of clubs

Seer

Joker 1

Artifact

Joker 2

Horseman

Tarokka decks aid fortune tellers in revealing information about specific individuals. The focus of a tarokka reading might be the individual having their fortune told or someone else. A fortune teller randomly selects or invites another to choose what tarokka card best represents the focus of the reading. To begin the tarokka reading, this card is laid face up in position 1 in the pattern below.

The fortune teller then draws four additional cards, laying one face down in each of the other numbered positions in the pattern. These positions each represent something about the focus:

Position 1. The focus of the reading and the lens through which the other cards are interpreted

Position 2. The past, something that might or might not be apparent to those other than the focus

Position 3. What opposes the focus, whether literal opponents or obstructing fortunes

Position 4. The future, an omen of what’s to come and a fate that might still be changed

Position 5. What aids the focus, whether literal allies or advantageous events

The reading proceeds with the fortune teller revealing the significance of each card’s position and what the card represents, as guided by the descriptions later in this appendix. After a card is interpreted, the card in the next position is revealed. This continues until all five cards have been interpreted. The fortune teller might reveal more details (see the following section) or claim that the cards remain silent on certain points. The fortune teller and characters then might speculate about the reading’s broader meanings. Once you’re satisfied with what’s revealed, the reading ends.

Tarokka Techniques.name: Tarokka Techniques
Tarokka Techniques.text:
The tarokka deck is a prop for inspiring adventures and creating dramatic fortune-telling scenes. Through a mix of creative interpretation and card manipulation, a DM can influence what the tarokka reveals and the accuracy of its revelations. Consider the following techniques during tarokka readings.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.EzkXUCBaauYVVo62 cite=false]

Stacking the Deck

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.Tcx6WOOIL1VxqBUs classes="float-right size-three" cite=false]

You can use sleight of hand tricks to ensure tarokka cards appear where you desire during a reading. Select the desired cards before the tarokka reading and put them in the order you want to draw them so you can produce them effortlessly and place them in the desired positions. Cards might be stacked on the top of the deck or drawn from the bottom. Stacking the deck is useful for ensuring specific results or creating patterns that appear repeatedly between readings. Avoid letting players notice you’re stacking the deck, as it undermines the mystery of that and future tarokka readings.

Dramatic Interpretations

When interpreting tarokka cards, you can elaborate on the card descriptions in this appendix, reframing those details and adjusting them to better suit your adventure. Use these techniques to add onto and adjust the meanings of tarokka cards:

Broad Interpretation. Highlight broader card details, like the meanings of suits and the generalities they encompass, over card specifics.

Card Imagery. Focus on specifics in a card’s art, like the numbers of objects or the positions of figures, to suggest a deeper meaning.

Contextual Meanings. Suggest that multiple cards of the same suit or in specific relation to one another have greater or lesser meaning.

Diviner Experience. Reveal that a card arrangement has a specific meaning, or that the fortune teller has seen it in an ominous instance before.

Foreshadowing. Highlight details that align with impending events of which only you are aware.

Leading Questions. Have a fortune teller ask questions that lead characters to connect the reading with past events or their existing knowledge.

Manipulating Fate

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.COaNzaxr5pUOX0Mj inline classes="float-right"]

You control fate in your adventures. If you don’t know what a tarokka card means during a reading, note that and fulfill its prophecy in the future. For example, if the four of glyphs appears in a reading, its imagery might appear later as a shepherd NPC, a hooked staff, a wandering sheep, four runes, or the like. You can then tell the players about the connection between the image and the reading.

Additionally, listen in on players’ speculations about the meaning of tarokka readings, then use those suggestions to influence future revelations in your adventures.

High Deck

NameMeaning
ArtifactThe importance of some physical object that must be obtained, protected, or destroyed at all costs
BeastGreat rage or passion; something bestial or malevolent hiding in plain sight or lurking just below the surface
Broken OneDefeat, failure, and despair; the loss of something or someone important, without which one feels incomplete
DarklordA powerful evil individual; one whose goals have enormous and far-reaching consequences
DonjonIsolation and imprisonment; a prisoner of their own beliefs
GhostThe looming past; the return of an old enemy or the discovery of a secret buried long ago
ExecutionerThe imminent death of one rightly or wrongly convicted of a crime; false accusations and unjust prosecution
HorsemanDeath; disastrous loss of wealth or property, a horrible defeat, or the end of a bloodline
InnocentA being of great importance whose life is in danger (who might be helpless or simply unaware of the peril)
MarionetteThe presence of a spy or a minion of some greater power; an encounter with a puppet or an underling
MistsSomething unexpected or mysterious that can’t be avoided; a great quest or journey that will try one’s spirit
RavenA hidden source of information; a fortunate turn of events; a secret potential for good
SeerInspiration and keen intellect; a future event, the outcome of which will hinge on a clever mind
TempterOne who has been compromised or led astray by temptation or foolishness; one who tempts others for evil ends

 

Swords

CardNameMeaning

Two of swordsPaladinJust and noble warriors; those who live by a code of honor and integrity
Three of swordsSoldierWar and sacrifice; the stamina to endure great hardship
Four of swordsMercenaryInner strength and fortitude; those who fight for power or wealth
Five of swordsMyrmidonGreat heroes; a sudden reversal of fate; the triumph of the underdog over a mighty enemy
Six of swordsBerserkerThe brutal and barbaric side of warfare; bloodlust; those with a bestial nature
Seven of swordsHooded OneBigotry, intolerance, and xenophobia; a mysterious presence or newcomer
Eight of swordsDictatorAll that is wrong with leadership; those who rule through fear and violence
Nine of swordsTorturerThe coming of suffering or merciless cruelty; one who is irredeemably evil or sadistic

 

Stars

CardNameMeaning
Master of starsWizardMystery and riddles; the unknown; those who crave magical power and great knowledge
One of starsTransmuterA new discovery; the coming of unexpected things; unforeseen consequences and chaos
Two of starsDivinerThe pursuit of knowledge tempered by wisdom; truth and honesty; sages and prophecy
Three of starsEnchanterInner turmoil that comes from confusion, fear of failure, or false information
Four of starsAbjurerThose guided by logic and reasoning; warnings of an overlooked clue or piece of information
Five of starsElementalistThe triumph of nature over civilization; natural disasters and bountiful harvests
Six of starsEvokerMagical or supernatural power that can’t be controlled; magic for destructive ends
Seven of starsIllusionistLies and deceit; grand conspiracies; secret societies; the presence of a dupe or a saboteur
Eight of starsNecromancerUnnatural events and unhealthy obsessions; those who follow a destructive path
Nine of starsConjurerThe coming of an unexpected supernatural threat; those who think of themselves as gods

 

Coins

CardNameMeaning
Master of coinsRogueAnyone for whom money is important; those who believe money is the key to their success
One of coinsSwashbucklerThose who like money yet give it up freely; likable rogues and rapscallions
Two of coinsPhilanthropistCharity and giving on a grand scale; those who use wealth to fight evil and sickness
Three of coinsTraderCommerce; smuggling and black markets; fair and equitable trades
Four of coinsMerchantA rare commodity or business opportunity; deceitful or dangerous business transactions
Five of coinsGuild MemberLike-minded individuals joined together in a common goal; pride in one’s work

Seven of coinsThiefThose who steal or burgle; loss of property, beauty, innocence, friendship, or reputation

Glyphs

CardNameMeaning
Master of glyphsPriestEnlightenment; those who follow a deity, a system of values, or a higher purpose
One of glyphsMonkSerenity; inner strength and self-reliance; supreme confidence bereft of arrogance
Two of glyphsMissionaryThose who spread wisdom to others; warnings of the spread of fear and ignorance
Three of glyphsHealerHealing; a contagious illness, disease, or curse; those who practice the healing arts
Four of glyphsShepherdThose who protect others; one who bears a burden far too great to be shouldered alone
Five of glyphsDruidThe ambivalence and cruelty of nature and those who feel drawn to it; inner turmoil
