Ravenloft Setting Models.name: Ravenloft Setting Models
Ravenloft Setting Models.text:
What domains compose the Ravenloft setting is up to you. The Domains of Dread have no definitive map or proscribed orientation for how they relate to one another. This allows you to shape the setting however best serves your adventures. Consider the following setting models for how you might use the Domains of Dread or use your favorite elements from multiple domains.

Haunted House Domain

In this model, a single domain functions like a haunted house. Characters enter, terrifying forces trap them, and the party struggles to escape. This model features in the adventures I6: Ravenloft (1983) and its descendant Curse of Strahd (2016). This setting model excels if you want to …

  • Explore the tale of a single domain or Darklord.

  • Highlight a narrow band of horror genres.

  • Avoid interacting with multiple domains or traveling the Mists.

  • Make a Darklord your adventures’ focal villain.

Islands of Terror

In this model, the Domains of Dread are isolated realms that characters might reach by braving the Mists. Domains’ inhabitants might be aware of each other, but travel is uncommon and dangerous. Characters might be inhabitants of a domain or wayward souls from other realms, and events might lead them through the Mists to other nightmarish lands. This model is broadly used in this book. This setting model excels if you want to …

  • Highlight the mystery and surreal nature of the Domains of Dread.

  • Feature tours of multiple domains while controlling where the Mists lead.

  • Explore stories focused on the nature of the Mists and escaping the Domains of Dread.

  • Make Darklords part of a cast of villains.

Clusters and Cores

In this model, the Domains of Dread are similar to countries on globe. The Mists mark the edges of the world, rise when Darklords close their domains’ borders, and appear as they please, but otherwise domains are separated by geographical and political borders. Domain inhabitants know about one another, travel, and trade, while Darklords are powerful figures that might have political or warlike aspirations. Whether you create a cluster out of a small group of your favorite domains or meld many (or all) the domains into a continent-sized core is up to you. This is the model largely used in earlier explorations of the setting, including Ravenloft: Realm of Terror (1990) and Domains of Dread (1997). This setting model excels if you want to …

  • Encourage characters to roam between domains.

  • Highlight villains who are infamous in multiple domains or who must be pursued (or evaded).

  • Explore large-scale plots, such as wars between or the destruction of the Domains of Dread.

  • Obscure Darklords, making them figures that might not be widely known.

Gothic Earth

The similarities between Strahd von Zarovich and Dracula, between Viktra Mordenheim and Victor Frankenstein, and between other Ravenloft villains and icons of horror fiction and film are intentional. Because many of Ravenloft’s villains and domains have real-world roots, elements from Ravenloft are readily portable into frightful adventures set on a version of Earth steeped in magic and gothic horror. While the details of such historic horror are beyond the scope of this book, consider using the tools herein to pit characters against menaces from your favorite horror tales. This model is explored in Ravenloft: Masque of the Red Death and Other Tales (1994).

Domain Details.name: Domain Details
Domain Details.text:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.fRdp3My70NRigYcW inline classes="float-right size-three"]

To reinforce the types of horror your adventures explore and give domains their own malicious personalities, consider these setting elements.

Darklord Reflections

Every domain can be viewed through the lens of its Darklord, with every part of the domain reflecting some facet of the Darklord’s personality, ambition, or torment. You might think about adventures in a domain as explorations of a Darklord’s identity.

For example, Barovia’s gothic horror themes include deception, oppression, and predation, all of which are aspects of the domain’s Darklord, Strahd von Zarovich. While the village of Vallaki leans toward themes of deception, the village of Barovia, in the shadow of Castle Ravenloft, is steeped in oppression. The domain’s deadly wildernesses and beasts reinforce themes of predation. When a domain’s terrifying forces reflect aspects of its Darklord, that villain becomes a terrifying culmination of the evil in the land and the personification of the domain’s terrors.

Familiar Ground

Every Domain of Dread takes inspiration from stories within its related genres. Embrace this. Including tropes from your favorite horror stories reinforces expectations about the domain and provides a shorthand to help players understand a location. The Domains of Dread are intentionally filled with gothic castles, haunted manors, and beleaguered villages to hint at the types of characters and adventures you’ll find within. Don’t worry about recreating familiar stories, as the unpredictable actions of the players’ characters will create a unique adventure.

Twisted Tropes. Wildly deviating from tropes can create unique locations, but doing so requires more explanation to help players understand the setting. Consider instead featuring familiar horror settings with a twist to make them more appropriate to D&D or to enhance their terror. For example, characters escaping an alien laboratory in Bluetspur might be stalked by an elusive displacer beast, or the ghosts haunting a house in Mordent might be desperately trying to keep something worse sealed in the basement. Minor twists help setting elements feel distinct and work better in your game.

Isolation

Isolation can take multiple forms in a domain.

Character Isolation. In the Domains of Dread, the characters are often the land’s only opposition to a domain’s evil and their own only hope for survival. While characters might find allies or sanctuaries in a domain, the characters must look to themselves for solutions. Escape is rarely an option.

Physical Isolation. Each domain is a complete setting that the Mists might isolate from all other lands. Locations within a domain are often isolated by dark, monster-haunted wildernesses. Even settlements and sanctuaries might have their own grim secrets.

The Locals

The inhabitants of domains endure or ignore the horrors around them. Whether these individuals are oppressed people or soulless pawns of the Dark Powers is up to you, but regardless, they don’t effectively resist the horror within their homes. Characters should feel like they’re among the only ones who recognize a domain’s evil.

Incongruous Behavior. Consider treating a domain’s inhabitants as part of the strangeness of the setting. Such NPCs might willfully ignore proof of the domain’s horrors or passively behave in ways that reinforce a Darklord’s will. In more surreal cases, NPCs might forget the characters or even reappear after dying.

Nightmare Nature

To any question regarding the natural world within a domain, the scariest answer is likely true. Use details of a domain to create a beat of small, unsettling moments to support the percussion of unignorable terrors in your adventures. If you highlight the strange nature of a domain often, the land feels surreal and haunted. If you do so more rarely, the domain feels more realistic. You might even change the pace of how often you reinforce the strange nature of a domain, perhaps doing so more the farther characters are from civilization or safety.

For examples of frightful details you might highlight in a domain, roll on or choose a result from the Dread Details table.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.tables.RollTable.rhwDreadDetailKw rollable resultLabel="In a Quiet Moment, a Character Witnesses …"]

You can customize these suggestions to reinforce the themes of a domain or its Darklord. For example, in Falkovnia, a statue might momentarily resemble a rotting zombie, while in @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwHarAkir000000]{Har’Akir}, Darklord Ankhtepot’s visage might appear in a sandstorm. Strive to make such elements feel like moments of strangeness—harmless oddities that a character can dismiss as their eyes deceiving them, rather than suggestions of an immediate threat.

Using Domains of Dread.name: Using Domains of Dread
Using Domains of Dread.text:
Domains of Dread are backdrops to horror tales and the torments of their Darklord. These domains exist to confound and terrify those within. Everything in a Domain of Dread serves frightful ends, and if something seems like it doesn’t, you just haven’t revealed how yet. While creating horror adventures, consider the following facts about domains.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.Y69DisAVKXm2bhbC inline classes="float-right size-three"]

Valachan.name: Valachan
Valachan.text:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.g2TI5dMejqPg2cFA]

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.gz063xJaDsQrhvGK]

Domain of the Hunter

Darklord: @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwChakuna000000]{Chakuna}

Genres: Gothic horror and slasher horror

Hallmarks: Deadly predators, death traps, hostile wilderness, survival games

Mist Talismans: @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.items.Item.rhwDisplacerbeas]{Displacer beast skin}, @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.items.Item.rhwpoisonousflow]{poisonous flower blossom}, @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.items.Item.rhwrustyfoottrap]{rusty foot trap}

Aligned Tarokka Card: Shepherd

Opposed Tarokka Card: Darklord

In the wild domain of Valachan, only the deadliest predators survive. Bestial hunters, carnivorous plants, and ravenous monsters stalk the dense rainforests, but the land’s greatest threat is its Darklord: the devious, undying hunter Chakuna.

Chakuna stalks the jungle, testing herself against the domain’s deadliest beasts. Yet little delights her more than discovering strangers to her realm— visitors who soon realize they’ve become the Darklord’s newest quarry. Chakuna forces her victims to participate in the Trial of Hearts, a race through the wilderness to remote sanctuaries. Participants face deadly traps and all the dangers of the wilds, including Chakuna herself. Those who reach the goal at the end of the trial are permitted to leave Valachan. Those who don’t reach it die, either due to the deadly wilderness or at the hands of Chakuna herself.

Domain Features

Valachan is known for the following features:

Displacer Beast Packs. Packs of @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmDisplacerBeast]{Displacer Beasts} roam the jungle, led by Yana, a preternaturally large and cunning displacer beast that serves Chakuna.

Natural Hunters. Wild plants and creatures in Valachan usually seem hostile toward strangers. Most creatures here are carnivorous even if elsewhere they aren’t.

Trial of Hearts. The deadly Trial of Hearts is a battle royale that starts on the night of the full moon. Chakuna chooses participants.

Wilderness Domain. Valachan is covered by a fantastically dangerous rainforest. Visitors face constant perils, but the domain’s residents flourish.

Locations in Valachan

Valachan is lush and wild. Rare creatures and plants reign here, as do villages of people who understand and thrive in the rainforest. No roads pass through the land, but ships from other domains sometimes wreck on these shores. The settlement of Oselo and the Pantara Lodge are described below, and other notable locations are summarized on the Valachan Locations table.

Oselo

The former home of Darklord Chakuna, Oselo is a prosperous village that welcomes visitors. When night falls, the residents change, shifting from people into panthers, ocelots, tigers, and other hunting cats. These shape-shifters hunt animals for food and do battle with other peoples only to defend their home. Most fear Chakuna and want no involvement with the Trial of Hearts, fearing the Darklord’s wrath. However, in rare instances, Oselo’s shape-shifters might be convinced to guide, hide, or otherwise aid participants in the Trial. This displeases Chakuna, but she respects the decisions of her people’s elders. The villagers might adopt strangers deemed worthy, but outsiders first must prove themselves in an initiation ceremony focused on surviving unarmed in the forest for a week.

Pantara Lodge

The Pantara Lodge is a series of thatched structures strung together high in the forest canopy, suspended above the overgrown ruins of a burned castle. This is Chakuna’s home. Its interconnected treehouses include a great hall, the Darklord’s personal quarters, an armory, an infirmary, and several guest lodges. The Darklord’s @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmDisplacerBeast]{Displacer Beast} and @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmPanther0000000]{Panther} allies prowl the trees amid the lodge’s structures and can enter them freely.

Chakuna is Valachan’s deadliest hunter, a youthful werepanther with the intensity and calm of a veteran executioner. Few can elude her cold cunning, deadly traps, and packs of feline allies.

Chakuna is no mere murderer. She hunts because she must to protect the people of Oselo from a more terrible fate. To that end, she prefers to target strangers in her land, forcing them to participate in the Trial of Hearts. She relishes the chance to hunt the proud and disrespectful, as well as the skilled and unpredictable. Chakuna knows no mercy, though, and even those she comes to respect eventually face her blade. She spares the people of Oselo alone, even as her peoples’ fear of her grows.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.JQYO7JKBu8XYgb1g cite=false]

Valachan Locations

Location

Description

Council of the Whip Spider

Within this cavern, a sapient @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmGiantSpider000]{Giant Spider} presides over representatives of countless insect species. Those seeking insect allies might entreat these councilors for aid.

Forest of Fossils

Dinosaurs and megafauna seem common in the Forest of Fossils. The region’s most

notorious predator is Mother Heartless, a heavily scarred @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmTyrannosaurusR]{Tyrannosaurus Rex}.

Gasping Way

The steep walls of the forested ravine called the Gasping Way keep the forest below in near-constant shadow. Quicksand, common along this route, acts with deadly swiftness.

Poison’s Taste

People say the lake called Poison’s Taste is filled with the venom of a giant, sleeping snake. Most who drink from the lake die—but those who survive become immune to nearly all poisons.

Sangui Cove

Sharks, @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmPlesiosaurus00]{Plesiosaurs}, giant aquatic snakes, and other waterborne menaces battle each other constantly in Sangui Cove. Deep below lie the wrecks of ships, some laden with fantastic wealth.

Shuaran

The human warriors of the village of Shuaran value honor above all. They avoid Chakuna and might help her foes or those who offer aid against the deadly howling @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmApe00000000000]{Apes} that dwell nearby.

Sleepless Forest

Noisy animals and pests are common in the Sleepless Forest. Anything that dies here reanimates as a @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmZombie00000000]{Zombie} the following night, shambles away, and collapses in a quieter resting place.

Starving Water

Skeleton-covered ruins jut from the center of the Starving Water. The deadly waters frequently release gases in limnic eruptions capable of suffocating nearby creatures.

Throne of Eels

An @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmAboleth0000000]{Aboleth} named Apez-Zos lurks within bizarre ruins beneath the lake called the Throne of Eels. It has enthralled nearby lizardfolk communities into serving it and seeks to supplant Chakuna.

Weeping Rainforest

The predators in the Weeping Rainforest have a sadistic streak, grievously wounding their prey, then leaving it to struggle in high trees. The cries of the wounded ring through the canopy.

Yaguara’s Heart

Within the canyon of Yaguara’s Heart lies a temple, beneath which sprawl blood-painted caverns. Hidden inside is Chakuna’s beating heart, which binds her to the land.

Adventures in Valachan.name: Adventures in Valachan
Adventures in Valachan.text:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.wKsjrZIDYu8sRx0j inline]

Adventures set in Valachan involve merciless wilderness, predatory animals, and deadly hunts. Use this section to develop horror adventures in this domain.

Horror in Valachan

Adventures in Valachan often include the following elements.

Trial of Hearts Rules

Visitors to Valachan likely are forced to participate in the Trial of Hearts, a dangerous game where the domain’s Darklord hunts them. On the night of the full moon, Chakuna selects those she considers worthy to participate. At the Pantara Lodge, she shows her guests every courtesy, provides them with a map of the domain, and sets out these rules for the trial:

  • Participants can divide into small groups or go it alone.

  • Once groups are established at the trial’s start, alliances between contenders aren’t permitted.

  • Participants must reach one of two shrines: either on Kiru Island or between the lakes called the Scars atop Anquara Plateau.

  • Participants can leave the Pantara Lodge at dawn. The following dusk, Chakuna pursues the contenders with hunting partners of her choice, attempting to slay any participants she encounters outside a shrine.

  • Only one person or group can occupy a shrine.

  • Participants can kill one another for any reason— particularly to take a shrine.

  • The trial doesn’t stop for any reason until Chakuna arrives at both shrines and acknowledges any winners there.

  • The Darklord’s servants escort winners to the village of Shuaran, and from there, out of Valachan.

  • Losers rot where they fall.

  • Rule breakers are killed.

Chakuna wants the trial to be challenging. If she catches a competitor too soon, she might release them if they sacrifice an ally, a limb, or a valuable weapon. Anyone who dies in the trial feeds the domain’s bloodlust—even those who fall to a threat other than the Darklord herself.

Valachan Exploration

Valachan is a dense and deadly wilderness. When creating adventures here, refer to chapter 2 of the Dungeon Master’s Guide for details on travel and journeys with stages.

Characters use the rules in the “Travel Pace” section of the Player’s Handbook. Valachan’s forests prevent a party from traveling at faster than a Normal pace, and their density makes them Difficult Terrain, reducing travel pace by half. This means most characters can move through the domain at a Normal pace of 1½ miles per hour and 12 miles per day.

Flying. Difficult Terrain slows flying creatures in Valachan, due to the strong winds above the forest canopy. Flying creatures are also easy to spot, attracting the attention of @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmChimera0000000]{Chimeras}, @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmPteranodon0000]{Pteranodons}, and condor-like @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmRoc00000000000]{Rocs}. Chakuna might stipulate that flight is off-limits during the Trial of Hearts. If she does, the Mists aid in enforcing this rule.

Navigation. It’s easy to get lost in Valachan’s jungles. Unless they use gorges and waterways for guidance, characters must make [[/check survival 15 format=long]] to avoid getting lost. Those who fail spend 1d4 hours wandering and risk encountering deadly creatures (see “@UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwValachan00000.JournalEntryPage.AdventuresinVaDw#trial-of-hearts-rules]{Trial of Hearts}” for suggestions). Characters should make this check once during each stage of a journey, or after dramatic or disorienting encounters.

Trial Route: Kiru Island. An approximately 20-mile route from the Pantara Lodge to Kiru Island runs along the gorge south of the lodge to the Eirubamba River, then along the coast to Kiru Island. At a Normal pace, this route takes a day and a half (13 hours) to complete. Roughly 10 miles of this route follows the river. Characters who use a raft can move at Fast pace along the river. Rapids make the river Difficult Terrain, but even so, moving at this pace reduces the journey to just over a day (11 hours).

Trial Route: The Scars. Participants in the Trial of Hearts who travel from the Pantara Lodge to the Scars on Anquara Plateau have a route of approximately 20 miles if they follow the Shadowtaker Lakes east to the river called Killer’s Road, then ascend Anquara Plateau. Characters can follow most of this route at a Normal pace, but 2 miles involve climbing into the mountains—a Slow pace through Difficult Terrain, reducing movement to 1 mile per hour. This route takes over a day and a half (14 hours) to complete.

Valachan Campaign Arc

Campaigns set in Valachan often feature the Trial of Hearts and Chakuna’s connection to her domain. A campaign here might follow this outline:

Levels 1–3. Consider starting this campaign in another domain or world. During their travels, the characters become shipwrecked.

Level 4–6. The characters wash up on the shores of Valachan. They must find ways to survive the rainforest and fend off a pack of Displacer Beasts that stalks them. They eventually find shelter in Oselo or Shuaran.

Levels 7–9. Having heard of their exploits, Chakuna invites the characters to the Pantara Lodge, offering to help them leave if they participate in the Trial of Hearts (see the adventure below). Before the trial ensues, the characters might learn secrets from the head of Urik von Kharkov.

Levels 10+. If the characters survive the trial, Chakuna helps them leave the domain. As they characters travel out of Valachan, locals from Shuaran or Oselo beg the characters to stay and slay Chakuna. Should the characters kill Chakuna, chaos erupts and Urik von Kharkov strives to retake power in Chakuna’s absence.

Trial of Hearts.name: Trial of Hearts
Trial of Hearts.text:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.1xFUlibBgUZ5erMW cite=false]

@UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.adventures.Adventure.rMD3haPHHlB2njgp]{Adventure for Level 8 Characters}

Situation. To escape Valachan, the characters must participate in the Trial of Hearts.

Hook. The hunter @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwValachan00000.JournalEntryPage.ChakunayekzHPBbf]{Chakuna} (see earlier in this chapter) invites characters to @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwValachan00000.JournalEntryPage.ChakunayekzHPBbf#pantara-lodge]{Pantara Lodge} to discuss helping them leave the domain.

Encounters

The adventure uses @UUID[Scene.iqmabb3uAPORMqzQ]{map 2.34} and consists of these encounters.

@UUID[Scene.JeFw8mLHqAW7KC31]{Pantara Lodge.} @UUID[Actor.jd3vHQWMFX131MMX]{Chakuna} seems welcoming and polite, inviting characters to relax before a feast where she’ll explain how to leave Valachan. Note the Indifferent @UUID[Actor.BQcxldyx1Q5QHwk7]{Displacer Beasts} that prowl the lodge—Chakuna’s guards and hunting companions. The characters receive a comfortable guest room where they can rest before dinner.

Chakuna’s Guests. The characters aren’t Chakuna’s only guests. There are three other groups:

Lizardfolk. @UUID[Actor.X5ONO90E43riLvx7]{Tolocwa} (@UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmLizardfolkSove]{Lizardfolk Sovereign}) and three @UUID[Actor.hRKMdpC7CV83mRAS]{lizardfolk} (Medium @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmWarriorVeteran]{Warrior Veterans}) are their tribe’s best hunters.

Pirates. The human @UUID[Actor.BSNP34EnfO0wM6g5]{Rakengel} (Medium @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmPirateCaptain0]{Pirate Captain}) and three @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmPirate00000000]{Pirates} from his crew were recently shipwrecked.

Thieves. @UUID[Actor.4DltTEfkMh73o4LB]{Aian}, @UUID[Actor.XyVdKCaKZ61RcRaj]{Citana}, and @UUID[Actor.S2v9sVQbHPPjH5ns]{Lesar} are @UUID[Compendium.dnd-monster-manual.actors.Actor.mmWeretiger00000]{Weretigers} from Oselo who were caught stealing a rare herb.

Trial Rules. After Chakuna’s guests have all gathered for a meal of vegetarian dishes and mineral water, Chakuna does the following:

  • Explains the trial, outlining the rules from the “@UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwValachan00000.JournalEntryPage.AdventuresinVaDw#trial-of-hearts-rules]{Trial of Hearts Rules}” section.

  • Insists this is the only way to leave Valachan.

  • Notes that the group could attack her, but they would face her @UUID[Actor.BQcxldyx1Q5QHwk7]{Displacer Beast} allies. If they want to fight, they should wait for the trial.

The pirate group speaks up in protest, but growls from Chakuna’s allies silence them. The Darklord answers questions, then encourages her guests to rest, as the trial starts at dawn. Those who leave before then violate the rules.

Cheating. Chakuna catches anyone who tries to leave the lodge early or otherwise cheat. She encourages them to obey the rules and calls on six @UUID[Actor.BQcxldyx1Q5QHwk7]{Displacer Beasts} to enforce them. She calls the monsters off before anyone dies.

Choosing a Route. Have the characters choose a shrine as their destination and determine their route (see “@UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwValachan00000.JournalEntryPage.AdventuresinVaDw#valachan-exploration]{Valachan Exploration}”).

Trial Encounters. The trial is divided into three phases, including a night camping in the jungle. As you narrate the characters’ journey through the wilderness, roll on the following table for an encounter that happens during each of the first two phases and another that occurs while camping.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.tables.RollTable.rhwJungleEventlW rollable resultLabel="Characters Encounter …"]

Chakuna During the Trial. Chakuna might appear and menace characters, but don’t track her travels or have her fight the characters directly.

Kiru Island Event. During the last phase of their journey, groups traveling to Kiru Island must find a way to cross Sangui Cove. While crossing, they are attacked by a @UUID[Actor.4aIOpPAfKMeYIQ84]{Giant Shark} and a @UUID[Actor.oUSDe0cOaFzCOgYw]{Giant Crocodile}.

Scars Event. During the last phase of their journey, groups traveling to the Scars must climb 120 feet up Anquara Plateau. While they do, a @UUID[Actor.0RGKTkqUG21EPY6Y]{Giant Ape} attacks them from above.

@UUID[Scene.JeFw8mLHqAW7KC31]{Trial Shrine.} At both destinations, the trial shrine is a wooden platform bearing a statue of a panther. It hangs at the end of a 200-foot-long rope bridge suspended 60 feet over a spiked pit. Those who fall into the pit take [[/damage 6d6 bludgeoning average]] damage plus [[/damage 8d8 piercing average]] damage.

Rival. If the characters reached the shrine in less than 15 hours, they are the first ones there. After [[/r 2d4#Hours]] hours, the pirate group arrives at the Kiru Island shrine, and the lizardfolk group arrives at the Scars shrine. Both groups are missing any members who died in previous encounters, but they’re Hostile and fight the party. If the characters took longer than 15 hours, this rival group arrives first.

Conclusion. An hour after the group contends with its rivals, Chakuna arrives. If the characters followed the rules of the Trial of Hearts, she pronounces them victors and upholds her end of the bargain—helping them leave Valachan. If they broke the trial’s rules in even the slightest way, Chakuna knows it and fights them. In either case, each character earns a bonus [[/award 2100xp]].

Chakuna.name: Chakuna
Chakuna.text:
Darklord Who Hunts to Survive

Chakuna is Valachan’s deadliest hunter, a youthful werepanther with the intensity and calm of a veteran executioner. Few can elude her cold cunning, deadly traps, and packs of feline allies.

Chakuna is no mere murderer. She hunts because she must to protect the people of Oselo from a more terrible fate. To that end, she prefers to target strangers in her land, forcing them to participate in the Trial of Hearts. She relishes the chance to hunt the proud and disrespectful, as well as the skilled and unpredictable. Chakuna knows no mercy, though, and even those she comes to respect eventually face her blade. She spares the people of Oselo alone, even as her peoples’ fear of her grows.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwChakuna000000 statblock caption=false classes="double-column hide-in-embed"]

Chakuna’s Tale

For untold generations, Darklord Urik von Kharkov ruled Valachan from Castle Pantara and hunted the people of Valachan for sport. Yearly, the Darklord’s hunts grew in carnage, with his favorite prey being the shape-shifters of Oselo. Von Kharkov viewed himself as the domain’s greatest hunter, a title he confirmed every time he slaughtered one of Oselo’s supernatural warriors.

Chakuna was a werepanther warrior of Oselo and, like most of her people, was a skillful hunter. Seeing that von Kharkov eventually would slay her entire village, she swore to save Oselo. Realizing it would take a monster to slay a monster, she left her people and sought the deepest secrets of her deadly homeland. When she returned, she volunteered to let von Kharkov hunt her. During the murderous game that followed, Chakuna burned Castle Pantara and took the former Darklord’s head.

Valachan is no mundane land, though. A Domain of Dread demands a Darklord, and Valachan will be ruled only by its greatest hunter. Refusing to let von Kharkov return, Chakuna made a pact with the domain itself. Within the depths of the mazelike Yaguara Mountains, Chakuna ripped her own heart from her chest and consumed the heart of Urik von Kharkov. Doing so, she stole von Kharkov’s powers. Yet to retain them, she must feed the land constantly with the blood of the hunted. If she doesn’t, the plants and animals of Valachan grow murderous and howl for von Kharkov’s return. So Chakuna continues a cycle of bloodshed, embracing the violence of her peoples’ onetime oppressor to ensure her bitter domination of her vicious realm.

Chakuna’s Domain

Valachan is altered by Chakuna’s presence, creating the following effects.

Closing the Borders. While in her domain, Chakuna can close or open the borders to Valachan at will. While the borders are closed, shadowy beasts skulk or soar amid the Mists surrounding Valachan. A creature that enters the Mists for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there takes [[/damage 1d10 piercing]] damage.

Heightened Senses. Beasts and Monstrosities have Advantage on Wisdom checks.

Whispering Shadows. Humanoids subtract [[/r 1d8#Subtract from ability checks]] from any ability checks they make when they take the Hide action.

If Chakuna dies, these effects end immediately.

Chakuna’s Lair

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwArtHandouts00.JournalEntryPage.7uLMiV8zA6ExlfN6 classes="float-right" cite=false]

Built above the overgrown ruins of von Kharkov’s Castle Pantara, the Pantara Lodge is a maze of rope bridges, mighty boughs, and structures built among the branches of gigantic trees. Chakuna leaves those who die in the Trial of Hearts without demonstrating skill or honor dangling from the branches of her home.

Pantara Lodge

The Pantara Lodge rises 80 feet above the rainforest floor. The site is depicted on map 2.35 and has the following features:

Chakuna’s Chambers. In her quarters, Chakuna studies lore from other realms, fruitlessly seeking ways to placate her domain without further bloodshed. Secret doors hidden here lead to her meditation chamber and a room where the undying head of Urik von Kharkov spits curses and plots his return.

Great Hall and Banquet Room. Chakuna welcomes guests in the lodge’s great hall. Prior to the Trial of Hearts every moon, she hosts a lavish meal for her prey.

Trophy Gallery. The trophy gallery holds the skulls of deadly beasts, the weapons of those who failed to murder Chakuna, and the stuffed corpses of the Darklord’s rivals—animals and people alike.
